The word here is "facilitation" of the campaigns. But the Orange team call it bribery.

Original_Search_Title: The Saitoti Factor in Maasailand Politics
Headline: Kenya: The Saitoti Factor in Maasailand Politics
Body_Text:
Nairobi — At a homecoming party in May 1988 to celebrate the appointment of William ole Ntimama to the Cabinet, Prof George Saitoti was introduced as the new Maasai spokesman.
Interestingly, the statement was made by a Kamba politician, Laban Kitele, also minister of State in the Office of the President and got the approval of John Keen.

Original_Search_Title: Rising Referendum Stakes
Headline: Kenya: Rising Referendum Stakes
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As the Orange and Banana groups put up spirited campaigns to convince voters to reject or support the proposed new constitution, bribery and promises are evident in the North Rift.
In Trans-Nzoia District, the home of hundreds of squatters and displaced families, land is being dangled.

Original_Search_Title: ECK Launches Voter Education
Headline: Kenya: ECK Launches Voter Education
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Electoral Commission of Kenya (ECK) yesterday launched the voter
education programme ahead the November 21 referendum.
Speaking at the Holy Family Basilica in Nairobi where they launched the
exercise, two ECK commissioners Ann Wambaa and Kihara Mutu said enough staff
had already been trained and voting materials for the referendum were
ready.

Original_Search_Title: November 21 is Kibaki's Date With Destiny
Headline: Kenya: November 21 is Kibaki's Date With Destiny
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In the heat of the campaigns, both sides of the divide are forecasting
into life after November 21.
The Banana camp seems to believe it would emerge stronger and the
government would be more stable, no matter the result.

Original_Search_Title: Orange Chants As Ngilu Leads Banana Campaign
Headline: Kenya: Orange Chants As Ngilu Leads Banana Campaign
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Banana campaign team led by Health minister Charity Ngilu yesterday made a stylist entry into Machakos Town where it, however, encountered isolated incidents of hostility.
The convoy was met with chants of Orange, Orange as it snaked its way through the town before the dignitaries converged on Mulu Mutisya Gardens, the venue of the rally.

Original_Search_Title: Raila Camp Alleges Plot to Put Off Referendum
Headline: Kenya: Raila Camp Alleges Plot to Put Off Referendum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange camp yesterday accused the Government of hatching a plot to
call off the referendum - and warned it would resist the move.
The alleged plot would be executed through a motion in Parliament or a
court order, the team declared at a Press conference moments after a Kanu
and LDP Parliamentary Group meeting at County Hall, Nairobi.

Original_Search_Title: No Team Tells of State Plans to Postpone Poll
Headline: Kenya: No Team Tells of State Plans to Postpone Poll
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange group yesterday claimed there were government plans to use
Parliament or the courts to suspend the poll.
They raised the alarm after a joint parliamentary group meeting between
Kanu and the Liberal Democratic Party. The talks at County Hall, Nairobi,
were attended by Cabinet ministers Raila Odinga and Linah Kilimo, who on
Saturday decamped from the Yes team, and more than 20 MPs.

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani And the Banana Peel?
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani And the Banana Peel?
Body_Text:
Last week, it became six months since Chief Ken Nnamani assumed office
as the 10th Senate President. How has he fared? What has he done to sweep
away the infamous banana peels along the corridor of the highest legislative
chamber of the land? How has he influenced or changed the nature of politics
and politicking in the house? Just as many patriots in the land were
beginning to sing hurrah, believing that the current leadership would bring
about lasting peace and stability to the operations of the Senate, thus
bestowing honour on the legislative process, a group of senators have
stepped up a campaign that it was time that the Senate President stepped
aside.
Vibrancy, effectiveness

Original_Search_Title: Too Much Control Vested in the President
Headline: Kenya: Too Much Control Vested in the President
Body_Text:
Nairobi —  Cabinet ministers Raila Odinga and Kiraitu Murungi are at each other's
jugular once again over the executive powers in the proposed new
Constitution. In an interview with Nation's STEPHEN MBURU, Mr Odinga argues
the draft seeks to take the country back to a "strong-man syndrome."
Excerpts:
What exactly are the contentious issues about the
Executive in the proposed new Constitution?

Original_Search_Title: Banana Team Buying Voters' Cards, Claims Orange Camp
Headline: Kenya: Banana Team Buying Voters' Cards, Claims Orange Camp
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Agents of the Banana team were buying voters' cards in Ukambani ahead of the referendum, claimed Orange leaders in the region yesterday.
The Orange campaigners, including seven MPs, met in Athi River and declared they were ready to give the names of those involved in the racket.

Original_Search_Title: Minister's Sh500m Gift to Councils
Headline: Kenya: Minister's Sh500m Gift to Councils
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The huge salary increment that the Government promised councillors will
be paid through the Local Authorities Transfer Fund (LATF), it became clear
yesterday.
Local Government minister Musikari Kombo announced that the amount of
money set aside for LATF had been revised upwards by Sh500 million.

Original_Search_Title: Orange Campaigns Move to the North Rift
Headline: Kenya: Orange Campaigns Move to the North Rift
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A number of Orange meetings will be held in the North Rift
tomorrow.
The Orange Democratic Movement leaders have split into several groups
for the rallies in five districts.

Original_Search_Title: Kilimo to Attend Orange Campaign Rally in Marakwet
Headline: Kenya: Kilimo to Attend Orange Campaign Rally in Marakwet
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Immigration minister Linah Jebii Kilimo is today expected to make her first public appearance among the No campaign luminaries led by Raila Odinga when the Orange team holds a rally in her Marakwet District.
Kilimo, the MP for Marakwet East, announced her defection from the Yes team to the Orange group during a Press conference in Eldoret on Saturday, saying the move was motivated by the larger interests of her community.

Original_Search_Title: Orange Team Takes Battle for Vote to VP Backyard
Headline: Kenya: Orange Team Takes Battle for Vote to VP Backyard
Body_Text:
Nairobi — An Orange campaign team yesterday arrived at the Busia stadium in a convoy of more than 100 vehicles amid cheers from a large crowd.
The team was led by Roads minister Raila Odinga and Environment colleague Kalonzo Musyoka as well as official opposition leader Uhuru Kenyatta, and included Sports minister Ochillo Ayacko, Kanu first vice-chairman Chris Okemo, Dr Bonaya Godana, Kanu secretary-general William Ruto and his LDP counterpart Joseph Kamotho.

Original_Search_Title: Kilimo Defects to Orange Team
Headline: Kenya: Kilimo Defects to Orange Team
Body_Text:
Nairobi — State Minister Linah Chebii Kilimo's has deserted the Banana team for the Orange camp.
Impeccable sources said President Mwai Kibaki is having difficulties rallying his troops for a concerted onslaught against the advancing 'No' campaign.

Original_Search_Title: Raila Defends Himself, Jaramogi
Headline: Kenya: Raila Defends Himself, Jaramogi
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Roads minister Raila Odinga yesterday defended himself and his late father Jaramogi against criticism by members of the Banana team
In a statement, Raila accused his detractors of preaching ethnic chauvinism.

Original_Search_Title: Discontent As No Freebies Are Coming to Mt Kenya
Headline: Kenya: Discontent As No Freebies Are Coming to Mt Kenya
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A casual visitor would be forgiven for concluding that Nyeri and parts of Mt Kenya region are not in Kenya.
As referendum campaigns take centre stage throughout the country, no major rallies have been convened in the area, leaving seasoned campaigners, who expected a windfall from the events, disillusioned.

Original_Search_Title: Rival Referendum Camps Clash in Isiolo
Headline: Kenya: Rival Referendum Camps Clash in Isiolo
Body_Text:
Nairobi — People were injured yesterday when supporters of the Banana and Orange
campaigns clashed in Isiolo town.
Trouble started when the No team, led by former Isiolo North MP Haji
Charfano Mokku, were attacked by supporters of the Yes camp as they
renovated the local Kanu branch offices.

Original_Search_Title: Dangling of Carrot is No New Political Game
Headline: Kenya: Dangling of Carrot is No New Political Game
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Handing out carrots did not start with the referendum campaigns. As soon as the Narc came to power, it was keen to curry favour and the first beneficiaries were MPs.
When the 9th Parliament was sworn in, the first order of business was unprecedented increases in salaries, allowances and other perks. To cap it all, MPs were shielded from harambee contributions and given control of various constituency funds - development, bursary and Aids.

Original_Search_Title: Teams All Set to Step Up the Scramble for Mombasa
Headline: Kenya: Teams All Set to Step Up the Scramble for Mombasa
Body_Text:
Nairobi — With only 43 days left to the D-day, vote-hunting for the Banana and
Orange camps in Coast province is expected to intensify, especially in
Mombasa - the district with the highest number of registered voters in the
region.
Of the seven districts in the province, Mombasa's 281,091 registered
voters represent nearly one third of the total number of voters in the
province. All the four constituencies in the district - Changamwe, Kisauni,
Likoni and Mvita - are fancied hunting grounds.

Original_Search_Title: Mwenje's Ambition After Confinement
Headline: Kenya: Mwenje's Ambition After Confinement
Body_Text:
Nairobi — When the elected son of a constituency expresses ambition for the highest office in the land, it is more often than not a cause for happy celebration there. But the good people of Embakasi are behaving rather differently as they join their fellow countrymen in shrugging off the "good news" from their MP, Mr David Mwenje, that he will run for President in 2007.
The Orange and Banana bonanza has become a cesspit of intrigues, but trust Mr Mwenje to cut through all the pretences and stake his claim openly, never mind what you think of it. Never mind, too, that it took a spell in police cells and with all those mosquito bites to prompt him to scream out his ambition, at last. Others have done it before, from the late George Anyona and Mukaru Ng'ang'a to some folk like Koigi Wamwere who are now eating bananas.

Original_Search_Title: Movers And Shakers of Region's Vote
Headline: Kenya: Movers And Shakers of Region's Vote
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Apart from the provisions of the proposed new Constitution, Coast people
will either back or reject the new law depending on the influence of those
championing the interests of both the Orange and Banana camps.
Coast, for a long time, has had its power brokers and political
strategists who have determined how the region votes in general
elections.

Original_Search_Title: Mandaza Tastes Zanu PF Medicine
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mandaza Tastes Zanu PF Medicine
Body_Text:
A FEW months ago, I was invited to a private dinner along with a number of eminent Zimbabweans from various walks of life.
Among the guests was none other than my good friend and fellow journalist, Dr Ibbo Mandaza, the ardent Zanu PF supporter and favourite government media analyst and apologist. Some foreign dignitaries were also present.

Original_Search_Title: Jaguar Injures Six
Headline: Rwanda: Jaguar Injures Six
Body_Text:
Kigali — Barely months after a Regional Bus got an accident that claimed about 50 people, now another accident has occurred, this time involving a Jaguar coach.
The Kigali-bound coach injured six people critically after getting a head on collision with a Fuso truck loaded with bananas, about 40km from Kampala.

Original_Search_Title: Delicate Task of Nurturing Peace
Headline: Kenya: Delicate Task of Nurturing Peace
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Monkeys! Bloody monkeys! I hate you, I hate you and I hate you and I hate you ALL!" He was a man of Cushitic looks, his hair a huge mound under a baclava in Rasta colours. He was standing up in the plane, facing the tail end, despite the captain having switched on the seatbelt sign and announced our imminent arrival in Khartoum.
The man had been shouting and cursing in a mixture of English and Arabic. But until we started the descent to Khartoum, he had not exhibited threatening behaviour. But he had worked himself into a violent fury and it was looking as if we were going to have an incident 30,000 feet in the sky.

Original_Search_Title: Youth in Step With Bananas And Oranges
Headline: Kenya: Youth in Step With Bananas And Oranges
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Secondary school students have not been spared the growing euphoria over the upcoming vote on the proposed new Constitution. In Bungoma District, a group of students at Friends School, Kamusinga, have in their own small way literally picked up the battle for the Yes and No votes in the run-up to the November 21 referendum - but in a more ingenious manner. This was during a week-long cultural festival at their school, which ended with a gala night on Saturday last week.
Unlike politicians, who are scouring the country, fighting it out at public rallies and shouting themselves hoarse at dust-filled stadiums and trading centres, the students are "campaigning" on the theatre stage.

Original_Search_Title: Ngilu Absence From Home Rallies Raising Eyebrows
Headline: Kenya: Ngilu Absence From Home Rallies Raising Eyebrows
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The absence of Health Minister Charity Ngilu from a high-powered Banana campaign swing in Mwingi on Thursday has strengthened speculation that the temperamental Iron Lady of Ukambani was not quite in the Yes mix.
That was notwithstanding the fact that the Yes team - which included four cabinet ministers - was going to the home turf of Mrs Ngilu's avowed foe and Orange campaign adherent, the Environment Minister Kalonzo Musyoka.

Original_Search_Title: Envoy Warns of Loss of Donor Support Over State Resources
Headline: Kenya: Envoy Warns of Loss of Donor Support Over State Resources
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kenya risks losing donor budget support over the use of public resources in the campaigns, the German ambassador, Mr Bernd Braun, warned yesterday.
The country will find it difficult to justify why donors should continue to support the Budget when funds it receives from them are being wasted, he said.

Original_Search_Title: It's All Systems Go As Banana And Orange Camps Fight for Coast Votes
Headline: Kenya: It's All Systems Go As Banana And Orange Camps Fight for Coast Votes
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Referendum campaigns at the Coast are rekindling memories of the 2002 General Election.
And that is not just in tone, drama and acrimony. In the same manner that the Yes or No vote has split the Cabinet, so has it the Coast Parliamentary Group, the most powerful political pedestal and bargaining force for the region's 2002 voter estimate of 900,000.

Original_Search_Title: Hunting for the Maasai Votes
Headline: Kenya: Hunting for the Maasai Votes
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Struggle for land rights has always defined the tone of Maasai politics and leadership, writes John Kamau
Standing atop Mt Longonot, the harsh Maasai country stretches as far as the eye can see, just like the votes that it promises.

Original_Search_Title: Breaking the Law to Pass a Bad Law
Headline: Kenya: Breaking the Law to Pass a Bad Law
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Three chilling revelations emerged this past week: One, politicians in government will not hesitate to distort the truth, rob the taxpayer, bribe, incite, coerce, maim or incarcerate to pass that pretension of a constitution.
Two, the government is badly cornered. B

Original_Search_Title: Cracks in Yes Campaign As Forum Formed
Headline: Kenya: Cracks in Yes Campaign As Forum Formed
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Cracks emerged in the Yes Campaign team yesterday when some senior officials of the group launched a parallel secretariat in Nairobi.
The new secretariat will be headed by Mr Alex Muriithi, who was the National Rainbow Coalition (Narc) director of elections in the 2002 General Election.

Original_Search_Title: It is Bribery By the State, Claim Civil Society Leaders
Headline: Kenya: It is Bribery By the State, Claim Civil Society Leaders
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Members of the civil society, religious leaders and politicians in
Mombasa yesterday dismissed the councillors' pay increase and insurance
cover promises as political bribery by the government.
However, a section of councillors welcomed the pay rise but said it
would be difficult to implement due to financial problems most local
authorities in the country were facing.

Original_Search_Title: Yes Team to Venture Into Raila's Turf
Headline: Kenya: Yes Team to Venture Into Raila's Turf
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Three Nairobi Banana MPs yesterday launched a strategy to campaign in Roads minister Raila Odinga's Lang'ata Constituency.
Assistant ministers Maina Kamanda, Betty Tett and Beth Mugo said they had met grassroots leaders in the constituency who would spearhead the Yes campaign in the Orange leader's own backyard.

Original_Search_Title: War of Words On Draft Hots Up At Twin Rallies
Headline: Kenya: War of Words On Draft Hots Up At Twin Rallies
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The war of words over the proposed Constitution intensified yesterday as the Yes and No-vote teams went head to head with parallel rallies held only a few kilometres apart.
Twenty-one MPs from the Yes team, who included the Vice President and six ministers went to Gusii stadium while 23 MPs from the No group who included five ministers addressed a rally at Sameta.

Original_Search_Title: Confusion As MPs Snub Graca Meeting
Headline: Kenya: Confusion As MPs Snub Graca Meeting
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A planned meeting between former South African First Lady Dr Graca
Machel and Members of Parliament aborted after the MPs failed to turn
up.
There was confusion as Machel was forced to change her programme after
the meeting failed.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Plan Motion to Call Off Vote
Headline: Kenya: MPs Plan Motion to Call Off Vote
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A Motion seeking the postponement of the November referendum could be
brought to Parliament next week.
A group of MPs claiming neutrality in the constitution debate plan to
give notice of the Motion on Tuesday in the first step at efforts to broker
a ceasefire on a debate that has deeply divided the nation.

Original_Search_Title: Ministers Takes Yes Campaign to Rival's Backyard
Headline: Kenya: Ministers Takes Yes Campaign to Rival's Backyard
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Three Assistant ministers took the Yes campaign to Roads minister Raila
Odinga's Langata constituency, in Nairobi.
They accused Raila of pushing for an executive prime minister's office
through the back door.

Original_Search_Title: You Can't Trust Raila, Say 11 Yes Ministers
Headline: Kenya: You Can't Trust Raila, Say 11 Yes Ministers
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Eleven Cabinet ministers led by the Vice President Moody Awori yesterday
used the Yes (Banana) campaign to discredit Roads Minister Raila Odinga and
his late father Jaramogi.
The ministers who addressed two separate rallies - one in Bungoma and
another in Kisii - said Raila, who is leading the Orange (No) team together
with Kanu chairman Uhuru Kenyatta, could not be trusted.

Original_Search_Title: Gambia-Senegal Ferry Prices Down But Border Tension Still High
Headline: West Africa: Gambia-Senegal: Ferry Prices Down But Border Tension Still High
Body_Text:
Dakar — Gambia has announced plans to cut rates on ferry crossings to and from
Senegal, a possible first step in healing a widening rift between two
culturally-similar yet fractious neighbours left to deal with a difficult
border legacy.
As a gesture of goodwill marking the beginning of the Muslim holy month
of Ramadan, the government-owned Gambia Ports Authority (GPA) said this week
that it would partially reverse a fare increase that outraged Senegalese
transportation workers, already battered by high worldwide oil prices.

Original_Search_Title: Yes Team Out to Malign Orange Group, Says Raila
Headline: Kenya: Yes Team Out to Malign Orange Group, Says Raila
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Cabinet minister Raila Odinga yesterday claimed the Banana team had
launched a scheme to tarnish the Orange group's reputation.
Discussed at a Yes team meeting last week, the plot was to ensure that
voters endorse the proposed Constitution in the referendum.

Original_Search_Title: Big Pay Rise for Councillors As Vote Bribery Row Rages
Headline: Kenya: Big Pay Rise for Councillors As Vote Bribery Row Rages
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Councillors countrywide were given a hefty pay rise plus the promise of
free life insurance yesterday, even as the Government was being accused yet
again of making a series of concessions aimed at bribing voters to support
the proposed new Constitution.
As Local Government minister Musikari Kombo was revealing the pay rise
for councillors in Nairobi, his Cabinet colleague John Michuki was
announcing that chiefs and assistant chiefs will be sent letters next week
confirming they will still have jobs even if the new proposals which abolish
the Provincial Administration are accepted.

Original_Search_Title: State Warns Over Violence in Rallies
Headline: Kenya: State Warns Over Violence in Rallies
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government has warned against violence in today's referendum
campaign rallies in Gusiiland.
Nyanza provincial commissioner Hassan Noor said adequate security
measures had been put in place and asked politicians not to incite crowds to
hatred.

Original_Search_Title: Police At Rally Venues
Headline: Kenya: Police At Rally Venues
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There was heavy police presence in Kisii and Ogembo towns yesterday ahead of today's twin Orange and Banana rallies.
Police officers from neighbouring districts arrived in the two towns, which have been gripped by anxiety ahead of the rallies.

Original_Search_Title: Quit Now, 'Yes' Ministers Tell Orange Group
Headline: Kenya: Quit Now, 'Yes' Ministers Tell Orange Group
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Four Cabinet Ministers yesterday accused their counterparts fronting the Orange Campaign of sabotaging the Government and demanded that the five resign.
And several leaders interviewed by The Standard described the altercation between Roads minister Raila Odinga and his Lands counterpart, Amos Kimunya, in Parliament on Wednesday as shameful. However, Njenga Karume (Special Programmes), Joseph Munyao (Livestock) and John Michuki (Internal Security) said the clash in Parliament between Raila and Kimunya arose because the scheme to sabotage the Government had been exposed.

Original_Search_Title: Chiefs to Retain Their Jobs, Says Michuki
Headline: Kenya: Chiefs to Retain Their Jobs, Says Michuki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Banana team seeking support for the proposed new Constitution took
its campaign to Ukambani yesterday, with a rally in Mwingi district that was
skipped by Health minister Charity Ngilu.
During the meeting, Internal Security minister John Michuki announced
that the Government will issue chiefs and their assistants with letters next
week to reassure them of their jobs even after the new Constitution becomes
effective.

Original_Search_Title: Street Vendors Slip Back Quietly After Urban Clean Up Campaign
Headline: Zimbabwe: Street Vendors Slip Back Quietly After Urban Clean Up Campaign
Body_Text:
Harare — Five months after the Zimbabwean government cleared the streets of urban
centres across the country of vendors, beggars and street children, informal
traders are trickling back but are forced to play hide-and-seek as police
prowl the pavements on the lookout for illegal operators.
Pinos Muhacha leans against a wall, standing guard over empty cigarette
packets placed on a tattered cardboard box, a threadbare knapsack on his
back.

Original_Search_Title: Pambazuka news 224: 06 October 2005
Headline: Africa: Pambazuka news 224: 06 October 2005
Body_Text:
The Authoritative Electronic Weekly Newsletter And Platform For
Social Justice In Africa
NOTE: To view the links that are referenced below, please copy the
url and paste into your browswer.

Original_Search_Title: Raila And Kimunya Clash in Parliament
Headline: Kenya: Raila And Kimunya Clash in Parliament
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Cabinet ministers Raila Odinga and Amos Kimunya clashed in Parliament during an acrimonious debate over the use of public resources in the referendum campaigns.
MPs from the opposing camps in the Draft Constitution debate traded insults, banged tables and accused each other of insincerity during the heated exchanges on the debate.

Original_Search_Title: Kibwezi Squatters to Get Land
Headline: Kenya: Kibwezi Squatters to Get Land
Body_Text:
Nairobi — About 2,000 squatters in Kibwezi constituency were given 15,000 acres of
land, said the area MP yesterday.
Allotment letters for the land would be issued this month and titles
given shortly after that, said MP Kalembe Ndile.

Original_Search_Title: Kalembe Kicked Out Over Banana Team Attire
Headline: Kenya: Kalembe Kicked Out Over Banana Team Attire
Body_Text:
Nairobi — An outspoken MP was kicked out of Parliament for defying the speaker's
ruling to keep the referendum debate out of the House.
Deputy temporary speaker Samuel Poghisio (Kacheliba, Narc) ordered Mr
Kalembe Ndile (Kibwezi, Narc) out for entering the chamber wearing a
necklace with a ripe banana hanging on it.

Original_Search_Title: Teams to Hold Campaigns in Nyachae's Home Ground
Headline: Kenya: Teams to Hold Campaigns in Nyachae's Home Ground
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange and Banana teams will converge in Gusiiland tomorrow to step up their campaigns for the referendum on the proposed new Constitution.
Orange supporters will go to Gucha, while the Banana team will address a rally in Kisii Town to whip up support for the document.

Original_Search_Title: Banana Team Targets Voters in the Village
Headline: Kenya: Banana Team Targets Voters in the Village
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Banana campaigners in Kakamega are targeting villagers to canvass for
votes.
Ford-Kenya MPs from the district have concentrated their campaigns in
rural markets and churches for the past three weeks, instead of rallies in
major towns.

Original_Search_Title: Secretariats Beehive of Activity
Headline: Kenya: Secretariats Beehive of Activity
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Yes and No secretariats in Nairobi were yesterday a beehive of
activity as they geared up for the big poll.
The No camp was educating coordinators and meeting various lobby groups,
while the Yes group was giving out materials for distribution in the
constituencies.

Original_Search_Title: Behave Or Be Punished, Warns Kaparo
Headline: Kenya: Behave Or Be Punished, Warns Kaparo
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Punitive action will now be taken against MPs who create chaos and
turmoil in the House, speaker Francis ole Kaparo warned yesterday.
The tough-talking official said he would no longer sit back and watch
the dignity of the House being eroded.

Original_Search_Title: France Will Send Poll Observers, Says Envoy
Headline: Kenya: France Will Send Poll Observers, Says Envoy
Body_Text:
Nairobi — France will send observers to monitor the national referendum on
November 21.
French ambassador Hurbert Fournier said the observers would then compile
a report.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Allay Fears On Campaigns
Headline: Kenya: MPs Allay Fears On Campaigns
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Anxiety has gripped Kisii and Gucha districts, venues for twin Orange
and Banana rallies tomorrow.
Both camps have come out to allay fears of violence, with the No team
headed for a rally in Gucha saying it will not pass through Kisii town, that
will be hosting a Yes meeting.

Original_Search_Title: Unveiled: Sh2.5bn Bill for Vote On the Draft
Headline: Kenya: Unveiled: Sh2.5bn Bill for Vote On the Draft
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Preparations for the referendum on the proposed Constitution stepped
up a gear yesterday when the Electoral Commission of Kenya announced it
had a total budget of slightly more than Sh2.5 billion for the vital count
- and detailed exactly how the money would be spent.
Printing the 15 million ballot papers is about to begin, at a cost of
Sh600 million.

Original_Search_Title: Only Surprise in Taib's Political About-Turn Was in Its Timing
Headline: Kenya: Only Surprise in Taib's Political About-Turn Was in Its Timing
Body_Text:
Nairobi — When, on Saturday, Mombasa Mayor Taib Ali Taib finally declared his
stand in the raging referendum debate, many close associates were not
surprised that it was on the Orange side.
Before his declaration, Mombasa had been awash with rumours that the
civic leader was contemplating ditching the ruling coalition Narc- which
nominated him to the council - to join opposition Kanu.

Original_Search_Title: Councillors Paid for Attending Yes Meeting
Headline: Kenya: Councillors Paid for Attending Yes Meeting
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Each of the 295 councillors who attended Monday's Yes campaign meeting
in Kakamega will pocket a two-day nightout allowance of Sh7,000.
Local Government minister Musikari Kombo directed that the councillors
be paid the allowances amounting to Sh2,065,000 in the presence of the
Provincial Local Government Officer, Mr Charles Onchieku.

Original_Search_Title: 'I Am Interested in Resting'
Headline: Uganda: 'I Am Interested in Resting'
Body_Text:
In an exclusive interview with the mayor of Kampala, Ssebaana Kizito, Simon Kasyate asked him about the bottlenecks he has faced and whether he has intentions to participate in active politics
It has been six years now that you have been Kampala Mayor, how can you rate your performance?

Original_Search_Title: Violence Undermines Gains Made in Tourism Sector
Headline: Kenya: Violence Undermines Gains Made in Tourism Sector
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Fears expressed by industry players that the violence being witnessed in
the country ahead of the November 21 constitutional referendum could
seriously damage Kenya's tourism sector are not far-fetched.
Earnings from tourism rose by more than Ksh13 billion ($175.6 million)
in 2004, which is more than 50 per cent of the money made the previous
year. But with the international media focusing on the violence, the gains
registered could be easily lost.

Original_Search_Title: Welcome to East Africa, the Land of Continous Electioneering
Headline: East Africa: Welcome to East Africa, the Land of Continous Electioneering
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In Kenya, the politicians have taken to the hills and valleys to push
for a Yes vote in the November 21 referendum on the proposed new
constitution (this is the Banana group), and for a No vote (this is the
Orange camp).
In Tanzania too, four-wheel drive cars are toughing it out on impassable
rural roads as candidates shop for votes in the end-of-October elections. It
seems less exciting, because Jakaya Kikwete, the candidate of the ruling
Chama cha Mapinduzi (CCM), is so sure of victory, he has probably already
sent out invitation cards for his swearing-in party.

Original_Search_Title: Accusing Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Accusing Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Roads minister Raila Odinga and Kanu Chairman Uhuru Kenyatta yesterday
led the Orange team in accusing President Kibaki of turning to "bribery" in
the referendum war.
They dismissed the President Kibaki's decree that part of Mau Forest
where the Government recently violently evicted inhabitants be opened up for
selected squatters and the rest be resettled elsewhere, as some of the
'sweeteners' he was dishing out in exchange for a Yes vote.

Original_Search_Title: Ford-K Split By Wrangles And Kombo's 'Yes' Position
Headline: Kenya: Ford-K Split By Wrangles And Kombo's 'Yes' Position
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ford Kenya officials, already affected by internal wrangles, are now
sharply divided on the referendum.
While the chairman, Musikari Kombo, recently announced that the party
supports the Banana campaign, various constituencies in the party's Western
Province stronghold have rallied behind the Orange team.

Original_Search_Title: Don't Burn Bridges, Orange Team Told
Headline: Kenya: Don't Burn Bridges, Orange Team Told
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Justice and Constitutional Affairs assistant minister Njeru Githae
yesterday cautioned the critics of the Wako draft against burning their
"political bridges".
Githae described those blaming President Mwai Kibaki for perceived
mistakes in the draft constitution as "naÃve".

Original_Search_Title: Youths Attack Reporters At Yes Meeting
Headline: Kenya: Youths Attack Reporters At Yes Meeting
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A journalist was injured when armed youths attacked reporters covering a Yes campaign meeting yesterday.
The reporters from the Nation and Standard were attacked outside a hotel in Kakamega Town, where Vice-President Moody Awori and ministers Musikari Kombo and Mukhisa Kituyi met councillors to drum up support for the proposed new Constitution.

Original_Search_Title: Mayor's No Call Dismissed
Headline: Kenya: Mayor's No Call Dismissed
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Mombasa mayor Taib Ali Taib's support for a No vote was yesterday
dismissed as a ploy to divert public attention from corruption at the
council.
Tourism minister Morris Dzoro and assistant ministers Anania Mwaboza
and Danson Mungatana told Mr Taib to focus on ending misuse of funds at
the council, where they said corruption was rampant.

Original_Search_Title: Press Freedom Must Come With Responsibility!
Headline: Rwanda: Press Freedom Must Come With Responsibility!
Body_Text:
Kigali — It is said that the media thrive on chaos, war, human suffering and all
other things that make life miserable and gloomy.
The sensationalist types, which have found their way from London - the
home of the tabloids - to the streets of African cities, are a case in
point. Gossip, especially if it pries into the private lives of the high and
mighty, sells faster than bananas and oranges at referendum rallies in
Kenya. You will be attracted by a headline that will hint at something that
is non-existent in the story.

Original_Search_Title: Wako And Kalonzo in Face-Off On New Draft
Headline: Kenya: Wako And Kalonzo in Face-Off On New Draft
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Attorney-General Amos Wako and Environment minister Kalonzo Musyoka yesterday faced off over the proposed Constitution, as Germany warned the Government that donors might withdraw billions of shillings promised to help inform people about the Draft.
The showdown came in front of the cameras at the home of the German ambassador to Kenya, Mr Bernd Braun, during celebrations of the 15th anniversary of German reunification.

Original_Search_Title: 'Yes' Team Winds Up Coast Tour, Plans Kisumu Rally
Headline: Kenya: 'Yes' Team Winds Up Coast Tour, Plans Kisumu Rally
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Coast Banana campaign team wound up their Lamu tour on Sunday and
declared that their next stop would be Moi Stadium in Kisumu.
Tourism and Wildlife minister Morris Dzoro said they would traverse the
country to promote their agenda for the proposed constitution.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Finally Walk to Freedom
Headline: Kenya: MPs Finally Walk to Freedom
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Embakasi MP David Mwenje and his Makadara counterpart, Reuben Ndolo,
yesterday strode to freedom after three nights of battling with cold and
swarms of mosquitoes in police cells.
Makadara Senior Principal Magistrate Mrs Grace Nzioka freed the MPs on
Sh10,000 cash bail after they denied breaking two chairs and a table worth
Sh 14,000 belonging to City Cabanas Hotel in Nairobi where violence broke
out after they stormed a Banana campaign strategy meeting.

Original_Search_Title: Mwenje's Family Protests As They Fail to Trace Him
Headline: Kenya: Mwenje's Family Protests As They Fail to Trace Him
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Embakasi MP David Mwenje's family yesterday raised fears over his health after failing to trace him at 10 police stations. The family said the MP, who is diabetic, had not taken his drugs since Friday, adding that this was likely to worsen his condition.
At the same time, the Orange team yesterday accused the Government of "persecuting" Mr Mwenje and his Makadara MP Reuben Ndolo, who were arrested on Friday. The team said the confinement of the MPs at various undisclosed police stations was unfair, adding the arrest had sinister motive.

Original_Search_Title: Kenyans Abroad Decry Campaign Violence
Headline: Kenyans Abroad Decry Campaign Violence
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kenyans working or studying overseas have expressed concern over what they call increased violence and the use of government resources in the campaigns.
The Kenya Community Abroad organisation said it was disturbed by what it called wanton disrespect for the rule of law in the Banana and Orange camps.

Original_Search_Title: Why the Referendum Must Go On
Headline: Kenya: Why the Referendum Must Go On
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The marathon that saw the putting together of the proposed New Constitution for Kenya and the lives that have been lost might have been thought to have ended at the publication of the so-called Wako draft.
This may not be the case. The race continues as acrimony and heat at the campaigns in support of the respective positions taken for the oncoming referendum is evident. Contrary to political propaganda, there has been no short-cut to the process that has culminated in the current dilemma of whether to choose yes or no.

Original_Search_Title: Kombo Takes Yes Campaign to Home Turf
Headline: Kenya: Kombo Takes Yes Campaign to Home Turf
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ford Kenya chairman Musikari Kombo took the Yes campaign to his Bungoma home turf at the weekend and said a vote for the proposed new Constitution would be one for good governance and continued economic growth.
The country would slide back into the bad governance reminiscent of the Kanu era if the Orange group won, he warned.

Original_Search_Title: Raila Wanted to Be VP in 2002, Minister Says
Headline: Kenya: Raila Wanted to Be VP in 2002, Minister Says
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ford Kenya chairman Musikari Kombo has claimed Roads minister Raila
Odinga wanted to be offered the vice presidency when he led Kanu defectors
to Narc in 2002.
Kombo said the Liberal Democratic Party leader and Langata MP was bitter
with President Mwai Kibaki's administration because he had been denied the
VP position, which had been reserved for the late Michael Wamalwa.

Original_Search_Title: Warning Over Public Funds
Headline: Kenya: Warning Over Public Funds
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government has been warned not to misuse public resources in its quest to push for the adoption of the proposed Constitution.
Cabinet minister William ole Ntimama cautioned that those who would use taxpayers' money to fund their campaigns would be held accountable, one day.

Original_Search_Title: Parliament Unlikely to Cope With Bills, Says ICJ
Headline: Kenya: Parliament Unlikely to Cope With Bills, Says ICJ
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The International Commission of Jurists (ICJ) Kenya has said Parliament was unlikely to enact various pieces of law prescribed in the proposed constitution if it sails through.
Kanyi Kimondo, the ICJ Kenya chapter secretary, said Parliament only passes about five pieces of legislation annually and wondered whether MPs will be able to pass the Wako Bill in such a limited period.

Original_Search_Title: Forcing Bill On People Could Be Treason, Says Raila
Headline: Kenya: Forcing Bill On People Could Be Treason, Says Raila
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Roads minister Raila Odinga yesterday warned that it would amount to treason if the President were to force the Wako draft into a new constitution.
He said pro-Banana ministers were out to overthrow the constitutional order and that the Orange team would ensure that the Proposed New Constitution is rejected at the referendum.

Original_Search_Title: Petrol Bombs Scare Disrupts Nairobi Banana Rally
Headline: Kenya: Petrol Bombs Scare Disrupts Nairobi Banana Rally
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There was a stampede at a Banana rally in Nairobi yesterday after three bottles suspected to be petrol bombs were thrown at the main dais.
Though they didn't explode, they caused panic and speeches were interrupted for about 20 minutes.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Gives Out Land for 'Yes' Review Vote
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Gives Out Land for 'Yes' Review Vote
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government will resettle families evicted from Mau Forest before the November 21 referendum in a move clearly aimed at wrong-footing the No platform.
President Kibaki will announce the resettlement of the families this week as the Yes platform, which was re-launched on Saturday in Nakuru, gathers momentum.

Original_Search_Title: Ford-K Boss to Meet Western Councillors
Headline: Kenya: Ford-K Boss to Meet Western Councillors
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ford-Kenya Chairman Musikari Kombo will today hold a crucial meeting in Kakamega town with all civic leaders from Western Province to discuss the referendum.
Kombo is expected to sell the 'Yes' stand taken last week after the party's executive meeting.

Original_Search_Title: Orengo Return Big Boost for Orange Team
Headline: Kenya: Orengo Return Big Boost for Orange Team
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Clearly, it is a replay of the 2002 General Election. Mammoth rallies,
accusations and counter-accusations. Tension, threats and hot campaigns. As
the country awaits the first referendum polls ever, Nyanza is
boiling. Politicians from both sides of the divide are burning the midnight
oil, laying campaign strategies as the clock ticks to the November 21
referendum.
The Orange team, led by the Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) and Kanu
luminaries, claims it has won the contest but the Banana camp, fronted by
President Kibaki himself and other Cabinet ministers, says the match is just
about to begin.

Original_Search_Title: Mayor Taib Shifts to the Orange Campaign
Headline: Kenya: Mayor Taib Shifts to the Orange Campaign
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange campaign yesterday received an unexpected boost at the coast
as Mombasa Mayor Taib Ali Taib led 24 councillors in declaring opposition to
the proposed new Constitution. Mr Taib also said that he would be working
with his erstwhile political rival, National Heritage minister Najib Balala,
to ensure success for the No campaign in Coast Province.
Putting aside political differences seemed to be the order of the day in
the Yes campaign as well as yesterday in Kakamega, Vice-President Moody
Awori and two Cabinet ministers said they would work together to give the
Yes campaign victory in Western Province.

Original_Search_Title: Politicians Welcome Path to Political Rebirth With Action
Headline: Kenya: Politicians Welcome Path to Political Rebirth With Action
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As campaigns for and against the Proposed New Constitution continue,
former MPs from Western Province who were swept aside by the 2002 Narc
euphoria have found grounds to reinvent themselves.
Most of the re-emerging leaders are opposed to the proposed law,
ostensibly to discredit sitting MPs, many of who support the banana
camp.

Original_Search_Title: Rival Camps Hold Campaign Rallies
Headline: Kenya: Rival Camps Hold Campaign Rallies
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The political tide against allies of the Wako Draft mounted yesterday
when Mombasa Mayor Taib Ali Taib and 14 councillors abandoned the 'Yes' camp
and joined the Orange team.
The announcement came as prominent Maasai leaders led by Cabinet
Minister William ole Ntimama and John Keen led thousands of the Maa
community in rejecting the Draft at a rally in Suswa, Narok District.

Original_Search_Title: Former MPs Take Strategic Positions in 'Yes' And 'No' Camps
Headline: Kenya: Former MPs Take Strategic Positions in 'Yes' And 'No' Camps
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In the Central Rift, some former legislators have dusted their boots and
hit the campaign trail in support of the Yes and No camps.
With the chance to revive their political careers, the former MPs from
Nakuru, Baringo, Koibatek and Bomet districts have taken up strategic
positions in the two camps.

Original_Search_Title: A Campaign Tool for Next General Election
Headline: Kenya: A Campaign Tool for Next General Election
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Former MPs from Gusii are using the referendum campaigns to popularise themselves for the 2007 General Election.
The referendum, observers say, offers them the opportunity, and those who read the mood well, may find the going easy in 2007.

Original_Search_Title: They Are Banana, Orange Cues for 2007
Headline: Kenya: They Are Banana, Orange Cues for 2007
Body_Text:
Nairobi — At the Coast, six politicians who lost in the 2002 General Election following the Narc euphoria are returning through a similar wave -referendum campaigns.
The former MPs could be slowly re-gaining popularity through the No campaign.

Original_Search_Title: Are We Going Bananas Over New Law?
Headline: Kenya: Are We Going Bananas Over New Law?
Body_Text:
Nairobi — If I were to judge my friends based on the number of lies they've told me, it is unlikely that I would have so many. "You look wonderful!" they often told me in my ninth month of pregnancy. That, despite my hugely swollen nose, big belly and even bigger behind.
Pretentiously, I claim not to have known better because my cheeks were so fat that I could hardly see the mirror but, well let's be honest; I did know the truth and only took their lies because like food, they appealed to me.

Original_Search_Title: VP And Four Ministers Take Yes Vote to Grassroots
Headline: Kenya: VP And Four Ministers Take Yes Vote to Grassroots
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Vice President Moody Awori and four Cabinet ministers yesterday took the Yes campaign to the grassroots.
The Banana team held separate meetings with local leaders in Busia and Bungoma and at public rallies in Marsabit and Kisii.

Original_Search_Title: State Hands Back Amboseli National Park to the Maasai
Headline: Kenya: State Hands Back Amboseli National Park to the Maasai
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government has handed back Amboseli National Park to the Maasai in what appears to be a move to woo the community into the Banana camp before the November 21 referendum.
President Kibaki on Thursday directed that a legal notice be issued to ensure the park was returned to the community as trust land.

Original_Search_Title: Femalespeak: the Perfect Politician
Headline: Kenya: Femalespeak: the Perfect Politician
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Last weekend, my friends and I got together for a brainstorming session to discuss matters of personal concern. Our perpetual state of broke-ness and our lack of impressive social status and clout have become rather worrying.
So, we decided to band together and devise a strategy to get ourselves out of this state of hopelessness. Various business ideas were mooted - garbage collection, trading in body parts and opening a brothel were some of the ones that came up. But after lots of discussion, we landed upon a killer idea that is guaranteed to earn us lots of money and lots of social clout.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Appeals to Mbeki Over Besigye
Headline: Uganda in Plea to SA Over Besigye
Body_Text:
Kampala — President Yoweri Museveni has appealed to South African President Thabo Mbeki to stop former presidential candidate Kizza Besigye from using the country to campaigns against his government.
The President told a press conference at State House Nakasero that had Uganda not been a "liberal country," his government would have asked South Africa to "throw him (Besigye) out or shut him up."

Original_Search_Title: Nyachae Scoffs At Claims of Ebbing Political Clout
Headline: Kenya: Nyachae Scoffs At Claims of Ebbing Political Clout
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Contrary to claims by the Orange group, Ford People leader Simeon Nyachae was still popular in his own backyard, he asserted yesterday.
He challenged the Orange crusaders gunning for a No vote in the referendum on the proposed new Constitution to a popularity contest during the Yes campaign at Kisii stadium next Friday.

Original_Search_Title: Now VP Takes Yes Campaign to His Home Turf
Headline: Kenya: Now VP Takes Yes Campaign to His Home Turf
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Vice President Moody Awori yesterday took a quiet Banana campaign to his backyard in Funyula constituency in Western Province and decried mass ignorance on the proposed constitution.
The VP expressed concern that propaganda had infiltrated the campaign and called for fair judgement on the Wako draft.

Original_Search_Title: Electoral Commission to Vet Teachers for Poll Jobs
Headline: Kenya: ECK to Vet Teachers for Poll Jobs
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Teachers seeking referendum jobs will be thoroughly vetted because their
union is partisan, the Electoral Commission said yesterday.
ECK chairman Samuel Kivuitu said the electoral rules barred it from
hiring anyone whose political stand was publicly known. Therefore only
non-partisan teachers would be enlisted for voter education and other
roles.

Original_Search_Title: To Save This Country, Don't Vote
Headline: Kenya: To Save This Country, Don't Vote
Body_Text:
Nairobi — English statesman Winston Churchill once said that democracy is the
worst form of government except for all those others that have been
tried.
It would be impossible for any thoughtful observer of the opposing
referendum campaigns to reach a contrary conclusion. Even so, the difference
is that the competing Kenyan political elites do not have the foggiest idea
about the proper exercise of democratic freedoms.

Original_Search_Title: Health Guidelines for Ramadan
Headline: Gambia: Health Guidelines for Ramadan
Body_Text:
Banjul — The Holy Month of Ramadan is just few days away, which is why it is
important for Muslims to get prepared for this blessed month.
This is why we present an article being authored by a prominent Islamic
scholar of Islamic Medical Association of South Africa, Dr. Farouk
Haffejee. The article gives a lot of insights about common problems being
encountered during Ramadan. Those who follow the advice contained in the
article will comfortably fast and enjoy the spiritual benefits of the
blessed month.

Original_Search_Title: Orange Team Raises Query Over Voters' Registers
Headline: Kenya: Orange Team Raises Query Over Voters' Registers
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange team yesterday claimed that it had unearthed a plot to deny
its supporters a chance to vote during the November 21 referendum.
In a statement read by Eldoret North MP William Ruto, the team claimed
there were anomalies in the voter registers, which included wrong spelling
of names or wrong ID and voting card numbers.

Original_Search_Title: What Credentials Does a Hero Need?
Headline: Zimbabwe: What Credentials Does a Hero Need?
Body_Text:
VERY few events have such stunning emotional similarities as the burial of Henry Hamadziripi on Monday at Glen Forest Cemetery and the interment of Sheba Tavarwisa at a remote village in rural Gutu.
Both were icons of the Zimbabwe war for national independence. Coincidentally, both came from the same rural area and both were inexplicably denied national hero status although they deserved burial at the national Heroes' Acre.

Original_Search_Title: 'Yes' Side Gets Nod for Rally As 'No' Camp Cries Foul
Headline: Kenya: 'Yes' Side Gets Nod for Rally As 'No' Camp Cries Foul
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange team lost its bid to hold a rally in Kisii Town on October 7
as police yesterday gave the Banana camp an okay to campaign at the same
venue.
But the losers for the Gusii Stadium venue announced they would hold
their rally in the neighbouring Gucha District, in Ogembo Town, on the same
day.

Original_Search_Title: Ministers Attack Envoys Over Warning to Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Ministers Attack Envoys Over Warning to Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Eight Cabinet ministers yesterday reacted angrily to a statement by
Kenya's development partners that State resources should not be used in the
on-going referendum campaigns.
The ministers branded the 27 envoys, who on Wednesday spoke out against
the use of public resources and violence in the campaigns, supporters of the
Orange (No) campaign.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Hide in Parliament to Avoid Arrest
Headline: Kenya: MPs Hide in Parliament to Avoid Arrest
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Embakasi MP David Mwenje and his Makadara counterpart, Reuben Ndolo,
were last night holed up in Parliament's precincts where they took advantage
of its privileged status to avoid arrest.
A contingent of about 50 police officers had taken position outside
Parliament waiting to pounce on the MPs following claims that they had
invaded and disrupted a Banana meeting called by two Assistant ministers on
Wednesday.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Asks Mbeki to Shut Up Col. Besigye
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Asks Mbeki to Shut Up Col. Besigye
Body_Text:
President Yoweri Museveni has called South African head of state Thabo
Mbeki and asked him to stop former Presidential candidate, Col. Dr Kizza
Besigye from using the country to launch political campaigns against the
Ugandan government.
The President said that had Uganda not been a "liberal country",
government would have asked South Africa to "throw him (Besigye) out".

Original_Search_Title: Shots Fired After Two MPs Disrupt Yes Talks
Headline: Kenya: Shots Fired After Two MPs Disrupt Yes Talks
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Shots were fired after two MPs opposed to the proposed Constitution yesterday stormed a meeting called to drum up support for the new law.
The shots came as the MPs were chased from the meeting hall by a crowd of Yes supporters who threatened to attack the two.

Original_Search_Title: Minister Warns of Big Fallout in Cabinet
Headline: Kenya: Minister Warns of Big Fallout in Cabinet
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Cabinet minister Prof Anyang' Nyong'o yesterday called on President
Kibaki to summon an urgent Cabinet meeting to discuss the referendum.
The Planning minister warned of a major fallout in Narc over the
proposed draft if no action was taken to diffuse rifts in the
coalition.

Original_Search_Title: 25 Donor States Warn Kibaki On Referendum
Headline: Kenya: 25 Donor States Warn Kibaki On Referendum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kenya's development partners yesterday waded into the controversy over
the use of State resources in the campaigns for the November 21
referendum.
Twenty-five foreign missions warned against the use of State resources
in the 'Yes' (Banana) and No (Orange) campaigns on the proposed
constitution.

Original_Search_Title: ECK Wants Orange, Banana Teams to Abide By the Rules
Headline: Kenya: ECK Wants Orange, Banana Teams to Abide By the Rules
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Electoral Commission of Kenya will not allow the Banana and Orange teams to campaign on the referendum, while their agents will be ejected out of polling stations, if the groups fail to register them with the commission.
ECK Vice Chairman Gabriel Mukele warned yesterday that the commission would not accept agents of unregistered groups to campaign for the 'Yes' or 'No' vote.

Original_Search_Title: Ford-K Calls 'No' Team 'Liars' And Goes Banana
Headline: Kenya: Ford-K Calls 'No' Team 'Liars' And Goes Banana
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ford Kenya chairman Muskari Kombo addresses a press conference in Nairobi yesterday, where he declared the party would support the proposed constitution. He is with a party member and former freedom fighter Achieng' Oneko.
Ford Kenya yesterday opened up a new front in the raging referendum war by declaring it was firmly in the Banana team and branded the Orange group as thriving on lies and distortions.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Predicts a 'Yes' Win, Terms Orange Team Jokers
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Predicts a 'Yes' Win, Terms Orange Team Jokers
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki yesterday said the momentum was with the 'Yes' campaign and predicted that it would triumph in the November 21 referendum.
And the President accordingly advised the 'No' campaign to read the signs of the times, join the winning team and wait for another chance to try for victory.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Excited As Referendum Fever Catches the House
Headline: Kenya: MPs Excited As Referendum Fever Catches the House
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The heavily plastered right hand of Nyatike MP Ochola Ogur could have as well been illustrative of the bruising nature of the month-long recess that ended yesterday.
Apparently in jest, a colleague teased Ogur that he had probably suffered the injury in a scramble for oranges (orange is the symbol for the 'NO' vote in the November 21 referendum).

Original_Search_Title: Magara Threatens to Table Motion On MPs' Sh105m
Headline: Kenya: Magara Threatens to Table Motion On MPs' Sh105m
Body_Text:
Nairobi — South Mugirango legislator James Magara has threatened to table a motion to censor the Treasury and Members of Parliament who received Sh500,000 each for the Banana campaign.
Speaking to The Standard in Ogembo Town, Magara said Kenyans were robbed of Sh105 million by people they elected to represent them.He said it was illegal for the Government to use public funds to campaign for the 'Yes' vote.

Original_Search_Title: Teachers Urge No Vote As Ford-K Backs the Yes Camp
Headline: Kenya: Teachers Urge No Vote As Ford-K Backs the Yes Camp
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Teachers gave the Orange team a big boost yesterday by announcing they
would back the No vote in the referendum on the proposed new Constitution,
while Ford Kenya finally threw its weight behind the Banana group
campaigning for a Yes vote.
It had been widely expected that the teachers' would endorse the Draft
as a way of thanking the Government for honouring the teachers' 1997 pay
deal.

Original_Search_Title: Call for Team to Fix Proposed Law
Headline: Kenya: Call for Team to Fix Proposed Law
Body_Text:
Nairobi — An archbishop wants a neutral committee formed to look into the
contentious issues before a motion seeking to postpone the referendum is
moved in Parliament.
Kisumu Catholic archbishop Zacchaeus Okoth said Kenyans had a right to
be heard on the "objectionable areas" of the proposed law.

Original_Search_Title: Editors And No Group Hold Talks
Headline: Kenya: Editors And No Group Hold Talks
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Leading lights of the Orange movement, which supports a No-vote in the
Constitution referendum, met senior Nation Media Group editors
yesterday.
The two-hour private meeting, held at the editors' request, focused on
the group's coverage of their campaign for a no-vote in the Constitution
referendum to be held in November.

Original_Search_Title: Hybrid Banana Thrives As 'Kayinja' Survives
Headline: Uganda: Hybrid Banana Thrives As 'Kayinja' Survives
Body_Text:
Mityana — FOR many years, Bulera, Ssekanyonyi and Bukuya in Mityana district were
the highest producers of kayinja bananas in the country. Kayinja grows on
stony grounds, which is abundant in Mityana. However, this changed in the
last few years. Kayinja was destroyed by the banana wilt disease.
"Our development has retarded since the disease attacked kayinja. We are
yet to find an alternative," says Christine Kitaka, a resident of
Kyamusisi. These are species mainly used to produce banana juice.

Original_Search_Title: Uganda to Host UN Research
Headline: Uganda to Host UN Research
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDA has been selected alongside nine other African countries to host a UN millennium research village project.
The other countries are Senegal, Ethiopia, Mali, Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, Rwanda, Tanzania and Malawi.

Original_Search_Title: Church Warns On Crusades
Headline: Kenya: Church Warns On Crusades
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The referendum campaigns have been turned into a political supremacy contest between certain politicians and tribes, a church head has said.
Archbishop Benjamin Nzimbi of the Anglican Church in Kenya (ACK) expressed concern that the political atmosphere - "too much violence and too much noise" - was not conducive to civic education.

Original_Search_Title: Why Marwa, Why?
Headline: Nigeria: Why Marwa, Why?
Body_Text:
I was in London in 1996 when the late General Sani Abacha named
Brigadier-General Muhammad Buba Marwa administrator of Lagos state. If you
believed the erstwhile NADECO irredentists, a great majority of who have now
obviously lost their fangs and venom, the period coincided with the darkest
period of our nationhood.
And in truth, Abacha, who was an infantry General, took very few
prisoners and was unsparing with the rod in dealing with the 'opposition'
most of whom, had cases to answer before the various Failed Bank's Tribunals
at the time anyway. At that time public resentment over the military
annulment of the June 12 elections had been reduced to a sectional affair,
no thanks to the tactless attitude and slaug-hter of innocent northerners by
our brethren from the Southwest axis of the country earlier in 1992. Indeed,
the orgy of unbridled violence and lawlessness encou-raged Abacha's
intervention and much of what followed there-after. And so it was that over
lunch, on a steamy summer after-noon in 1996, I found myself in the midst of
no fewer that four compatriots from the southwest at the time the Marwa
appoin-tment was made public.

Original_Search_Title: Now MPs Take Draft Battle Back to House
Headline: Kenya: Now MPs Take Draft Battle Back to House
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A group of MPs are taking the battle over the proposed new Constitution back to Parliament as it reopens today, raising hopes of finding an alternative to continued fighting over the referendum.
The MPs want the referendum delayed until consensus is found over the contentious issues in the Draft currently being put to the public.

Original_Search_Title: Battle Goes to House
Headline: Kenya: Battle Goes to House
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The referendum battle on the proposed constitution moves to Parliament this afternoon when the House resumes after a three-week recess.
The first contentious issue on the table will be the threat by Kanu and the Liberal Democratic Party to move a motion of censure against Justice and Constitutional Affairs minister Kiraitu Murungi.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Want Voters to Read Proposed Document
Headline: Kenya: MPs Want Voters to Read Proposed Document
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Several Coast MPs in the Banana camp on Sunday asked voters to read the Wako draft and make choices at the referendum.
Only Garsen MP Danson Mungatana pushed for the 'Yes' vote during a meeting held in Kilifi District.

Original_Search_Title: Do Not Threaten Journalists, Media Chiefs Tell Politicians
Headline: Kenya: Do Not Threaten Journalists, Media Chiefs Tell Politicians
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Media Owners Association has asked politicians who feel aggrieved or get unfair coverage from media houses to follow the right channels in seeking redress.
Ms Rose Kimotho, the association's chairperson, told politicians to seek audience with the respective media houses instead of issuing blanket bans or threats.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Leads 'Yes' Team to Isiolo
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Leads 'Yes' Team to Isiolo
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki this morning hits the campaign trail in Isiolo in an attempt to rally the vast Eastern Province to vote "Yes" in the November 21 referendum.
And even as Parliament re-opens today amids rising tensions that could paralyse Government Business, calls for the referendum to be called off and the middle ground group planning a Bill to postpone the plebiscite, the President has affirmed the process will go on.

Original_Search_Title: Orange MPs Launch Campaign Movement
Headline: Kenya: Orange MPs Launch Campaign Movement
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange team concluded the first leg of its campaign in Kisumu yesterday with the launch of the Orange Democratic Movement to spearhead the rest of the lobbying.
Unveiling the new movement under what he described as the Kisumu Declaration, the National Heritage minister, Najib Balala, said it would create political awareness in the country.

Original_Search_Title: Orange MPs Launch Campaign Movement
Headline: Kenya: Orange MPs Launch Campaign Movement
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange team concluded the first leg of its campaign in Kisumu yesterday with the launch of the Orange Democratic Movement to spearhead the rest of the lobbying.
Unveiling the new movement under what he described as the Kisumu Declaration, the National Heritage minister, Najib Balala, said it would create political awareness in the country.

Original_Search_Title: Raila, Uhuru Launch 2007 Orange Coalition
Headline: Kenya: Raila, Uhuru Launch 2007 Orange Coalition
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Roads minister Raila Odinga and Kanu chairman Uhuru Kenyatta yesterday
launched a new political coalition ahead of the 2007 General Election as
they wound up the first phase of the Orange campaign.
The coalition named Orange Democratic Movement was unveiled in a
star-studded rally in Kisumu attended by at least 40 Members of Parliament
drawn from the Liberal Democratic Party and Kanu. In what was dubbed "The
Kisumu Declaration", Heritage minister Najib Balala announced the formation
of the movement, which he said was capable of wresting power from President
Kibaki.

Original_Search_Title: Lobby Group in Threat to Sue Ministers
Headline: Kenya: Lobby Group in Threat to Sue Ministers
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A lobby group has threatened to take legal action to stop Cabinet
ministers from using public resources during the on-going campaigns for the
November 21 referendum.
The Institute of Education for Democracy (IED) said it was the height of
arrogance for some ministers to publicly declare they would use public
resources to drum up support for the "Yes" vote.

Original_Search_Title: Don't Be Deceived On Bomas, Banana Group Tells Kenyans
Headline: Kenya: Don't Be Deceived On Bomas, Banana Group Tells Kenyans
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Four MPs, including two ministers, have warned Kenyans not to be
deceived that by rejecting the Wako Draft, the Bomas Draft would be adopted
instead.
Terming this as "a misconception and day-dream", they said some leaders
were telling Kenyans that by rejecting the propsed constitution the country
would revert to the Bomas Draft.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Brands 'No' Vote Team 'Liars'
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Brands 'No' Vote Team 'Liars'
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki yesterday bounced back on the 'Yes' vote campaign trail
branding the Orange team, which is opposed to the proposed constitution, as
"liars".
The President, who returned home on Friday from an official trip abroad,
went to Embu where he declared that the Orange team was insincere as it was
part of the process that gave birth to the document to be put to vote in the
November 21 referendum.

Original_Search_Title: 'Nation' And Citizen Locked Out of Rallies
Headline: Kenya: 'Nation' And Citizen Locked Out of Rallies
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange team excluded two media houses from covering their rally in Kisumu yesterday.
The latest action on the Nation Media and Royal Media groups was a culmination of nearly two weeks of threats to ban the two from covering orange rallies in the on-going campaigns ahead of the referendum on the Proposed New Constitution set for November 21.

Original_Search_Title: Group Warns On State Funds
Headline: Kenya: Group Warns On State Funds
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Leaders in the No campaign have asked President Kibaki to block ministers from using public resources in the referendum campaigns.
Nineteen MPs opposed to the proposed new Constitution warned of a national disaster if supporters of the document continued to use public funds.

Original_Search_Title: Is Democracy Worth the Pain?
Headline: Kenya: Is Democracy Worth the Pain?
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A diplomat once told me the reason he finds our newspapers "boring" is
that there are too many journalists quoting dead poets and ancient
philosophers. I may have quoted a dead poet here and there in a whimsical
moment, but I have never quoted Socrates, Plato, Aristotle. Not until
now. And what an occasion it is!
First, the Voices of the People global survey, which monitors trends in
democracy, has just been released and reports that 79 per cent of humanity
believes democracy to be the best form of government.

Original_Search_Title: Cracks Emerge in Churches Over Voting
Headline: Kenya: Cracks Emerge in Churches Over Voting
Body_Text:
Nairobi — No section of Kenyan society has been spared the bitter exchanges over
the referendum. But most prominent are the cracks that have emerged in the
mainstream religious organisations.
For fear that the vote will tear apart both their leaderships and
flocks, they have opted for the neutral ground.

Original_Search_Title: A Lone Voice Among the Commissioners
Headline: Kenya: A Lone Voice Among the Commissioners
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As the civic education programme for the referendum enters its second
week, one member of the Constitution of Kenya Review Commission has decided
to go against the tide.
The commissioners and civic education providers are expected to defend
their "baby", but Mr Mutakha Kangu is openly arguing that the proposed new
Constitution does not reflect the wishes of the people. In some areas like
the clause on the Executive, the current Constitution is better than the
proposed one, he argues.

Original_Search_Title: Uhuru Threatens Journalists
Headline: Kenya: Uhuru Threatens Journalists
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Opposition Leader Uhuru Kenyatta and Cabinet minister Najib Balala
yesterday accused the media of giving a biased coverage of the No campaign
rallies.
They gave examples of their rallies in Thika and Garissa, which they
said had only brief disruptions but the press portrayed them as having been
violent.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Being Misled, Says MP
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Being Misled, Says MP
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ntonyiri MP Maoka Maore yesterday told his constituents he had no
quarrel with President Kibaki, but disagreed with Meru leaders surrounding
him.
They had misled the President into appointing ministers, permanent
secretaries, district commissioners and parastatal heads from only one of
the four Meru districts.

Original_Search_Title: Nyachae to Head 43-Member Banana Referendum Team
Headline: Kenya: Nyachae to Head 43-Member Banana Referendum Team
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The 'Yes' campaign team has forwarded the list of its National
Referendum Committee to the Electoral Commission of Kenya for
registration.
Energy minister Simeon Nyachae chairs the 43-member committee, with
Chief Whip Norman Nyagah as secretary.

Original_Search_Title: Kenyans' Dim View of State's War On Graft
Headline: Kenyans' Dim View of State's War On Graft
Body_Text:
Nairobi — More than half of Kenyans think the Government is doing a poor job of
fighting corruption, - according to a new survey.
Transparency International's Kenya chapter says 31 per cent of Kenyans
rate the Government as "very good or good" in handling financial
scandals.

Original_Search_Title: State Out to Rig Poll, Claims Orange Campaigners
Headline: Kenya: State Out to Rig Poll, Claims Orange Campaigners
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange (No) campaign yesterday denied it plotted to topple President
Kibaki and accused the Government of planning to rig the November 21
referendum.
Roads minister Raila Odinga stormed his Cabinet colleague Simeon
Nyachae's backyard to front the Orange campaign and denied the team planned
to overthrow President Kibaki's Government.

Original_Search_Title: Minister Tells Constituents to Seek President's Successor
Headline: Kenya: Minister Tells Constituents to Seek President's Successor
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Residents of Othaya Constituency in Nyeri District should start shopping
for President Kibaki's successor in the 2007 General Election.
Assistant minister Mirugi says if the next General Election is held
under the new constitution, President Kibaki will not contest the Othaya
seat.

Original_Search_Title: Poll Won't Be Stopped, Declares Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Poll Won't Be Stopped, Declares Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There will be no negotiations on the Proposed New Constitution,
President Kibaki declared yesterday.
And the President said the referendum on the proposed constitution,
which is set for November 21, would not be postponed.

Original_Search_Title: Awori Leads Ministers On Yes Drive in Makueni
Headline: Kenya: Awori Leads Ministers On Yes Drive in Makueni
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Vice-President Moody Awori yesterday led five Cabinet ministers on a Yes
campaign in Makueni District.
He and Mr John Michuki (Internal Security), Mr Joseph Munyao (Livestock)
as well as Mr Njenga Karume (Special Programmes) arrived at Wote in an Air
Force helicopter at 1.30pm, bringing the otherwise sleepy town to life.

Original_Search_Title: Too Many Lies in the Review Debate
Headline: Kenya: Too Many Lies in the Review Debate
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In the raging constitutional debate, we must make some things clear. Constitutions are written to free, not to enslave. There are, however, some slaves that are impossible to free.
They block their ears not to hear the call of freedom. When freed, some put their chains right back on from fear of the unknown, or because they believe they cannot feed themselves.

Original_Search_Title: Most Voters Will Support Candidates Who Bribe Them, Says Report
Headline: Kenya: Most Voters Will Support Candidates Who Bribe Them, Says Report
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Most Kenyans will vote for a person who bribes them in an election, a new report says.
The Transparency International Kenya chapter report says urban dwellers are more likely to vote for a candidate who gives a bribe than their rural counterparts.

Original_Search_Title: Attempts to Disrupt Meeting Thwarted
Headline: Kenya: Attempts to Disrupt Meeting Thwarted
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The No camp yesterday held a rally in Garissa amid tight security as police thwarted an attempt by Yes supporters to disrupt the meeting.
Fracas broke out moments after the No brigade of 18 MPs set foot in Baraza Park and lasted for about half an hour.

Original_Search_Title: Let's Save Our Country
Headline: Kenya: Let's Save Our Country
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There are many ways to make a new Constitution for a country. However, the bloody and ugly scenes of violence we witnessed on Wednesday in Thika and yesterday's skirmishes in Garissa are certainly not some of them.
There are many ways to debate the case for the "Yes" and "No" vote in the November referendum. But the abuses and tribal incitement that the two sides are hurling at each other are definitely not some of them.

Original_Search_Title: We'll Use State Resources to Win Vote, Ministers Vow
Headline: Kenya: We'll Use State Resources to Win Vote, Ministers Vow
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government announced yesterday that it would mobilise all its
resources to win the November 21 referendum on the new constitution.
Justice Minister Kiraitu Murungi, speaking within a background of
mounting concerns over the use of State machinery and resources in the
ongoing campaigns, said the Government would use all its resources to carry
the day.

Original_Search_Title: Garissa Erupts As 'Yes' Ministers Talk of Coup
Headline: Kenya: Garissa Erupts As 'Yes' Ministers Talk of Coup
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Violence rocked a star-studded Orange rally for the second day running
yesterday as a 'Yes' minister claimed the 'No' campaign was a plot to seize
power "through the backdoor."
Garissa town, where last week violence broke out as five 'Yes' (Banana)
ministers were ejected, was yesterday yet again the seat of running
battles.

Original_Search_Title: Youth Torn Over 'Yes', 'No' Sides
Headline: Kenya: Youth Torn Over 'Yes', 'No' Sides
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Narc Youth Congress leaders yesterday clashed at a meeting convened to
agree on whether to support 'Yes' or 'No' campaigns on the referendum.
Some youth supporting the 'Yes' team stormed out of the meeting after
differing with supporters of the 'No' camp.

Original_Search_Title: Banana, Orange Campaign Teams Head for Phase Two
Headline: Kenya: Banana, Orange Campaign Teams Head for Phase Two
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange team will next week launch Phase Two of its campaigns with
top politicians assigned specific provinces in the scramble for the 11.7
million votes to be cast at the November 21 referendum.
The team, campaigning for the 'No' vote at the referendum, will end
Phase One of the campaign this Sunday in Kisumu.

Original_Search_Title: Bloodshed After 2006 is Avoidable
Headline: Uganda: Bloodshed After 2006 is Avoidable
Body_Text:
Mzee Boniface Byanyima's claim that Ugandans should either let President
Yoweri Museveni rule for as long as he likes or fight to kick him out is not
correct.
But we can allow it to become a self-fulfilling tragedy.

Original_Search_Title: General Election Rivals Back in the Ring
Headline: Kenya: General Election Rivals Back in the Ring
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The campaign for Yes and No in the three Kisii districts ahead of
referendum is a match between the 2002 General Election winners and
losers.
It is apparent that former area MPs, who lost in the elections, will be
using the November 21 referendum to test the waters for a comeback. They
have teamed up with upcoming politicians interested in the 2007
elections.

Original_Search_Title: MPs in New Push to Have Referendum Put Off
Headline: Kenya: MPs in New Push to Have Referendum Put Off
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A group of MPs yesterday launched a new initiative to have the November
21 public vote postponed.
They want Parliament to amend the Constitution of Kenya Review
(Amendment) Act to provide for the suspension, arguing that the countdown
threatened to tear the country apart.

Original_Search_Title: Supporters, Opponents of New Constitution Clash
Headline: Kenya: Day of Chaos
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Thika was yesterday the scene of a bloody clash when 'Orange' supporters fought it out with a group that tried to disrupt the first 'No' campaign rally in Central Province.
Wielding hoe-length sticks and with chairs and oranges turned into weapons, 'Yes' and 'No' supporters square up on the pitch of the Thika Stadium yesterday before calm was restored.

Original_Search_Title: MPs in Bid to Stop Referendum Poll
Headline: Kenya: MPs in Bid to Stop Referendum Poll
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Members of Parliament opposed to the referendum have embarked on raising enough signatures to petition for the postponement of the November 21 poll.
They plan to bring an amendment motion before Parliament when it reopens next Tuesday to have the poll put off arguing that the country was too polarised.

Original_Search_Title: The Postmortem of Muhwezi's "Censure"
Headline: Uganda: The Postmortem of Muhwezi's "Censure"
Body_Text:
Parliament, as expected, failed to find the moral sense to sign a motion
to censure Health Minister Jim Muhwezi.
Of all ineffectual tools rendered redundant by Uganda's return to
multi-party rule in 2006, censure ranks high.

Original_Search_Title: 13 Ministers Meet As Yes And No Teams Open Offices
Headline: Kenya: 13 Ministers Meet As Yes And No Teams Open Offices
Body_Text:
