Nairobi — Thirteen Cabinet ministers allied to the Yes camp held a strategy meeting in Nairobi yesterday as the two opposing camps in the referendum debate unveiled their campaign secretariats.
The ministers  met under Vice-President Moody Awori and emerged from the private consultations to announce the second phase of their rallies will start in Ukambani on Friday.

Original_Search_Title: Why MPs Were Rejected in North-Eastern
Headline: Kenya: Why MPs Were Rejected in North-Eastern
Body_Text:
Nairobi — North-Eastern Province is very nearly the poorest and certainly the least densely populated province in Kenya. The inhabitants live on the edge of poverty, for they are too poor to make their voices heard.
They are often not properly represented, and therefore, they cannot convey their needs. They do not have the power to voice their opinions where major decisions are made about the future - their own future.

Original_Search_Title: Zoning Coast is Foolhardy, Says Politician
Headline: Kenya: Zoning Coast is Foolhardy, Says Politician
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Proponents of the 'No' vote at the Coast yesterday told off rivals who
allegedly issued decrees banning them from campaigning.
The remarks came as the 'Yes' planned a rally at Changamwe Sports
Grounds on September 24.

Original_Search_Title: Religious Leaders Say Vote Should Be Put Off
Headline: Kenya: Religious Leaders Say Vote Should Be Put Off
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Religious leaders in Coast province yesterday asked President Kibaki to
postpone the referendum.
The Islamic and Christian leaders asked Kibaki to call a meeting between
the Orange and Banana campaigners to iron out contentious issues in the Wako
Bill.

Original_Search_Title: 'Yes' And 'No' Teams Open Secretariats in Nairobi
Headline: Kenya: 'Yes' And 'No' Teams Open Secretariats in Nairobi
Body_Text:
Nairobi — While the leading lights of the No campaign turned out in force to witness the launch of their secretariat yesterday, their Yes counterparts skipped a similar event at their secretariat.
Cabinet ministers Raila Odinga and Anyang' Nyong'o turned up at the Orange (No) team's secretariat in Upper Hill as did several assistant ministers and MPs from the Liberal Democratic Party and Kanu.

Original_Search_Title: Keep Off Moi, Raila Tells Banana Proponents
Headline: Kenya: Keep Off Moi, Raila Tells Banana Proponents
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Roads minister Raila Odinga yesterday told 'Yes' proponents to keep off retired President Moi's name in their campaigns.
Raila said leaders should address the contents of the Wako Bill compared to the current constitution and the Bomas Draft and not to drag the name of Moi in the campaigns.

Original_Search_Title: Domestic Waste : Civic Education Imperative
Headline: Cameroon: Domestic Waste : Civic Education Imperative
Body_Text:
Filthy state of most streets in the nation's capital is mainly due to irresponsibility.
Many people have cultivated the habit of dumping waste material like pilings of bananas, ground-nuts, wrappings, etc along the streets. This explains why most of the streets in Yaounde appear untidy. Also, the problem of poor road network facilities adds to the inhygienic nature of some neighbourhoods.

Original_Search_Title: How Kivuitu Could Crack the Whip On Campaigners
Headline: Kenya: How Kivuitu Could Crack the Whip On Campaigners
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Electoral Commission has the powers to disqualify either the Yes or No camps from the referendum if it invokes the elections Code of Conduct which provide for penalties against violence.
Under the law, ECK chairman Samuel Kivuitu can complain to the High Court about a camp that has engaged in violence or intimidation of voters and seek an order to disqualify the camp from taking part in the November vote.

Original_Search_Title: Yes Team Tells Moi Not to Be Worried
Headline: Kenya: Yes Team Tells Moi Not to Be Worried
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Former President Daniel Moi and Kanu are opposed to the new Constitution because it will pave way for investigations into past crimes against Kenyans, a Yes rally was told in Muranga yesterday.
But speakers at the meeting, hosted by National Security minister John Michuki, said Mr Moi need not be worried because the proposed law provided for forgiveness.

Original_Search_Title: Ain't No Stopping the Slugfest Now
Headline: Kenya: Ain't No Stopping the Slugfest Now
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There's this song we really used to boogie to in our younger, wilder days. It was by a duo called, if this increasingly foggy memory serves me right, McFadden and Whitehead.
The refrain that got everybody on their feet went something like this: Ain't no stopping us now/we're on the move.

Original_Search_Title: Demand for Oranges Up As Campaign Hits Town
Headline: Kenya: Demand for Oranges Up As Campaign Hits Town
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Orange sellers in Migori District recorded booming business on Sunday during a rally to reject the proposed new Constitution.
Traders in Rongo, Awendo and Migori towns reported good business when LDP and Kanu members bought at least one orange each before entering the Migori municipal stadium where the rally was held.

Original_Search_Title: Reconstructing Mungo Bridge Or Bridging The Cameroons?
Headline: Cameroon: Reconstructing Mungo Bridge Or Bridging The Cameroons?
Body_Text:
Premier of the Republic of Cameroon, Chief Ephraim Inoni, Friday, September 16, laid a foundation stone for the construction of a new bridge over the Mungo. After the Mungo Bridge collapsed last year, and upon inauguration of the makeshift floating bridge, the then Prime Minister, Peter Mafany Musonge, announced that within one year, construction of a new bridge would begin.
Exactly one year after, the regime has kept its promise - though many people believe that laying the foundation stone is mere throwing of dust in the eyes of the people during this countdown to October 1, to blindfold them against any activities by Southern Cameroons liberation movements.

Original_Search_Title: Raila Says 'No' Will Win As VP Goes to Banana
Headline: Kenya: Raila Says 'No' Will Win As VP Goes to Banana
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Roads minister Raila Odinga yesterday told the 'Yes' camp to prepare for
defeat as Vice President Moody Awori led four Cabinet ministers and 16 MPs
to a symbolic rally in Banana Hill, Kiambu.
The VP hit the campaign trail for the second time a day after a banana
(symbol for Yes) rally in Garissa turned violent, forcing police to lob
tear-gas canisters and fire in the air to disperse an unruly crowd.

Original_Search_Title: Minister's Car Pelted With Oranges During a 'Yes' Rally
Headline: Kenya: Minister's Car Pelted With Oranges During a 'Yes' Rally
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Rowdy youths in Bureti District yesterday pelted the vehicle of a
Cabinet minister with oranges as he tried to campaign for a Yes vote on the
proposed constitution.
Police officers were forced to draw guns as the crowd heckled John
Koech, of East African and Regional Co-operation, at Litein trading
centre.

Original_Search_Title: I Won't Stop the Referendum, Declares Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: I Won't Stop the Referendum, Declares Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki has ruled out suspending the planned referendum on a new Constitution as three ministers urged him to call an urgent Cabinet meeting to discuss the rising political tension in the country.
The President said the national referendum scheduled for November 21 will go on as planned, and it was up to Kenyans to pass or reject the proposed new Constitution.

Original_Search_Title: Stop Campaign Violence, Say VP And Raila
Headline: Kenya: Stop Campaign Violence, Say VP And Raila
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Vice President Moody Awori and Roads Minister Raila Odinga yesterday called for an end to violence that marked referendum campaigns at the weekend.
Mr Awori spoke when he led the Yes team campaigns in Banana Hill, Kiambu while Mr Odinga spoke at Isebania and Migori in Nyanza.

Original_Search_Title: Fortportal's Vintage Exhibition Brings the Past Into the Present
Headline: Uganda: Fortportal's Vintage Exhibition Brings the Past Into the Present
Body_Text:
In a move to keep their cultural values alive and known to the young generation, the people of Tooro kingdom organised a cultural exhibition that showcased items used in the kingdom from the 18th century to date.
Among the oldest items displayed were hoes made out of stone. The head of this three-centurion hoe is shaped like your modern hoe but with a long pointed base that would be tied on a seven figure -shaped stick using sisal, in order to cultivate. The first impression is that this hoe was probably meant to cultivate during rainy seasons when the ground was soft and easy to dig.

Original_Search_Title: Orange Team to Withdraw Anti-Referendum Lawsuit
Headline: Kenya: Orange Team to Withdraw Anti-Referendum Lawsuit
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kanu Secretary-General William Ruto says Kanu and LDP are seriously
considering withdrawing a court case they, in conjunction with the so-called
Yellow Movement, had filed last month seeking to call off the
referendum.
The reason Mr Ruto gives for what they are contemplating is ostensibly
about bias. "Looking at the way the case was handled, it became obvious
there would not be any fairness. How could the judges argue about [James]
Orengo for three days and only allow only two hours for final submissions in
the main case?" (The CKRC had demanded Mr Orengo be disqualified from the
plaintiffs' legal team on the grounds that he had a conflict of interest for
having previously acted for the CKRC).

Original_Search_Title: The Lies, Noise And Political Deals
Headline: Kenya: The Lies, Noise And Political Deals
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Our politicians, it would appear, are determined not to rest until there
is blood on the streets. I am shocked that we are applauding people who are
lying, inciting, insulting and generally wasting time - ours and theirs -
and taking the country right to the brink of violence.
There is a group of hecklers in this country who love nothing better
than to dress up in some uniform or other and perform to crowds, bask in
momentary mob approval. We came out of the MoU distraction, slap into the
face of contentious issues, now it is the Kilifi Draft. After than we shall
probably move back to contentious issues - this time in the constitution,
which will involve rejecting the document - before moving back to the MoU
and finally the election.

Original_Search_Title: Garissa Yes Rally Ends in Chaos
Headline: Kenya: Garissa Yes Rally Ends in Chaos
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A high-powered team from the Yes campaign was yesterday forced to flee
Garissa town where bloody chaos forced the cancellation of a scheduled
rally.
The Cabinet Ministers and Members of Parliament who were caught up in
the violence were whisked back to the local airstrip, where they had landed
only hours earlier, under a heavy police escort.

Original_Search_Title: Missing Matiba factor in the referendum
Headline: Kenya: Missing Matiba factor in the referendum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — When politician Kenneth Matiba appeared at his Marlborough House, the headquarters of the Saba Saba Asili party in Nairobi's Westland's suburbs, his loyal lieutenants were, as usual, on tow.
For a week, former Nakuru town legislator, Francis Wanyange was herding together the remnants of Matiba's former Ford Asili, who now coil around Saba Saba Asili, as the party struggles to remain relevant as the country heads to a historic referendum.

Original_Search_Title: Rock Blast Leaves 600 Homeless in Rombo
Headline: Tanzania: Rock Blast Leaves 600 Homeless in Rombo
Body_Text:
Arusha — More that 60 families, consisting of about 600 people, in the Mwetamburu
ward of Tarakea division in Rombo district of Kilimanjaro have been rendered
homeless after a powerful explosion reduced their houses into debris early
last week.
The blast was a result of the on-going construction of the
Kamwanga-Rongai road project being implemented in the area.

Original_Search_Title: VP Leads 8 Eight Ministers in Yes Campaign Blitz
Headline: Kenya: VP Leads 8 Eight Ministers in Yes Campaign Blitz
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Vice President Moody Awori yesterday led eight Cabinet ministers in a
display of might at a campaign rally for the proposed constitution in
Kajiado District.
Also on the campaign blitz, mirroring the face of national elections
because of busing in of supporters and the use of posters and fliers, were
eight assistant ministers and six Members of Parliament.

Original_Search_Title: Commission Vows to Punish Errant Politicians
Headline: Kenya: Commission Vows to Punish Errant Politicians
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Electoral Commission of Kenya would bar politicians who incite
people from addressing gatherings.
ECK chairman Samuel Kivuitu warned that politicians who utter words
loaded with inflammatory or tribal sentiments would be dealt with.

Original_Search_Title: GTDP Unveils Urgent Valley Needs
Headline: Zambia: GTDP Unveils Urgent Valley Needs
Body_Text:
THE construction of the Kariba dam in the late 1950s led to the displacement of about 57,000 Gwembe Tonga people from the valley of the Zambezi River to the shores of the lake.
During this period, the valley inhabitants were forcibly moved and resettled in areas where the size of the land could not adequately support them and their livestock due to lack of designed resettlement plan.

Original_Search_Title: Africa, Heed Call for Food Security
Headline: Uganda: Africa, Heed Call for Food Security
Body_Text:
Kampala — AS world leaders meet at the United Nations Summit to assess the progress so far made on the Millennium Development Goals (MDG), Africa is facing unprecedented levels of food shortage and disease.
The North African State of Niger has recently been in the news having experienced the worst food shortages the continent has ever suffered. Recently, Mozambique reported severe shortage of food and soaring grain prices.

Original_Search_Title: VP, Raila Snub Call to Stop Rallies
Headline: Kenya: VP, Raila Snub Call to Stop Rallies
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Vice President Moody Awori will today lead over 10 ministers in a
campaign for the proposed constitution despite a plea by the Electoral
Commission of Kenya that the rallies be suspended.
ECK chairman Samuel Kivuitu on Wednesday asked the protagonists in the
Yes (Banana) and No (Orange) campaigns to put a halt to their campaigns
until the civic education exercise was completed.

Original_Search_Title: Kanu/LDP Leaders Plot Counter Strategy
Headline: Kenya: Kanu/LDP Leaders Plot Counter Strategy
Body_Text:
Nairobi — LDP and Kanu MPs yesterday held closed-door meetings in Nairobi to
strategise on how to counter a major onslaught by the "Yes" team that begins
tomorrow in Kajiado.
And Kanu chairman Uhuru Kenyatta declared that the "No" team was ready
for a showdown with the Wako Bill supporters led by Vice-President Moody
Awori.

Original_Search_Title: Civic Booklet With Orange Tint Rejected Civic
Headline: Kenya: Civic Booklet With Orange Tint Rejected Civic
Body_Text:
Nairobi — At least 2,000 copies of a 45-page booklet being used in civic education
have been recalled after they were printed in the wrong colour.
The Constitution of Kenya Review Commission (CKRC) told Colour Print
Limited to print a fresh run of the booklet because it had an orange cover
yet the commission wanted it in white.

Original_Search_Title: Threat of Government Quit Motion
Headline: Kenya: Threat of Government Quit Motion
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kanu and the Liberal Democratic Party will move a motion in Parliament
to compel President Kibaki's Government to resign if the proposed new
constitution is rejected at the referendum.
LDP Secretary for Constitutional and Legal Affairs Otieno Kajwang' and a
Kanu Vice-Chairman Chris Okemo further claimed that the orange vote would
triumph.

Original_Search_Title: We Shall Fight It Out With 'No' Team, Says Minister
Headline: Kenya: We Shall Fight It Out With 'No' Team, Says Minister
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A Cabinet minister in the banana campaign team yesterday ruled out
reaching a consensus with their orange camp counterparts.
Mr Njeru Ndwiga, of Co-operative Development, said his faction was all
set to fight it out with the Wako draft opponents.

Original_Search_Title: Now Kivuitu Wants Draft Rallies Stopped
Headline: Kenya: Now Kivuitu Wants Draft Rallies Stopped
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Elections chief Samuel Kivuitu yesterday asked both sides in the battle
over the proposed new Constitution to call off their rallies, as chaos
erupted in Kisumu and Eldoret at the start of civic education.
Mr Kivuitu urged the Yes and No teams to postpone their mass meetings
until the start of the official campaign period next month, saying, "It is
for their good and for the good of our great nation."

Original_Search_Title: Condom Factory Churns Out the Latex
Headline: South Africa: It All Starts With a Glass Dildo
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — Condoms everywhere, the stink of latex ... thousands of them rolling off the machines like sausages.
From its nondescript factory in Turffontein, south of Johannesburg, Latex Surgical Products makes about 200 000 condoms every day.

Original_Search_Title: Yes Ministers Hold Strategy Talks As No Team Hits Streets
Headline: Kenya: Yes Ministers Hold Strategy Talks As No Team Hits Streets
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Key ministers in the group supporting the proposed new Constitution
yesterday went to a top level secret strategy meeting in Nairobi, as more
than 50 MPs in the No group took their campaign to the capital.
Vice-President Moody Awori chaired the Yes group strategy meeting with
10 ministers, after which he announced his team will kick off their campaign
in Kajiado Town on Friday, before going to Garissa on Saturday and then
Banana Hill in Kiambaa constituency on Sunday.

Original_Search_Title: Remembering the Genocide in Rwanda 11 Years Later...
Headline: Kenya: Remembering the Genocide in Rwanda 11 Years Later...
Body_Text:
Nairobi —  Recently, the Kenya National Commission on Human Rights took Kenyan
MPs and journalists to Rwanda to experience first hand what irresponsible
statements can lead to. MAINA KIAI recounts their experience
Eleven years ago in Rwanda, Africa witnessed the climax of a systematic
campaign of hate, division and negative ethnicity with the genocide that
killed about one million people in 100 days.

Original_Search_Title: Constitution: VP Leads Ministers in Strategy Meeting
Headline: Kenya: Constitution: VP Leads Ministers in Strategy Meeting
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Vice President Moody Awori yesterday met 11 Cabinet ministers to
strategise on how to roll back the gains made by the "No" vote campaign
blitz by five 'rebel' ministers at the Coast last week.
The meeting, coming after the high-profiled rallies in the Coast
conducted by five ministers who included Mr Raila Odinga (Roads), ended with
the release of a programme of nationwide rallies to campaign for the
Proposed New Constitution.

Original_Search_Title: Kenyans Don't Deserve This
Headline: Kenyans Don't Deserve This
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The debate on the merits and demerits of the Attorney General's draft
Constitution seems set to continue with the announcement of the date of the
referendum.
The referendum process itself has raised a set of complicated concerns,
primary among them being what we will be expected to vote on. It has been
clear from the close of the Bomas process that the so-called "contentious
issues" are limited in number. The groups most vociferous in their
articulation of the issues have also been limited to members of some
political parties and some Christian churches. Yet, instead of voting on the
issues, we will be expected to vote on the entire document.

Original_Search_Title: Minister Threatens to Sack Chiefs for Backing 'No' Vote
Headline: Kenya: Minister Threatens to Sack Chiefs for Backing 'No' Vote
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Members of the provincial administration sabotaging the Yes campaign will lose their jobs, a minister has said.
Finance minister David Mwiraria warned District Commissioners, District Officers, chiefs and their assistants against inciting the public to vote against the Proposed New Constitution.

Original_Search_Title: Is History Repeating Itself Here?
Headline: Kenya: Is History Repeating Itself Here?
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It was 2002 and pivotal general elections were just around the
corner. On one side were the incumbents, under a flag that everyone had
grown tired of. President Moi was retiring, but Kenyans wanted not just him,
but the entire Kanu system and machinery out of the way. And they did not
want any possibility of Moi continuing to rule by proxy. So no room for his
hand-picked protege, Uhuru Kenyatta.
Despite the advantage of incumbency, control of the State administrative
and security machinery and a massive war chest, Kanu was going down.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Take Sides As Referendum Campaign Hots Up
Headline: Kenya: MPs Take Sides As Referendum Campaign Hots Up
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The November referendum on the proposed new constitution has split MPs
down the middle with one group backing the document and the other vowing to
derail its enactment.
A Nation survey to establish the number of legislators for and against
the proposed law found that the 222 MPs including the 12 nominated ones were
split into nearly equal numbers.

Original_Search_Title: Eight MPs Claim Raila Wants President to Fail
Headline: Kenya: Eight MPs Claim Raila Wants President to Fail
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Eight MPs took their Yes campaign to Tana River District and accused
their opponents of creating bad blood between the public and the
President.
They accused Roads minister Raila Odinga and his Environment colleague
Kalonzo Musyoka of indirectly fighting President Mwai Kibaki.

Original_Search_Title: Lull Before Banana Lobbying
Headline: Kenya: Lull Before Banana Lobbying
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There is an unusual lull in Central Province before the region goes
bananas.
MPs Kalembe Ndile, Mutua Katuku and minister Joseph Munyao drumming up
support for the 'Yes' campaign.

Original_Search_Title: Misreading The Orange and Banana Narrative
Headline: Kenya: Misreading The Orange and Banana Narrative
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Given the enormous status that reading has acquired in redefining the
national identity and consciousness, it is perhaps only proper that our
interest recognises its potential subversive reification.
The constitution review has introduced a new dimension into our national
awareness. None other than the President has implored Kenyans to read the
Draft Constitution before commenting on it. It is assumed that reading
ultimately empowers one to make an informed decision.

Original_Search_Title: Orange Ups The Stakes in Nyachae's Turf
Headline: Kenya: Orange Ups The Stakes in Nyachae's Turf
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There are sharp divisions among political leaders in the Gusii community
over the referendum.
Contrary to the perception that the Nyachae-led 'Yes' campaign has an
upper hand, recent realignments cast doubt as to whether Nyachae's word will
be taken unchallenged.

Original_Search_Title: Campaign Exposes the Deep Divisions in Society
Headline: Kenya: Campaign Exposes the Deep Divisions in Society
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Banana and Orange campaign has exposed the deep divisions and
differences in Kenya's civil society.
The lobby groups, which pushed the reform agenda for decades, appear
unable to find a common ground on the referendum on the proposed new
Constitution.

Original_Search_Title: 'No' Campaign Takes Mombasa By Storm
Headline: Kenya: 'No' Campaign Takes Mombasa By Storm
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The campaign against the proposed new Constitution got a major boost
when more than 70 Kanu and LDP leaders held a public rally in Mombasa.
Roads and Public Works minister Mr Raila Odinga denied that the campaign
for or against the new Constitution was a contest between him and President
Kibaki.

Original_Search_Title: Arusha Rotarians Plan to Build Marangu Banana Market
Headline: Tanzania: Arusha Rotarians Plan to Build Marangu Banana Market
Body_Text:
Arusha — After successfully completing the Tengeru Women Vegetable market in Arumeru district, Arusha region, the Rotary club of Arusha now intends to do the same construction service to ease the daily operations being undertaken by local traders at the Marangu banana market, located in the rural Moshi district of Kilimanjaro region.
This was revealed recently by the Chairman of the Arusha Rotary club, Abbas Moledina who explained that the Marangu market whose local traders, just like in Tengeru are women, is the major supplier of bananas for the entire Northern zone comprising Arusha, Tanga, Kilimanjaro and Manyara regions. Consignments of bananas are also exported to Kenya from Marangu.

Original_Search_Title: And I Am Christine Mukulu
Headline: Uganda: And I Am Christine Mukulu
Body_Text:
Kampala — CHRISTINE Sseremba Mukulu performed with Alex Mukulu in the latter's
productions. But in Mukulu's latest productions, she is absent. Pidson
Kareire recently chatted her up at her home in Kasanga. Below are
excerpts:
Have you watched Akattambwa?

Original_Search_Title: Don't Stir Up Emotions, Kibaki Warns Ministers
Headline: Kenya: Don't Stir Up Emotions, Kibaki Warns Ministers
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki yesterday sounded a thinly veiled warning to leaders and his Cabinet critics, telling them sternly to stop whipping up emotions over the proposed Kenya Constitution.
Instead there should be sober debate over the new reform law before it is put to a national referendum on November 21 referendum.

Original_Search_Title: Travel: a Sojourn At Lake Jipe
Headline: Kenya: Travel: a Sojourn At Lake Jipe
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Once feared to be drying up, the area around this lake is today getting back its lustre through community efforts
The area around Maktau and Taveta is full of interesting war historics. Maktau is a tiny roadside village along the dusty road between Voi and Taveta.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Rebuke Kalonzo for Talking Tough At President
Headline: Kenya: MPs Rebuke Kalonzo for Talking Tough At President
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Five Kamba leaders yesterday reacted bitterly to minister Kalonzo Musyoka's utterances at a rally in Machakos.
Livestock minister Joseph Munyao, assistant ministers Mutua Katuku, Kyalo Kaindi, MP Kalembe Ndile and Nominated MP Adelina Mwau told the Environment minister to apologise for daring President Mwai Kibaki to sack him.

Original_Search_Title: These Symbols Are Heavily Loaded
Headline: Kenya: These Symbols Are Heavily Loaded
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Electoral Commission chairman Samuel Kivuitu has prejudiced the November 21 referendum by asking voters to choose between bananas and oranges. These are value-laden, culture-bound symbols that will influence voters.
Many voters who do not fully comprehend the intricacies of constitutionalism will rely on the symbols. Many who are indifferent or unexcited about the issues will wake up to the symbolism of a banana or orange. Why did Mr Kivuitu go for such loaded symbols in a contest that is purely seeking a "yes" or "no" answer?

Original_Search_Title: Referendum Battle Lines Drawn
Headline: Kenya: Referendum Battle Lines Drawn
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The battle lines in the referendum debate were drawn yesterday, with
Cabinet minister Kiraitu Murungi declaring the war was between his colleague
Raila Odinga and President Kibaki.
'Those who will vote ÃNo' will be backing Raila Odinga in 2007 whereas
those for ÃYes' will stick with Kibaki,' Kiraitu told a rally in Meru
District.

Original_Search_Title: Conspiracy Or Lobbying?
Headline: South Africa: Conspiracy Or Lobbying?
Body_Text:
When does political lobbying against an opponent become a political conspiracy? Put differently, if we accept that President Thabo Mbeki is opposed to Jacob Zuma succeeding him, is there evidence to suggest that he has improperly intervened to make sure Zuma is not the next president?
Or are we dealing here with stock-standard politics: factions jockeying for influence and control of the ruling African National Congress, which has always been a broad church, the history of which has been the management of inherent tension?

Original_Search_Title: Education And MPs: How Much is Enough
Headline: Kenya: Education And MPs: How Much is Enough
Body_Text:
Nairobi — How much education your MP should have is a question that continues to rage in the political arena. Does it really matter whether or not they are able to produce 100,000-word dissertations on an obscure facet of leadership? FRANKLIN AWORI went in search of answers from the very same parliamentarians.
Imagine yourself in the middle of a hot debate on the orange and banana campaigns. Then some smart-mouthed opponent throws in this sentence: "I beg to substantiate my considered position dialectically with a little bit of Cartesian logic, failing which I will employ regressional analysis."

Original_Search_Title: Kanu Faction Wants House to Amend Bill
Headline: Kenya: Kanu Faction Wants House to Amend Bill
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The chorus for the suspension of the referendum on the new Constitution continued yesterday, with a Kanu faction saying it should be referred back to Parliament.
The faction's position was echoed by Central Organisation of Trade Unions boss Francis Atwoli, who said that unless contentious issues were ironed out, workers would campaign for a No vote.

Original_Search_Title: Let's Talk, No Team Tells Opponents
Headline: Kenya: Let's Talk, No Team Tells Opponents
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Opponents of the proposed new constitution yesterday toned down their criticism of President Kibaki's leadership and called for a renegotiation of the document.
Speaking in Kajiado on the second day of their campaign to push for a No vote in the November 21 referendum, the leaders, mainly from Kanu and the Liberal Democratic Party, said everyone was agreed that only sections of the Constitution were bad.

Original_Search_Title: Stop Misleading Public On Abortion, Says Tuju
Headline: Kenya: Stop Misleading Public On Abortion, Says Tuju
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Some politicians have been accused of misleading the public by saying the proposed Constitution legalises abortion.
The politicians were dictating to Kenyans which way they should cast their votes instead of teaching them about the new law, Information and Communications minister Raphael Tuju said yesterday.

Original_Search_Title: We Won't Take Any More False Promises, Leaders Tell President
Headline: Kenya: We Won't Take Any More False Promises, Leaders Tell President
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kanu chairman Uhuru Kenyatta and LDP leader Raila Odinga yesterday said
Kenyans should not listen to any more promises from President Kibaki.
They said assurances that the proposed constitution would be amended
later should not be taken seriously since the Narc Government had failed to
deliver on its past promises.

Original_Search_Title: Recall MPs, Biwott's Kanu Team Tells Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Recall MPs, Biwott's Kanu Team Tells Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Former Cabinet minister Nicholas Biwott yesterday urged President Kibaki
to recall Parliament to postpone the referendum.
He warned that regardless of how the vote went, the referendum could
cost the country dearly.

Original_Search_Title: President Rules Out Possibility of Postponing November Vote
Headline: Kenya: President Rules Out Possibility of Postponing November Vote
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki has ruled out the possibility of postponing the referendum.
In a meeting with Ford Kenya MPs at State House on Monday, the President told Ford Kenya led by the chairman Musikari Kombo that it was "too late in the day" to think about postponing the national vote.

Original_Search_Title: Of Contentious Constitutions . . .
Headline: Zimbabwe: Of Contentious Constitutions . . .
Body_Text:
Harare — ON NOVEMBER 21, Kenyans hold a referendum to vote for a new constitution in the former British colony. Kenyan voters will be asked to approve or reject an amended version of a draft constitution recently passed by Kenyan legislators.
While President Mwai Kibaki is urging voters to approve the draft law, five of his cabinet ministers have launched a fierce campaign against the proposed draft which they say concentrates power on the president.

Original_Search_Title: Cabinet Rebels Wage War On Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Cabinet Rebels Wage War On Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The nationwide campaign against the Constitution Bill was launched
yesterday, with a gloves-off message to President Kibaki that he had
committed political suicide by supporting the proposed new law.
The President would pay dearly for betraying the trust Kenyans had
placed on his leadership by voting him into office, said a group of Cabinet
ministers at the heart of the No campaign.

Original_Search_Title: 50 MPs Demand Talks On Civic Education Content
Headline: Kenya: 50 MPs Demand Talks On Civic Education Content
Body_Text:
Nairobi — More than 50 MPs yesterday demanded a meeting of all political parties
to agree on the content of civic education on the Constitution Bill.
The Kanu and LDP MPs asked the Review Commission to convene the meeting
so parties could draw a programme and content for the education.

Original_Search_Title: Don't Take Sides, Poll Chief Warns the State
Headline: Kenya: Don't Take Sides, Poll Chief Warns the State
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government should not campaign for either a Yes or No vote in the
forthcoming referendum on the new Constitution otherwise it will be
fraudulent, the head of the Electoral Commission said yesterday.
If that happened, the referendum would not be seen as free and fair and
the outcome would not reflect the proper view of the majority of Kenyans,
chairman Samuel Kivuitu told the BBC.

Original_Search_Title: Give Details On Wako Bill, Says Raila
Headline: Kenya: Give Details On Wako Bill, Says Raila
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Constitution Review Commission of Kenya was yesterday asked to
distribute pamphlets explaining reasons for and against the Constitution
Bill.
"Only this way will Kenyans be able to choose either a banana republic
or an orange queen," Cabinet minister Raila Odinga said.

Original_Search_Title: Major Blow to Yes Camp As Minister Quits
Headline: Kenya: Major Blow to Yes Camp As Minister Quits
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Yes campaign suffered a serious setback yesterday as Cabinet minister Lina Jebii Kilimo defected ahead of the referendum.
And in Bungoma, scores were injured as police fought it out with a mob that attempting to disrupt an Orange campaign rally.

Original_Search_Title: Groups Petition Kibaki On Park
Headline: Kenya: Groups Petition Kibaki On Park
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Twenty-eight conservation groups have appealed to President Kibaki to
rescind his decision to hand over a national park to a community.
The move, they said, was a threat to the country's international tourism
which contributes Sh73 billion to the economy.

Original_Search_Title: Ministers, MPs in Ugly Clash Over Referendum
Headline: Kenya: Ministers, MPs in Ugly Clash Over Referendum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Referendum tempers shook Parliament yesterday as Lands Minister Amos
Kimunya claimed his Roads counterpart Raila Odinga had called him a "stupid
idiot."
There was a tense moment during the adjournment motion to discuss use of
public resources in the Yes and No campaigns when an angry Raila gave
Kimunya, who was sitting on his left, a quick sharp jab on the rib with his
elbow.

Original_Search_Title: Bhang Trade Booming On Border
Headline: Kenya: Bhang Trade Booming On Border
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Bhang traffickers on the Kenya-Uganda border are enjoying a boom - despite increased police surveillance and campaigns against drug abuse.
And the peddlers attribute it to the Government's inability to improve the economy and create more job opportunities.

Original_Search_Title: Ken Nnamani's Senate
Headline: Nigeria: Ken Nnamani's Senate
Body_Text:
Lagos — It has been said several times but needs to be emphasised once more even at the risk of repetition that several years of military rule prior to 1999 had the most negative impact on the legislature.
While the executive and the judicial arms of government remained in one form or the other, the legislature was nowhere to be found. It functions were subsumed at various times under the Supreme Military Council, Armed Forces Ruling Council or National Defence and Security Council.

Original_Search_Title: Raila Dismisses Claims of Plan to Grab Power
Headline: Kenya: Raila Dismisses Claims of Plan to Grab Power
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange group has no intentions of taking over the Government, Roads Minister Raila Odinga stated yesterday.
He dismissed claims by the Banana group that they were plotting a takeover as "a red herring."

Original_Search_Title: Divided Cabinet Skips Meetings for 3 Months
Headline: Kenya: Divided Cabinet Skips Meetings for 3 Months
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki's unity Cabinet faces a severe test after a group of influential ministers resolved to campaign for the sacking of colleagues opposed to the proposed new Constitution.
The weekend resolution in Nakuru comes in the wake of mounting speculation that the Cabinet, which has not met for the last three months, may be too polarised to work together.

Original_Search_Title: Rebel Ministers Warned of Impending Purge
Headline: Kenya: Rebel Ministers Warned of Impending Purge
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Cabinet ministers Simeon Nyachae and Njenga Karume yesterday warned of impending sacking of six rebel ministers crusading against the proposed constitution.
In a strong message targeting ministers Raila Odinga, Kalonzo Musyoka, Anyang' Nyong'o, Ochilo Ayacko, Najib Balala and William ole Ntimama, Nyachae said President Kibaki was getting impatient.

Original_Search_Title: Oops, Kiraitu Has Done It Again!
Headline: Kenya: Oops, Kiraitu Has Done It Again!
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Sometimes, it seems like the Government is determined to hand victory on a silver platter to the No side in the upcoming referendum.
Take the statement last week by Justice and Constitutional Affairs minister Kiraitu Murungi on how Government resources would be marshalled for the Yes campaign.

Original_Search_Title: Uhuru Fears Losing Land, Says Kiunjuri
Headline: Kenya: Uhuru Fears Losing Land, Says Kiunjuri
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Assistant minister for Energy Mwangi Kiunjuri yesterday claimed that
Uhuru Kenyatta was opposing the proposed new constitution because he feared
losing land.
Kiunjuri said Uhuru feared losing the land his father Mzee Jomo
Kenyatta, the first President of Kenya, acquired in various parts of the
country.

Original_Search_Title: Draft Campaigns Produce Strange Bedfellows
Headline: Kenya: Draft Campaigns Produce Strange Bedfellows
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange campaign in the South Rift has created strange
bedfellows.
Sworn political enemies have put their rivalries aside in Bomet, Bureti,
Kericho, Narok and Transmara districts to drum up support for a 'No' vote in
the referendum.

Original_Search_Title: Suspend November Vote, Now Leaders Tell Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Suspend November Vote, Now Leaders Tell Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As the Yes group prepares to launch its campaign today, calls were made
yesterday to President Kibaki to put off the November referendum and recall
Parliament to reconsider key issues in the proposals.
At the same time, the No team pushed on with its campaign, taking it to
Bondo District's Rarieda constituency, the home turf of Information minister
Raphael Tuju, who is campaigning for a Yes vote.

Original_Search_Title: Five Groups Listed As Possible Observers At Referendum
Headline: Kenya: Five Groups Listed As Possible Observers At Referendum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Five international organisations have been listed as possible observers
during the forthcoming referendum on the Proposed New constitution.
He listed the organisations as the Commonwealth, the United Nations, the
European Union, the Jimmy Carter Centre and the African Union.

Original_Search_Title: Parties Asked to Give Names for Yes Or No Teams
Headline: Kenya: Parties Asked to Give Names for Yes Or No Teams
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Political parties will form two national committees to campaign for the
Yes and the No vote in November's referendum.
At meeting yesterday, Electoral Commission of Kenya chairman Samuel
Kivuitu gave the parties until until October 15 to come up with their list
of officials to be included in the committees to partner with ECK in voter
education.

Original_Search_Title: The Killing of the Journalist
Headline: Sierra Leone: the Killing of the Journalist
Body_Text:
Freetown — It had the hallmarks of a sordid banana republic thriller: a rogue
politician, a journalist getting killed, a staunchly incurious police, and
the media in frenzy over it all.
But in this war-wracked but oddly vibrant democratic experiment, nothing
can be so simple. In the first place, the politician is a previously unknown
freshman Member of Parliament with no apparent motive to murder a
journalist; the case initially had nothing to do with politics and
everything to do with a simple tenancy agreement; and there is little to
suggest official cover-up since the government promptly, with little
pressure, appointed an inquest to look into the death of the
journalist.

Original_Search_Title: Excitement Over Banana And Orange Symbols in Referendum
Headline: Kenya: Excitement Over Banana And Orange Symbols in Referendum
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The symbols to be voted for at the national referendum, a banana and an
orange, yesterday sparked excitement in various centres across the
country.
In Nairobi, radio presenter Joash Ombega Nyambane protested to the
Electoral Commission Chairman Samuel Kivuitu the choice of banana as one of
the referendum symbols.

Original_Search_Title: Review Team On the Spot Over Civic Education
Headline: Kenya: Review Team On the Spot Over Civic Education
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Constitution of Kenya Review Commission was yesterday put on the
spot over its curriculum for civic education.
Roads minister Raila Odinga, who is leading the campaign to reject the
Proposed New Constitution, wants CKRC to tell Kenyans about the views of
both the "Yes" and "No" teams.

Original_Search_Title: Germany 2006: Nigeria Stepped On Banana Peel - Angolan FA
Headline: Nigeria/Angola: Germany 2006: Nigeria Stepped On Banana Peel - Angolan FA
Body_Text:
Lagos — Even as many soccer pundits in the country continue with their mathematical calculations that would miraculously land the Super Eagles the sole group D ticket for the World Cup next year in Germany, Angola Football Association Vice President, Miguel Mangora, has advised Nigerians to stop all such permutations and start preparation for the 2010 edition, which would be hosted in Africa for the first time.
Mangora was reflecting on the results of the matches in the group last Sunday that saw both contending teams: Panagras Negras of Angola and Super Eagles of Nigeria winning their respective matches thus pushing the qualification to the wire in the last matches in the series next month in Kigali and Abuja respectively.

Original_Search_Title: Surprise! Campaign Hasn't Started
Headline: Kenya: Surprise! Campaign Hasn't Started
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It's official. On November 21 we will be voting for either a banana or
an orange. I have already forgotten which is Yes and which is No of the
symbols unveiled yesterday by the Electoral Commission for the forthcoming
referendum.
ECK boss Samuel Kivuitu also unveiled another gem: Those canvassing for
or against the proposed new Constitution will take 30 days - from October 21
to November 19!

Original_Search_Title: State House Must Not Meddle in Public Affairs
Headline: Uganda: State House Must Not Meddle in Public Affairs
Body_Text:
Kampala — The struggle raging over the position of Assistant Commissioner (Construction Management) in the Ministry of Education and Sports could only take place in a banana republic.
The scenario vividly illustrates the difficulties facing those who are concerned with the building of institutions and procedures of government in these countries. As reported in a spotlight article in the New Vision last Thursday, the Ministry of Education "is embroiled in a recruitment scandal".

Original_Search_Title: PDP: Waiting for Self-Destruct
Headline: Nigeria: PDP: Waiting for Self-Destruct
Body_Text:
Lagos — WHEN shortly after the death of General Sani Abacha, the Peoples Democratic Party was set up, the expectation was that it would ultimately emerge the strongest party in Africa in terms of strength and the observance of due process and the rule of law which are the hallmark, of democracy.
Before PDP, the United Nigerian Congress Party (UNCP) had taken the nation by storm and gave promise of a truly national party. It was only after the charade that characterized the adoption of General Sani Abacha as the sole presidential candidate by all the parties that it dawned on Nigerians that it was a mere pawn in the hands of the military.

Original_Search_Title: Pambazuka news 219: 01 September 2005
Headline: Africa: Pambazuka news 219: 01 September 2005
Body_Text:
The Authoritative Electronic Weekly Newsletter And Platform For Social
Justice In Africa
NOTE: To view the links that are referenced below, please copy the url
and paste into your browswer.

Original_Search_Title: Condoms Lose Ground in HIV Prevention
Headline: Uganda: Politics:Condoms Lose Ground in HIV Prevention
Body_Text:
New York — Central America and Uganda this week became the latest targets for right-wing leaders to blast HIV/AIDS prevention efforts that stray from the George W. Bush administration's "abstinence-only" ideology.
One of the country's most influential faith-based organisations, Focus on the Family (FOF), charged that a U.S. not-for-profit group, Advocates for Youth, "is asking its members and supporters to contact key Ugandan health and government officials and urge them to more fully embrace condoms and other forms of birth control".

Original_Search_Title: Deceit, Witchcraft for Money in Church
Headline: Uganda: Deceit, Witchcraft for Money in Church
Body_Text:
Play: Nekolera Maali
Playwright/Director: Abbey Sewakiryanga

Original_Search_Title: Batu Asks for Protection Against Imposters
Headline: Uganda: Batu Asks for Protection Against Imposters
Body_Text:
Arua — BRITISH American Tobacco Uganda (Batu) has asked the government to grant
registered players of the leaf industry exclusive protection against
impostors.
They also want new entrants excluded from joining the trade in areas
where other legitimate tobacco companies operate.

Original_Search_Title: This EA Federation Thing is Driving Me Bananas, Frankly
Headline: East Africa: This EA Federation Thing is Driving Me Bananas, Frankly
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The East African federation project is in a shambles. The East African
common market is pie in the sky. That's what the Doubting Thomases are
saying.
And to witness the squabbles over the fine print in the Customs Union,
and now the "integration tax", and with all the stories of a broke EAC
secretariat at Arusha, it seems the smart money is on the Doubting
Thomases.

Original_Search_Title: Bukenya Wants Second Term
Headline: Uganda: Bukenya Wants Second Term
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDANS stand to lose if they do not grant President Yoweri Museveni
another term in office, the Vice-President has said.
Prof. Gilbert Bukenya, speaking at a rally in his Busiro North
constituency on Friday, said Museveni and the Movement government had
introduced progressive politics and another term would help fulfil their
mission.

Original_Search_Title: Wambiizi the Slaughterhouse is in Need of an Overhaul
Headline: Uganda: Wambiizi the Slaughterhouse is in Need of an Overhaul
Body_Text:
Although it is one of the biggest providers of pork, Wambizzi's hygienic standards leave a lot to be desired
Kkabilizzi, Mutambuzza dembe, ka Nnadduli, Yakobo, Chinaha, Akegayaza. All these refer to pork. Depending on the hang out and probably the different people who enjoy it, pork has earned itself a trail of humorous nicknames. Over the years, it has grown to be one of the most popular snacks in Kampala.

Original_Search_Title: Open-Air Prisons Concept Impress South Africa
Headline: Zambia: Open-Air Prisons Concept Impress South Africa
Body_Text:
SOUTH African High Commissioner to Zambia Masala Mziwandila has said that his country is yet to learn from Zambia's successful open-air prison concept that has been on going for a long time.
And Commissioner of prisons Jethro Mumbuwa has revealed that a maximum prison would soon be opened in Mwembeshi to help decongest prisons.

Original_Search_Title: Need a Reason?
Headline: South Africa: Need a Reason?
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — KWAZULU-Natal is an ideal investment choice for some investors because of its coastal position. However, there is so much more to the province than the obvious.
1. Good value for money compared with other coastal provinces

Original_Search_Title: Gov't Admits Cameroon Merited Corruption Champion
Headline: Cameroon: Gov't Admits Cameroon Merited Corruption Champion
Body_Text:
Contrary to its earlier stance, the government of Cameroon has admitted that the German Anti-Corruption NGO, Transparency International, TI, was right when it classified Cameroon as the most corrupt country in the world for two consecutive years. When TI declared Cameroon the world corruption champion in 2000, government went in for an all-out war to gainsay the classification.
Some bigwigs of the ruling Cameroon People's Democratic Movement, CPDM, even wrote motions of support to the government while castigating the TI boss, Peter Eigen, for being biased against Cameroon.

Original_Search_Title: Kinsmen Reject Tribunal's Ruling On Ngige
Headline: Nigeria: Kinsmen Reject Tribunal's Ruling On Ngige
Body_Text:
Lagos — Umuezisi kindred that met in Lagos have rejected the Tribunal's judgement against the embattled Governor Chris Ngige, squashing his election as the governor in the 2003 gubernatorial elections.
Umuezisi is a community in Alor Town, Idemili South Local Government area of Anambra State, where the governor hails from.

Original_Search_Title: 'Trade Imbalance Slows Down Intergration'
Headline: East Africa: 'Trade Imbalance Slows Down Intergration'
Body_Text:
Kampala — TRADE imbalance between Uganda and Kenya is one of the factors slowing down the East African integration process, a tax analyst has said.
In his presentation on the East African Customs Union titled: Doing business in East Africa at the Sheraton Kampala Hotel on August 19, Senior Manager Tax Services PricewaterhouseCoopers, Mr Osborne Wanyoike, said the trade deficit between Kenya and Uganda is $428 million.

Original_Search_Title: Mkapa to Bid Farewell to Uganda MPs
Headline: Uganda: Mkapa to Bid Farewell to Uganda MPs
Body_Text:
Kampala — TANZANIAN President Benjamin Mkapa jets into the country today to bid farewell to Ugandans as his second and last term in office expires in November this year.
Mkapa was elected president of Tanzania in 1995 and re-elected in 2000 for a second term in office. The Tanzanian Constitution does not allow presidents to serve beyond two-five-year terms.

Original_Search_Title: Iganga's Poor Sanitation And Lost Municipal Status
Headline: Uganda: Iganga's Poor Sanitation And Lost Municipal Status
Body_Text:
Former Vice President Dr. Specioza Wandira Kazibwe had hoped to turn her constituency into a municipal status in 2005 but with the way things are, that might be just a dream far from being achieved
A visit to Iganga town council will leave one amazed at what greets them; herds of cattle, garbage or flying polythene (buveera) are pretty much the first things you will see when you enter the town.

Original_Search_Title: Why Buea Prisoners Broke Free
Headline: Cameroon: Why Buea Prisoners Broke Free
Body_Text:
On Sunday, August 14, 53 prisoners escaped from the Buea Central Prison. In their efforts to arrest the fleeing inmates, the prison guards, shoe-horned by the gendarmes and police, shot and arrested eight of the escapees. One of the prisoners, Conrad Fobin, who is said to have led the operation, died a few minutes later after receiving seven bullets in his hips. Two others, Rene Agbor and Benedict Esua died several hours later following pains from bullet wounds and lack of medical attention.
Though 12 of the escapees have been re-arrested, 41 of them are still at large.

Original_Search_Title: A Taboo Called Self-Succession
Headline: Nigeria: A Taboo Called Self-Succession
Body_Text:
One must start this analysis with a question: can self-succession
advocates resist the rapidly moving armour of popular and moral resistance?
At every turn, those canvassing for the extension of President
obasanjo's tenure beyond 2007 are now fast realizing that Nigerians are far
smarter and perspicacious in seeing through their shenanigans than they
could possibly ever believe. Their quandary is heightened by the fact that
their strategy of denial is being roundly defeated every passing day. And
since they want to be foxier than the fox, a few self-serving, sycophantic
politicians are trying desperately hard to make Nigerians believe such
obnoxious agenda does not exist.

Original_Search_Title: Legacy of Poverty And Prosperity
Headline: Zimbabwe: Legacy of Poverty And Prosperity
Body_Text:
Harare — FOR thousands of years the waters of Africa's fourth largest river - the 2 650km mighty Zambezi - meandered from north-western Zambia and cascaded over numerous waterfalls and rapids and gracefully roared to the Indian Ocean off the coast of Mozambique.
But 47 years ago in December 1958, for the first time, the great thread of water encountered a barrier to its flow, just about 1 000km from the sea at Kariba Gorge.

Original_Search_Title: Music Awards Organisers Reach Compromise
Headline: Namibia: Music Awards Organisers Reach Compromise
Body_Text:
Windhoek — A DEFINITE duplicating and overlapping absurdity of two bodies organising the same annual musical event has been averted through an agreed compromise by the timely intervention of the country's only artist union.
The Sanlam-NBC Music Awards and the Namibia Music Awards, both creating opportunities for Namibian musicians to exhibit their musical talents, were planned to have taken place 10 days apart respectively on December 2 and 12 this year.

Original_Search_Title: Get Thabo's Cash And Run
Headline: Zimbabwe: Get Thabo's Cash And Run
Body_Text:
Questions, questions SO many questions For instance, why does Uncle Bob hate talking to Morgan so much that he prefers to talk to Tony who is thousands of kilometers away when the MDC leader is only a shouting distance away?
Why does the Great Leader believe a chat with Tony at 10 Downing Street (his own words) would solve Zimbabwe's problems when a homegrown solution such as the Canaan Banana-brokered talks between him and "Father of the Nation" Joshua Nkomo in the mid-1980s brought peace to the Matabeleland provinces and the Midlands?

Original_Search_Title: Chinese Food Takes Zim By Storm
Headline: Zimbabwe: Chinese Food Takes Zim By Storm
Body_Text:
Harare — FOOD is like a man or a woman - when one is smitten with love, there is
no time to think who the person is or where they come from.
Thus, love and food share similar traits.

Original_Search_Title: I Would Be Richer, Says Museveni
Headline: Uganda: I Would Be Richer, Says Museveni
Body_Text:
Kampala — President Yoweri Museveni has said he would have made more money through self-employment than working in government.
"Some people think that money is got from working in government. I'm just helping Ugandans to be in government, otherwise I would be making money from my home," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Weaning States Off Subsidies
Headline: South Africa: Weaning States Off Subsidies
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — EUROPEAN Commission draft plans to reform the decades-old "sugar regime" were finally unveiled in June, to the dismay of sugar producers from Europe's former colonies.
The commission intends to cut guaranteed prices of white sugar 39% over four years. The plans are to be considered by the European Union (EU) council of ministers in November and will become policy only upon approval by member states. If fully implemented, the proposed cut is expected to cost African, Caribbean and Pacific sugar-producing countries an estimated Ã¢,B,(B400m a year.

Original_Search_Title: Politics in Pre-Colonial Uganda
Headline: Uganda: Politics in Pre-Colonial Uganda
Body_Text:
Political Power in Pre-Colonial Buganda: Economy, Society, and
Warfare in the Nineteenth Century.
By Richard Reid and James Currey

Original_Search_Title: Anti-Poverty Scheme Fails to Start
Headline: Uganda: Anti-Poverty Scheme Fails to Start
Body_Text:
Jinja — President Yoweri Museveni's Bonna Baggaggawale (wealth for all)
programme has failed to take off in Busoga region.
Deputy Speaker of Parliament Rebecca Kadaga, who is also the Chairperson
of the Busoga Parliamentary Group, said the programme cannot take off until
Parliament passes the budget estimates for the financial year
2005/2006.

Original_Search_Title: Growing Earnings Put the Fizz Back in Coke
Headline: Kenya: Growing Earnings Put the Fizz Back in Coke
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In 2000, at a time when many businesses were finally starting to crumble after nearly a decade of economic devastation, the Coca-Cola Company turned its back on Kenya.
Though Coke, Sprite, Fanta and Krest continued to be produced and sold locally, the decision to relocate the North African Division from Nairobi to Casablanca, Morocco was a big vote of zero confidence on the prospects of the Kenyan economy.

Original_Search_Title: Dubious Boozing Record
Headline: Uganda's Dubious Boozing Record
Body_Text:
Nairobi — FROM MORNING UNTIL THE EARLY afternoon, they are shops, with sacks of beans, posho and rice on display and weighing scales on the counter. On the shelves behind the counters are packets of wheat flour, tins of Vim, Jik, toilet paper rolls and other items.
But by 5pm, the sacks are of cereals are replaced with plastic chairs and stools. Compound umbrellas are mounted on tables lining every bit of available space on shop verandahs.

Original_Search_Title: What Mwenda Said
Headline: Uganda: What Mwenda Said
Body_Text:
Kampala — Mwenda: (break, adverts)...Coming up we shall be playing the voice of
Mr. Yoweri Museveni in his attack on me and I am going to launch a counter
attack on him and he may find that I am taking residence at Nakasero,
shifting from Kololo and he may be going to take residence in Rwakitura, one
of these days.
Museveni voice: These newspapers, I am the elected leader of Uganda. I
therefore have the ultimate mandate to run their affairs. I will no longer
tolerate a newspaper which is like a vulture... I will simply close
it. Finish. End. Gasiya tu. I have been seeing this young boy, Mwenda,
writing about Rwanda, writing about Sudan, writing about UPDF, he must
stop. Completely. He is an expert on SPLA, he knows the minutes which took
place where... he must stop. And this other paper called Observer, what is
being said in the army. This is not how a country is run... Red Pepper
also. I thought those were confused young boys busy with naked
girls... These newspapers must stop or we shall stop them. If they want to
continue doing business in Uganda, they must stop interfering in security
matters of the region...

Original_Search_Title: Budget 2005: Sound Vibes From Senate
Headline: Nigeria: Budget 2005: Sound Vibes From Senate
Body_Text:
Lagos — TOUGH guy. That is how best to describe Ken Nnamani, the president of the Senate. And why not? He has just rallied his colleagues to do what all his predecessors, save one, failed to do. Not that they didn't know they should do it. It was just that their courage left them. And that, when it mattered most. But can he go far? I pray he does.
Evan Enwerem, the first senate president in the first senate of the 4th republic never thought of it. He was still angling for a large legislative swathe when he was shoved out of his seat. Then came the highly cerebral Chuba Okadigbo. He had his quiver full of legislative darts and was just settling down to fire when he stepped on the banana peeling. He wobbled, stumbled and got knocked. He did have the mind to ask this all-important question but fell to the high velocity of the executive fury. And all this while, the gods in Aso Rock were having a bazaar. They munched. They pinched. And they swallowed every available morsel that petro-dollar could throw at them.

Original_Search_Title: 120-Year-Old Woman Dies
Headline: Uganda: 120-Year-Old Woman Dies
Body_Text:
Kampala — A woman believed to have lived for 120 years has died at her home in Sisiyi sub-county, Sironko district.
Rose Zemeyi, who claimed to have been born around 1880, died on Wednesday, barely one month after she failed to walk due to old age. She had told the new vision in an earlier interview that her hips could no longer support her weight

Original_Search_Title: Besigye Addresses Gulu Rally On Phone
Headline: Uganda: Besigye Addresses Gulu Rally On Phone
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE exiled chief of the Forum for Democratic Change (FDC), retired
Col. Dr. Kizza Besigye , on Sunday addressed a vast crowd who braved the
scorching sun at Kaunda ground to witness the launch of the party FDC office
in Gulu.
He addressed them by telephone from South Africa.

Original_Search_Title: The Economy - Our Big Mistakes
Headline: Namibia: The Economy - Our Big Mistakes
Body_Text:
"The private sector has failed us. We want to create a state which is
going to promote economic growth, a state which is going to lubricate the
private sector to really kick-start growth in the economy" - Prime Minister
Nahas Angula, Insight Magazine 2005.
" Africa seriously and I daresay urgently needs rethinking and
reordering of its developmental priorities. Africans seem to have become
incapable of own initiatives. A mindset change is needed in growing and
managing the Namibian economy on a sustainable basis" - former Prime
Minister and current Speaker of the National Assembly Theo-Ben Gurirab, New
Era Newspaper, 2005.

Original_Search_Title: What's Got Into Nnamani, Udenwa? (2)
Headline: Nigeria: What's Got Into Nnamani, Udenwa? (2)
Body_Text:
OUR supposed democracy practitioners are still suffering the heavy
hangover of military rule. Sycophancy in high places, which was widely
practiced as a means of political survival during the General Babangida and
Abacha eras, is still the vogue. Public demonstration of loyalty to the
President in power even by elected state governors has become the surest
dramatisation of survival instincts.
On the other hand, governors have tended to seal off the political space
in their various states of jurisdiction in ways that were unthinkable before
the war. In other words, most of our governors have adopted the attitude of
excessive and undignifying servility towards the President in power, while
at the same time running their states as personalised fiefdoms. There is a
sycophant in every dictator and a dictator in every sycophant.

Original_Search_Title: Anatomy of a Big Blind Country
Headline: Nigeria: Anatomy of a Big Blind Country
Body_Text:
Abuja — Philipe Troussier, the French Football Coach will never have had the effrontery to tell Bukina-Faso that a job he purportedly applied for, he could no longer take it on a trumped up excuses of knee injury, through international media network.
This is because the Bukina-Faso Football Authority would never have sent a delegation to interview an European in Europe for a job that will be done in their country. Chief Obasanjo likes to drum up the rhetoric that things are getting better. But all manner of public office holders are putting lie to his insinuation.

Original_Search_Title: Buea Council Yet to Begin Demolition
Headline: Cameroon: Buea Council Yet to Begin Demolition
Body_Text:
On July 23, the Mayor of Buea Rural Council, Charles Mbella Moki, announced that the council would embark on the demolition of structures and buildings constructed near and within ravines, to prevent floods and also save lives and property.Floods that hit the municipality on July 19, causing loss to property, prompted the Council to make this decision.
The Mayor set up a commission that identified the areas that were vulnerable to floods. The commission handed its report on July 25, same day that the demolition was supposed to begin. But 10 days later, the exercise is yet to start.

Original_Search_Title: Boda Boda Man Didn't Have to Die
Headline: Uganda: Boda Boda Man Didn't Have to Die
Body_Text:
Finally Kampala City Council, which often appears impervious to criticism, this week succumbed to mob pressure and exhumed bodies in city graves in search of a boda-boda cyclist believed to have drowned in an uncovered sewer in a bad storm.
KCC got busy after unrelenting mobs of boda-boda cyclists demonstrated outside the mayor's White Hall office, barricaded city streets with stinking garbage, and fought street battles with riot police.

Original_Search_Title: Baskets Hit New York Market
Headline: Rwandan Baskets Hit NY Market
Body_Text:
Kigali — A Rwandese company, Gahaya Links has signed a business deal to supply Rwandan Baskets to USA's top store, MACY'S in NY.Speaking to this Business Times reporter in her office, Janet Kubana said that these are efforts geared towards exploiting the AGOA opportunity.
"When the AGOA market was opened, I exploited that opportunity and Rwandan baskets won the market in USA where other handicraft traders had tried including those from China, and Madagascar among others," Kubana said.

Original_Search_Title: IMF Must Hear This
Headline: Cameroon: IMF Must Hear This
Body_Text:
An IMF mission is currently in the Cameroons to find out whether the government has respected the conditionalities to reach the completion point of the Highest Indebted Poor Country, HIPC-Initiative, and benefit from a debt cancellation of FCFA 1,400 billion.
In fact, if the IMF and the other Bretton Woods institutions are serious, they would know that Cameroon cannot reach the completion point, because the government is not serious and is dishonest.

Original_Search_Title: Industrial Growth a Priority Says Chirwa
Headline: Malawi: Industrial Growth a Priority Says Chirwa
Body_Text:
Lilongwe — Minister of Industry, Science and Technology Khumbo Chirwa says
achieving industrial growth that will turn around Malawi's economy
remains its main goal.
Speaking during a press briefing on the ministry's development
framework on Thursday in Lilongwe the minister said it is the
government's intention to make Malawi gradually emerge as an industrial
nation and, through this, create wealth and jobs for the nation.
"Alleviating the poverty of the majority of the people of Malawi
remains government's overriding goal and a priority for this nation,
hence the Malawi Economic Growth Strategy (MEGS) which suggests that a
steady growth rate of at least 6 percent per annum is required to
significantly impact positively on poverty," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Obasanjo And 3rd Term Campaign
Headline: Nigeria: Obasanjo And 3rd Term Campaign
Body_Text:
Matthew Olusegun Aremu Okikiolakan Obasanjo, is a lucky man. Lucky
indeed. He had a humble background, so humble that he pointed out in one of
his publications, Not My Will, that he was the butt of jokes by childhood
friends and school mates. No one thought his legs could carry him far from
his Owu, Abeokuta homestead.
But, not only have those legs taken him to all continents of the world,
he has been on the Nigerian throne for a record three terms. First, he was
thrown in the saddle following the death of General Murtala Muhammed, on
February 13, 1976. He took full, absolute charge of Nigeria as military Head
of State until October 1, 1979.

Original_Search_Title: The Pathway to a New Liberia - the Need for Reconciliation, Unity And Peace
Headline: Liberia: The Pathway to a New Liberia - the Need for Reconciliation, Unity And Peace
Body_Text:
A speech delivered by Wilmot L. Harmon, Sr.,former Senator, Grand Bassa County, to the Liberian Community Association of Columbus, Ohio on the observance of Independence Day.
THE NATIONAL LEVEL

Original_Search_Title: Comedy And Controversy As Referendum Takes Off
Headline: Uganda: Comedy And Controversy As Referendum Takes Off
Body_Text:
Ugandans go to the polls tomorrow to decide on the political destiny of
this banana republic through a referendum that critics are having a field
day in ridiculing.
By the fall of last week, officials at the Electoral Commission were
close to pushing the panic button after a four-day wait for ballot papers
from South Africa.

Original_Search_Title: Statement By IMF Deputy Managing Director Takatoshi Kato At the Conclusion of a Visit to Mozambi
Headline: Mozambique: Statement By IMF Deputy Managing Director Takatoshi Kato At the Conclusion of a Visit to Mozambique
Body_Text:
Republic of Mozambique — Mr. Takatoshi Kato, Deputy Managing Director of the International
Monetary Fund (IMF), made the following statement today in Maputo:
"This is my first visit to Mozambique and I would like to thank the
authorities for their warm hospitality. At my meeting with President Armando
Guebuza, I congratulated him on Mozambique's impressive progress in recent
years. Real GDP growth over the past decade has averaged 8 percent - well
above its regional peers - and the external position has improved further,
against the backdrop of declining inflation. Sound macroeconomic policies
and structural reforms, supported by successive programs under the IMF's
Poverty Reduction and Growth Facility (PRGF) and substantial donor
assistance, have underpinned this strong economic performance.

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani And the Banana Peel in the Senate
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani And the Banana Peel in the Senate
Body_Text:
Abuja — FACTS OF THE MATTER: On Tuesday, July 19, the Senate president, Ken
Nnamani announced the dissolution of all the 54 committees of the
Senate.
But that was not the shocking thing to most senators as it had long been
expected. The South West senators, however, got a rude shock when most of
them found their names in committees considered "not too strategic" A
National Assembly source described this move as a well-calculated attempt to
undo all that Wabara did by surrounding himself with senators of the South
West extraction. According to the source, the influence of the South West
senators was in part attributed to the role played by Senator Tokunbo
Afikuyomi of the AD in the emergence of Wabara as Senate president.

Original_Search_Title: Essanja Welcomes Bukenya in Rakai
Headline: Uganda: Essanja Welcomes Bukenya in Rakai
Body_Text:
Kampala — RAKAI residents on Monday excited Vice-President Prof. Gilbert Bukenya when they welcomed him with branches of trees and essanja (dry banana leaves) in Kakuuto and Kooki counties.
Bukenya, who was on a three-day tour of Rakai district to mobilise people to vote for the "tree" symbol in the July 28 referendum, commended the district for supporting the Movement.

Original_Search_Title: Orphaned, Kiyingi's Law Firm Misses Its Founder
Headline: Uganda: Orphaned, Kiyingi's Law Firm Misses Its Founder
Body_Text:
When Dorothy Nandugga Kabugo, the late Robinah Kiyingi's partner, was driving to work on Tuesday July 12, the last thing she expected to hear - from a client - was that her partner had been gunned down.
Dorothy Nandugga Kabugo heard her phone ring. She ignored it. The phone rang - again. She ignored it - again.

Original_Search_Title: Pambazuka news 216: 21 July 2005
Headline: Africa: Pambazuka news 216: 21 July 2005
Body_Text:
A Weekly Electronic Forum For Social Justice In Africa
NOTE: To view the links that are referenced below, please copy the url
and paste into your browswer.

Original_Search_Title: Boycott Won't Help the Opposition Bell the Cat
Headline: Uganda: Boycott Won't Help the Opposition Bell the Cat
Body_Text:
Kampala — Norway has cut some aid to Uganda reportedly in protest against the slow pace and direction of democratic reforms in the country.
The Norwegian Ambassador, Tore Gjos, put a sum to his displeasure at "mishandling of the democratic process" at a whopping sum of $4m which is 10 per cent of the total aid to Uganda. The British government earlier in April took a similar line when it withheld $9.5 m. It will not be surprising if one or two more so-called donor countries took a similar action. But they will be merely symbolic.

Original_Search_Title: Increased Aid And Cancelling Debt Do Not Reduce Poverty
Headline: Uganda: Increased Aid And Cancelling Debt Do Not Reduce Poverty
Body_Text:
British Prime Minister, Tony Blair's twin agenda of debt cancellation
and doubling of aid may sound very humanitarian, but it may actually turn
out to be a medicine that makes the patient worse.
As seen in previous articles in these series, debt cancellation and
doubling of aid flows is not new. Uganda was given debt relief under the
Highly Indebted Poor Countries (HIPC) initiative - both original (1998) and
enhanced (2000).

Original_Search_Title: ACP Sugar Producers Concerns Legitimate
Headline: Southern Africa: ACP Sugar Producers Concerns Legitimate
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — The African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) sugar producers have legitimate
concerns over the compensatory aid being offered by the European Union (EU)
after its proposal to cut sugar prices, suggests a new research paper.
Previous experience of EU compensation when the prices of bananas, cocoa
and rum were cut "have been unpleasant, so they have reason for concern",
said Calvin Manduna, a researcher with the nonprofit Trade Law Centre for
Southern Africa, who wrote the paper.

Original_Search_Title: Turmoils of Nigeria's Statehood
Headline: Nigeria: Turmoils of Nigeria's Statehood
Body_Text:
Lagos — The recent United States National Intelligence Council report that
predicted, amongst other things, an outright collapse of Nigeria as a nation
state within the next 15 years was received with a range of responses across
the national and international communities.
The report is not an official Washington statement. This much was
evidenced in the rapid denial by the United States Consul in Nigeria by
which an emphatic detachment of the United States government's position was
emphasised. The report appears to have emerged from a conference of academic
and non-Governmental experts convened by the United States National
Intelligence Council to discuss likely trends over the next few years

Original_Search_Title: Support Referendum, Museveni Urges
Headline: Uganda: Support Referendum, Museveni Urges
Body_Text:
Kampala — The National Resistance Movement (NRM) has recruited more than 12 million supporters in the ongoing registration of party members.
President Yoweri Museveni said on Saturday that the high demand for the NRM cards was a clear testimony that most Ugandans had been "movementised".

Original_Search_Title: Understanding Our Problems
Headline: Nigeria: Understanding Our Problems
Body_Text:
Akure — On October 1, 1960, almost some forty-five years back, Nigeria, once a
British Colony, became a sovereign nation.
In January 1966, largely taking advantage of some political tensions in
the country, the Nigerian Army abandoned its traditional role of defending
Nigeria's territory and made incursion into the country's polity. And since
then, the army remained unyielding and clung firmly to the reins of
government of Nigeria until God brought Olusegun Obasanjo from the shadow of
death to glory. For these years of Nigeria's nationhood, the army has ruled
for 31 years. A look at the scoreboard of Nigeria since independence shows a
grim picture with nothing to write home about. Nigeria started exporting
crude oil since early 1970s and at that time Nigeria had almost the same GNP
as the following countries:- Singapore, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Thailand,
Malaysia. Even Taiwan and South Korea, now the two rising star countries of
East Asia were little known then.

Original_Search_Title: Rwamakuba's Defence Hits Brick Wall in Cross-Examination
Headline: Tanzania: Rwamakuba's Defence Hits Brick Wall in Cross-Examination
Body_Text:
Arusha — The defence of the former Minister of Primary and Secondary Education, Andre Rwamakuba, last Monday hit a brick wall when it began cross-examining a prosecution witness at the International Criminal Tribunal of Rwanda (ICTR).
The witness code-named HF to protect her identity, is a survivor of the 1994 genocide in Rwanda in which an estimated one million Tutsis were killed. She is the 13th witness to be called by the prosecution.

Original_Search_Title: In Praise of Arusha City
Headline: Tanzania: In Praise of Arusha City
Body_Text:
Arusha — New Safari Hotel in the heart of Arusha City
How does one get to know and remember a Tanzanian city? Is it by the sights you see - and their distinctive character - or is it something else?

Original_Search_Title: IBB's Flight of Fancy
Headline: Nigeria: IBB's Flight of Fancy
Body_Text:
Lagos — The only thing it takes for Mrs Maryam Babangida to muster the temerity, boldness and audacity to insult the collective intelligence of Nigerians once again that they will return to Aso Rock in 2007 was a statement credited to President Olusegun Obasanjo in London that allegations of corrupt enrichment and other criminal activities being levelled against IBB were unsubstantiated coffee shop rumours.
I laughed and laughed until tears started rolling down from my eyes when I read Mrs Maryam Babangida's joke of the century that Nigerians will reward them again in 2007. It never ceases to fill me with wonders how some people overrate themselves even when history does not support their claims, even when all odds are

Original_Search_Title: Nigeria's Debt And Illusion of Cancellation
Headline: Nigeria's Debt And Illusion of Cancellation
Body_Text:
The officially induced hysteria that Nigeria's excruciating debt burden
is substantially reduced by one of its principal creditor's cartel, the
Paris club, is a hoax, politically motivated delusion and a ruse.
The fact is that President Obasanjo and his new-liberal clique have
finally and successfully shoved Nigeria to the precarious orbit of global
finance capital, totally rend-ered her dependent to the vagaries of its
unaccountable operators. Several years of anti-colonial struggle and
subsequent ones, more than four decades in a whole to consolidate the post
colonial, independent state has in one single swoop been defeated for the
political expediency of one man and his friends. The fact that, Nigeria by
the latest arrangement in which 60per cent or $18 billion of her ext-ernal
debt has been purportedly forgiven does not worry the kinpins or rather
'queen pins' of this dastard plot.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Woos Anti-Party Group
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Woos Anti-Party Group
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni last week met the Movement Referendum
Committee to persuade them to stop campaigning against a return to
pluralism, a presidential adviser said.
The aide, who preferred anonymity, told Daily Monitor yesterday the
President was disturbed by the group's position.

Original_Search_Title: Close, But No Cigar
Headline: South Africa: Close, But No Cigar
Body_Text:
