Cigars have always conjured up a certain ineffable quality, a romance or mystique. What would Winston Churchill and Fidel Castro have been sans cigars?
But when is a cigar not a cigar?

Original_Search_Title: Uganda Could Shun EU for Regional Markets
Headline: Uganda Could Shun EU for Regional Markets
Body_Text:
Entebbe — UGANDA may have missed out on its quota to supply the EU with sugar
under the EU-ACP sugar protocol, but instead, the country is being urged to
look to regional markets.
"Why should we be preparing only for the EU market? EBA [Everything But
Arms market access initiative] is unilateral and can be removed. What will
countries depending on the EU do then? We do not have to export to the EU as
one market. Markets around us could even be better. Look at the Eastern DRC,
Southern Sudan, North Western Kenya, Northern Tanzania and Rwanda," said
Mr. Cyprian Batala, Assistant Commissioner for Trade, Ministry of Tourism,
Trade and Industry.

Original_Search_Title: Bigo Bya Mugenyi, the Abode of Chwezi Gods
Headline: Uganda: Bigo Bya Mugenyi, the Abode of Chwezi Gods
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Chwezi, also called Bachwezi, were said to be demigods. They are believed to have occupied Bigo bya Mugenyi and other parts of central and western Uganda between the 11th and 16th centuries. Their identity is an enigma. Nobody knows why they disappeared or where they went but their presumed places of abode are revered
Bigo bya Mugenyi is a deserted savannah wilderness. Its air smells of a peculiar mixture of cow dung, raw clay and the strong fragrance of wild flowers and shrubs. Crickets and numerous bird species, resting in the woods, chat amicably as if in rebellion against tranquillity.

Original_Search_Title: Littoral MPs Invite the Prime Minister
Headline: Cameroon: Littoral MPs Invite the Prime Minister
Body_Text:
PM Ephraim Inoni yesterday granted an audience to Members of Parliament of the Littoral Province.
The 18 Members of Parliament from the Littoral Province yesterday, July 7 expressed their congratulations, encouragement and support to the Prime Minister, Ephraim Inoni in the execution of his daunting task to ensure economic growth and improvement in the living standards of Cameroonians. This was during an audience the Prime Minister granted the MPs. They were accompanied to the Star Building by some members of government from the Littoral Province.

Original_Search_Title: Will Cameroonians Accept Another Fuel Price Increase?
Headline: Cameroon: Will Cameroonians Accept Another Fuel Price Increase?
Body_Text:
The government last weekend increased the price of fuel, petrol, by FCFA 16. The FCFA 16 raise is the highest in recent times. The new price took effect all over the national territory on Monday, July 4.
The fuel price increase is the second this year. About five months ago the government raised fuel prices by FCFA 5.

Original_Search_Title: 'My Target is to Promote Indigenous Shipping'
Headline: Nigeria: 'My Target is to Promote Indigenous Shipping'
Body_Text:
Lagos — Director-General, National Maritime Authority (NMA), Engr. Festus
Ugwu, recently visited the headquarters of THISDAY Newspapers, during which
he entertained questions from some editors on various issues affecting the
maritime industry. Excerpts
What is the NMA doing currently to develop shipping in Nigeria?

Original_Search_Title: Where Are the Previous Winners?
Headline: Uganda: Where Are the Previous Winners?
Body_Text:
Nigeria's Oluchi was the first M-Net Face of Africa winner in 1998. She was only 17 then and got a three-year modelling contract with Elite Models Management, which sent her to the top of the fashion industry. She quickly became one of the most sought after models and has lived up to her promise that she would follow the footsteps of Iman and Alek.
She has graced the covers of Italian Vogue, I-D, Elle, Untold, Surface and also featured in Nylon, Marie Claire, Allure, and Vogue in a variety of different languages. Oluchi also became the face of the campaigns for Gianfranco Ferre, Victoria's Secret, Gap, Express, Banana Republic, and Ann Taylor. She has worked with designers like John Galliano, Christian Dior, Costume National, Chanel and Giorgio Armani. Oluchi is pursuing an associate's degree and plans to continue her studying afterwards.

Original_Search_Title: Carjack Alarm Lands Student in Police Cell
Headline: Kenya: Carjack Alarm Lands Student in Police Cell
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A 20-year-old schoolboy has been arrested on claims that he gave a false
alarm that triggered a massive police operation in three provinces.
Shortly before 1pm on Monday, Allan Ouma, a Form Three student of a
school in Homa Bay district, casually walked into the Ahero police station
in Nyando district and reported that four armed gangsters had hijacked his
elder sister's car and driven away with her.

Original_Search_Title: I've Lost Confidence in EFCC, Says Balogun
Headline: Nigeria: I've Lost Confidence in EFCC, Says Balogun
Body_Text:
Abuja — Former Inspector General of Police, Mr. Tafa Balogun, yesterday wrote to the Chairman of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), Mr. Nuhu Ribadu, asking that the commission's operatives that allegedly maltreateed him last week be released to the police for prosecution. He said he has lost confidence in the commission.
Balogun on trial in two separate courts for money laundering, stealing and gratification, had last week slumped in court after alleged brutalisation by EFCC operatives.

Original_Search_Title: 'For US, It's Been 25 Years of Celebration'
Headline: Nigeria: 'For US, It's Been 25 Years of Celebration'
Body_Text:
Lagos — It was a moment of celebration for the family of Bishop Mike and Rev. Peace Okonkwo as well as the entire The Redeemed Evangelical Mission (TREM) when both celebrated the 25th anniversary of their wedlock recently. And inline with their modest lifestyle, the event was modest and lively. For some of the guests, it was something to celebrate even when statistics show that many marriages collapse few years after their consummation.
Therefore, how have the Okonkwos managed to keep their marriage for the last 25 years, when even clerics' marriages are crashing frequently? To talk about the marriage, Rev. Peace Okonkwo elected to speak on what it means to live with Bishop Mike for past 25 years a couple. For her, it means so much in different areas depending on what area one looks at it. In the ministry, it has been "quite interesting having lived with him, because he will always make sure that you do all you are expected to do because you see him doing it-- praying, reading the word and all that. At the home front, the bishop is not a difficult person to live with.

Original_Search_Title: Districts for Rats, a President for Life - That's Our New Politics
Headline: Uganda: Districts for Rats, a President for Life - That's Our New Politics
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Last Tuesday, pro-democracy protesters took to the streets in Kampala
capital to protest an impending vote by Parliament - which is dominated by
the ruling National Resistance Movement-Organisation - to abolish term
limits and make the head of state a presidential monarch.
As the parliament voted 232 to 50 to move the constitutional amendment
to the next stage, the police were teargassing the protesters.

Original_Search_Title: Japadhola Protest New District Demands
Headline: Uganda: Japadhola Protest New District Demands
Body_Text:
Kampala — Ninety residents from Tororo municipality yesterday pitched camp at Parliament protesting new demands advanced by Tororo county MP.
The legislator, Geoffrey Ekanya, wants Tororo municipality divided into two, while curving a new district.

Original_Search_Title: Indoor Pollution a Silent Threat
Headline: Uganda: Indoor Pollution a Silent Threat
Body_Text:
In a small village of Nyakabanga in Bushenyi district, Grace Kyamaziima is carrying her baby boy on her back. It is 8a.m and she is preparing a family breakfast of millet porridge in a small grass thatched kitchen.
Outside, you can hear her cough endlessly and you can hardly see the grass that covers the structure, just white smoke oozing out.

Original_Search_Title: Economic Evolution: Slight Boom in Sight
Headline: Cameroon: Economic Evolution: Slight Boom in Sight
Body_Text:
BEAC survey earmarks slight boom in activities for the third semester.
The Cameroon economy is expected to witness two major changes by the end of the third semester this year: slight boom in activities and considerable stability in production. According to the results of the survey carried out by the Bank of Central African States (BEAC) in collaboration with business groups and enterprises, all the three major sectors of the economy, primary, secondary and tertiary are expected to experience some evolution during the third semester of 2005.

Original_Search_Title: Critical Perspectives On Dele Olojede's Pulitzer Story
Headline: Nigeria: Critical Perspectives On Dele Olojede's Pulitzer Story
Body_Text:
Lagos — It is imperative to subject Genocide's Child, Dele Ojedele's Pulitzer-winning story to metaphysics, psychoanalytic reading and a newer, pro-life criticism. Unlike The Diary of Anne Frank and The Story of Von Trapp Family that has become an epic film The Sounds of Music, Genocide Child turns out to be a curiously horrid story of an evil time. Not nuclear warheads, not chemical or biological weapons but ordinary machetes, weighing two feet long and two kilogrammes, imported en masse from the People's Republic of China were the weapons of mass destruction, which brought the Rwandan rivers of blood into being. The UN, and the self-appointed police of the world could only foot-drag over intervention. They never did. The resulting close-to-one million deaths remain a permanent skull hung on the conscience of the world.
Untypical for a report is the sleek, subtle vocabulary, breezily creative. Ojedele's inventive restlessness is in the lexical choices deployed like the materials of a supple prose. At times, the boldness and virtuosity of his phrases intoxicate and the cadence, a thing of beauty. But surprisingly, their effects end with the second section of the story. Genocide's Child is hence distinguished more by its content rather than the virtuosity that do not follow the story till the end.

Original_Search_Title: VP Bukenya Was Spot On
Headline: Uganda: VP Bukenya Was Spot On
Body_Text:
Vice President Gilbert Bukenya has got it right. Prof. Bukenya said during a talk show on Lira's Radio WA last week that President Yoweri Museveni was not the only credible leader who can steer Uganda.
"I don't think he is the only credible leader," Bukenya said. "There are definitely many others but he picked us from pieces. The country is changing due to his credible leadership."

Original_Search_Title: Malawi's Mobile Cross-Border Market Helps to Empower Lundazi Residents
Headline: Zambia: Malawi's Mobile Cross-Border Market Helps to Empower Lundazi Residents
Body_Text:
If you live along any of Zambia's border areas, finding a ready market for your produce is one problem you have to contend with everyday.
Whether it is in Kipushi or Kasumbalesa, Katimamulilo or Chavuma, you really need to possess or develop special bargaining skills to pull a fair price for your produce.

Original_Search_Title: Lukyamuzi Weeps
Headline: Uganda: Lukyamuzi Weeps
Body_Text:
Kampala — Lubaga South MP John Ken Lukyamuzi stunned residents in his village in
Rakai when he wept at a public rally, saying thieves traumatised his
mother.
Thieves have reportedly attacked Besi Nantenza of Lutunga in Kirumba
sub-county in Kyotera, 10 times, including last Wednesday.

Original_Search_Title: Commercial Sex Workers Bare Their Souls
Headline: Uganda: Commercial Sex Workers Bare Their Souls
Body_Text:
They're raped, beaten and abused. They'll have sex with you for just a few thousand shillings. Half of them have HIV. Ida Bergstrom chats with a few of Kampala's commercial sex workers
"Some men don't want to have sex. They just want to put a barrel or banana up them. Some just want to hear them scream. Some just want to beat them."

Original_Search_Title: Too Much Fuss Over a Spider Bite
Headline: Africa: Too Much Fuss Over a Spider Bite
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Africa, it seems, has been rediscovered. Pushed by British Prime
Minister Tony Blair, the world's rich nation's recently decided to forgive
100 per cent of the debt of Africa's poorest countries, and are set to
consider increasing aid at their summit in Scotland early next month.
Now you can't turn the papers or visit the website of a major world
newspaper without finding an article about whether the $55bn (Sh4.2
trillion) debt relief will change the desperate conditions in many parts of
Africa or, as usual, or the saving and new said will be stolen by corrupt
politicians and generals.

Original_Search_Title: DPP Using Chiefs to Entice MCP MPs
Headline: Malawi: DPP Using Chiefs to Entice MCP MPs
Body_Text:
The main opposition Malawi Congress Party (MCP) has claimed that
President Bingu wa Mutharika's Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) is
using Senior Chiefs Lukwa and Mazengela of Kasungu to woo MCP MPs
into supporting the government and DPP in exchange for a maize mill
or an equivalent amount of money to any MP who accepts the offer, The
Chronicle has been informed.
Sources in MCP allege that the DPP party is using the two Senior
Chiefs to convince MCP MPs in the district to ditch MCP and work with
DPP, a party that was formed weeks after Mutharika quit the UDF on
February 5, this year.

Original_Search_Title: UDF, MCP Impeachment Plot 'Backfires'
Headline: Malawi: UDF, MCP Impeachment Plot 'Backfires'
Body_Text:
Government MPs Thursday claimed that the motion the opposition moved
to amend the Standing Orders of Parliament to provide for a secret
ballot as a first step to impeach President Bingu wa Mutharika would
backfire because it would effectively give freedom to opposition MPs
to vote according to their conscious.
Independent MP for Balaka South West Gerald Mponda, who many believe
is working with both the UDF and MCP in the Mutharika impeachment
plot, moved the motion.

Original_Search_Title: Poor Countries Bitter About Sugar Reform
Headline: Africa: Development:Poor Countries Bitter About Sugar Reform
Body_Text:
Brussels — Sugar-producing countries from the developing world have slammed European Union proposals to reform its controversial sugar regime as too fast, too much, too soon.
The reform proposals by the European Commission, the executive arm of the European Union (EU) on Wednesday (Jun. 22) envisage cutting the guaranteed price of white sugar imported by the EU from African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) countries by 39 percent and the beet price by 42 percent between 2006 and 2008.

Original_Search_Title: Is the Kufuor Administration Driving the NPP Into Oblivion?
Headline: Ghana: Is the Kufuor Administration Driving the NPP Into Oblivion?
Body_Text:
Washington DC — As a republican (US), I'm a firm believer in the unspoken word of US
republican politics, the so-called eleventh commandment, "thou shall not
speak ill of a fellow republican". Today, I'm breaking this unspoken vow to
lambast the Kufuor administration.
NPP Credentials

Original_Search_Title: Suburbs Stink More Than the City Centre
Headline: Uganda: Suburbs Stink More Than the City Centre
Body_Text:
Dirty used plastic bags, paper debris, tree branches, banana peelings,
juice bottles, banana peelings, animal carcasses: The National Environment
Management Authority (NEMA) needs to save us from the scourge of domestic
waste disposal.
As we consume and dispose more in an unregulated environment, a lot of
energy is expended on castigating the unhygienic state of Kampala City
streets, markets and neighbourhoods.

Original_Search_Title: Al-Qaeda... is US Embassy in Nigeria a Target?
Headline: Nigeria: Al-Qaeda... is US Embassy in Nigeria a Target?
Body_Text:
Lagos — With the closure last Thursday of the Consulate of the United States Embassy in Lagos, followed the next day by the missions of Germany, Italy, Finland, Russia, Sweden, India and Lebanon, there are now palpable fears that the Al-Quaeda network of Osama Bin Laden, might have infiltrated Nigeria in its bid to wreck havoc on US interests. THISDAY gathered at the weekend that the tip-off relied upon by the US embassy was supplied by an anonymous Nigerian but the questions remain: how real and genuine was this terror scare? Or was there more to it than mere security alert?
Walter Carrington Crescent is the most popular street in Lagos when it comes to diplomatic matters. It is shaped like a loop and has only one entrance. United States, Italy, Lebanon, the Netherlands and Russia have diplomatic missions on the road. In fact, nearly all the embassies of the most influential countries in teh world are located therein and despite the busy activities noticeable in the zone, the serenity of the crescent is second to none. A sharp contrast to the peacefulness of Walter Carrington was displayed last Friday when there was an unusual security presence in the zone. Vehicles were screened with bomb detection devices by members of the Nigerian police and hundreds of gun totting security men paraded the area.

Original_Search_Title: Art And Amusements in Garden
Headline: Kenya: Art And Amusements in Garden
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There's a fairground in Nairobi's Lavington. Right at the end of Argwings Kodhek Road. And the entrance is opposite the International Montessori School.
Actually, it's been there since the beginning of the year. I've passed it so many times going to and from town. As you drive along the short kink that links Argwings Kodhek and Gitanga roads, you can't miss the Big Wheel that well outdoes the trees.

Original_Search_Title: Muckraker, Reeking Ignorance
Headline: Zimbabwe: Muckraker, Reeking Ignorance
Body_Text:
Harare — MEDIA freedom debate in Zimbabwe and elsewhere in Africa is misconstrued
to be synonymous with freedom to write falsehoods, entailing negative
reporting that is critical of the Government to the extent of ignoring even
good things the same Government may be doing.
I shall delve into that discourse in the next instalment, but for now
let me disabuse my "learned" friend Muckraker of his dangerous
misconceptions regarding the crowning moment that heralded the birth of
Zimbabwe on that historic night, April 17/18 1980 at Rufaro Stadium.

Original_Search_Title: Fighting HIV/Aids: Time for Behavioural Change in the Northwest
Headline: Cameroon: Fighting HIV/Aids: Time for Behavioural Change in the Northwest
Body_Text:
A recent health demographic survey on the high prevalence rate of HIV/AIDS by the Ministry of Public Health in Cameroon put the Northwest Province in the first position with a score of 8.7 percent. Women of this Province scored 11.9 percent more than infected males with a 5.2 percent rate.
This situation is more disturbing to health officials who have gone on an aggressive campaign for behavioural change amongst the sexually active population, given the fact that the major ways of contracting the HIV virus that results in AIDS is through unprotected sex.

Original_Search_Title: Govt Boosts Aids Fight
Headline: Uganda: Govt Boosts Aids Fight
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Ministry of health has given out 30 double-cabin pick-up trucks worth US$672,000 (about sh1.2b) to boost the fight against AIDS, TB and malaria.
Handing over the vehicles at the ministry stores in Najjanakumbi yesterday, health minister Maj. Gen. Jim Muhwezi asked the beneficiaries to put the vehicles to good use.

Original_Search_Title: Uganda to Set Up Trade Centre in Iran
Headline: Uganda to Set Up Trade Centre in Iran
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDA plans to set up a permanent trade centre in Iran to strengthen
trade ties between the two countries and increase Uganda's export
revenue.
The centre, to be officially opened at the end of this month will be
located on African Avenue in Tehran (Iran capital).

Original_Search_Title: Draft Laws On Environment Faulty
Headline: Kenya: Draft Laws On Environment Faulty
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It is difficult to imagine anything more dreamy-eyed and impractical than Chapter Eight of the Draft Bomas Constitution.
Apart from the fact that the architects of this chapter did not seem to know what belongs to a constitution and what should be regulated by less exalted law, they certainly were unfamiliar with the topic as well as with the language.

Original_Search_Title: 50 Abducted in Western Uganda
Headline: Uganda: 50 Abducted in Western Uganda
Body_Text:
Fort Portal — About 50 people were last Sunday abducted by unknown gunmen.
They were held hostage for about 8 hours, before they escaped from
captivity. The victims were abducted at around 3.00 am from villages of
Rubanga, Kakuba and Bisanja in Mwenge North County in Kyenjojo district,
police says.

Original_Search_Title: To Cut Reliance On Donors, Suruma Went for Higher Taxes
Headline: Uganda: To Cut Reliance On Donors, Suruma Went for Higher Taxes
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Uganda's Finance Minister, Dr Ezra Suruma, read his first budget speech against the background of a need to increase the proportion of the government's expenditure that is financed from domestic revenue. The theme of this year's budget was, "Promoting economic growth and household incomes through increased savings, investment, employment and productivity." The minister outlined three main purposes of the budget:
(i) To deliver economic growth, so as to rapidly increase the average standard of living;

Original_Search_Title: Cleaning House
Headline: Kenya: Cleaning House
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Scepticism greets Official Opposition Kanu's plan to undertake major changes to strengthen its position
Faced with the task of keeping an all-powerful Narc in check, Kanu is set to undergo a major re-organisation amid dipping confidence levels within and without its ranks.

Original_Search_Title: UB-40's Red-Red Wine Laced With Banana Wine Leads to Chaos
Headline: Tanzania: UB-40's Red-Red Wine Laced With Banana Wine Leads to Chaos
Body_Text:
Arusha — What happens when you mix UB 40's Red-Red Wine tune with the locally produced, Banana wine liquor? Well, all hell is bound to break loose as it did last Sunday at the Kijenge road junction during an encore music dancing feat to cap the Municipal 'Environment' campaign.
Arusha municipality was marking the peak of the 'World's environment day' on the 5th of June 2005 at the open grounds adjacent to the Kijenge roundabout, near Impala Hotel. A land reserved as public gardens.

Original_Search_Title: Miss Arusha 2005 Pageant Slated for June 24
Headline: Tanzania: Miss Arusha 2005 Pageant Slated for June 24
Body_Text:
Arusha — The Miss Arusha 2005 beauty pageant is scheduled for Friday, the 24th of June 2005. This year's event, just like Miss Arusha 2004, will be staged at the Blue Triple A night club in Sakina area. The contest is being organized by Saida Kessy.
On Saturday the 4th June 2005 at 11.00 am the aspiring local ladies out to vie for the regional Beauty Crown paid a visit to the Mount Meru Regional Hospital's Childrens' Ward and handed out foodstuffs such as milk and fruits plus some items of clothing.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Campaigns for Bukenya
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Campaigns for Bukenya
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Museveni has urged Busiro North voters to give Vice-President
Gilbert Bukenya a third term as their MP, reports Jude Etyang.
Museveni said he spotted Bukenya from Parliament and made him
vice-president.

Original_Search_Title: Mafia Gang is Real - Muntu
Headline: Uganda: Mafia Gang is Real - Muntu
Body_Text:
Mbale — Former army commander Major General Mugisha Muntu has said a well-laid
mafia gang has evolved in the Movement government.
The gang, which he said was first revealed by Vice-President
Prof. Gilbert Bukenya recently, exists.

Original_Search_Title: Premiership Gone, Any Relief for Local Soccer?
Headline: Uganda: Premiership Gone, Any Relief for Local Soccer?
Body_Text:
Kampala — The city has never been so peaceful. It's a Saturday evening as I walk
past a chain of deserted pubs and bibandas (football kiosks) along
Nakulabye-Makerere road.
Previously, on any given Saturday, deafening cheers and crowded pubs
would be heard and seen here.

Original_Search_Title: Lagos Writers Hold Confab to X-Ray Nigeria
Headline: Nigeria: Lagos Writers Hold Confab to X-Ray Nigeria
Body_Text:
Lagos — As civil rights movements and pro-democracy crusaders warm up for the
celebration of the 12th anniversary of the June 12, 1993 presidential
election - which was generally adjudged the freest and fairest election in
the history of Nigeria, creative writers in Lagos State, under the aegis of
Association of Nigerian Authors (ANA), Lagos Branch, are not left out.
The Branch will hold an extra-ordinary reading/meeting on Saturday, June
11, 2005, to critically analyse the socio-political and other problems,
which have reduced our beloved country - one of the most naturally endowed
in the world, to a banana republic. The Guest Speaker at the special event
is Comrade Femi Aborisade, General Secretary of National Conscience Party,
NCP, who will speak on the theme: THE PLACE OF THE WRITER IN AN EMERGENT
DEMOCRACY.

Original_Search_Title: Buganda Has Little to Thank the Movement for
Headline: Uganda: Buganda Has Little to Thank the Movement for
Body_Text:
I propose to be candid here, in order to put straight matters in which
the perpetrators of falsehoods have repeated them so often that they
themselves now seemingly believe them. "Great speech sir, the one you made
last Saturday!" sang the sycophant to his political mentor. "I particularly
admired the straightforward manner in which you twisted the issues!"
I read with consternation, and not without considerable disbelief, the
content of the front page report regarding what the President had said about
Mengo: clearly meaning the volunteers in the once GREAT Lukiiko and His
Majesty's non-salaried ministers: titled "Movement to clean up Mengo" (New
Vision, June 1).

Original_Search_Title: NASA Astronaut Finalist Lectures on New Information Technology
Headline: Cameroon: NASA Astronaut Finalist Lectures on New Information Technology
Body_Text:
The Cameroonian-born Dr Ernest Simo briefed IRIC students on the advantages of technology on Africa's socio-economic development.
The International Relations Institute of Cameroon (IRIC) was last week honoured with the presence of Dr Ernest Simo, a Cameroonian-born NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration) astronaut who gave a lecture on the theme "The New Information and Communication Technology and the Impact on the International System." After a brief presentation of the enviable curriculum vitae of Dr Ernest Simo by IRIC's Director Professor Jean Emmanuel Pondi, the astronaut dedicated the "Current Affairs Forum" to his colleagues who died in 1994 during the explosion of the Columbia space shuttle.

Original_Search_Title: Excessive Control Kills Exports
Headline: Zimbabwe: Excessive Control Kills Exports
Body_Text:
LET us also consider the case of a Zimbabwean exporter who imports some
of his raw materials, say 50%. Let's suppose he needs US$1 000 to make a
product, Z$9million at the official rate. Suppose he spends a further
Z$9million in Zimbabwe to finish the product. If he could get the forex on
the auction that would mean his costs would be Z$18million in total.
Continued from last weekBut getting foreign currency on the auction
system is next to impossible, the erstwhile governor himself can attest to
that. On the governor's terms the best option for him is to simply close
shop, to avoid making losses and eventually going under liquidation.

Original_Search_Title: The Killing Joke - Anthony Horowitz
Headline: South Africa: Books: The Killing Joke - Anthony Horowitz
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — ONE of the most difficult endeavours in the world is being consistently
funny, and Anthony Horowitz's novel THE KILLING JOKE (Orion R99,99) is
proof. It's not half as funny, or as consistent, as it could be. The
subtitle, "Making jokes is no laughing matter", is almost a universal
truth.
Horowitz attempted the near-impossible, and did fairly well, but has not
succeeded completely. This does not mean it is not a pleasant holiday or
aeroplane read, only that it is nothing more.

Original_Search_Title: Averting Threats to Nigeria
Headline: Nigeria: Averting Threats to Nigeria
Body_Text:
Lagos — Last week, President Olusegun Obasanjo reacted publicly to the report of the assessment and latest travel advice by the United States of America to her citizens, wishing to visit Nigeria. The said report, released on Tuesday, had painted a frightening picture of the Nigerian nation-state such that no reasonable foreigner, let alone American would attempt coming here without assuming that he/she is on a suicide mission.
The report is posted on the website of the Central Intelligence Agency(CIA).

Original_Search_Title: Miss Tourism Arusha 2005 Set for June 17
Headline: Tanzania: Miss Tourism Arusha 2005 Set for June 17
Body_Text:
Arusha — This year's Miss Tourism beauty pageant for Arusha Municipality will take place on Friday, June 17, at Hotel Seventy Seven Hotel. The contest is being organized by MS Unique promotions.
Arusha's own duo version of the US group, Kci and Jojo, the Sekei boys who are known as Man-

Original_Search_Title: So Much Change But Does It Matter?
Headline: Uganda: So Much Change But Does It Matter?
Body_Text:
It has been over a month since I returned to Kampala after an absence of one year. I was going to tell you where I was and what I was doing but then the editor for this section (let us skip her name because whether she is called Mary or Hadija doesn't really matter) reminded me that I am not Museveni, and so it doesn't matter to the readers where I have been or what I was doing there.
You wouldn't really care whether I was a ballet dancer in Paris, a bullfighter in Madrid or a gold dealer in Kisangani. But that last one would have been nice, especially if I didn't get waylaid and eaten by some tribesmen on the way back with my new riches.

Original_Search_Title: Banana Leaves Stay Green in London
Headline: Uganda: Banana Leaves Stay Green in London
Body_Text:
Kampala — Movement supporters in London suffered a setback when the green banana
leaves they airlifted from Kampala failed to dry up in time for the launch
of the "Kisanja" Committee.
A suitcase of the green leaves was flown from Uganda by members of the
NRM UK and Ireland chapter for the occasion where Cabinet ministers launched
the Kisanja Committee.

Original_Search_Title: Absa Dividend Policy Lights Up Results Presentation
Headline: South Africa: Absa Dividend Policy Lights Up Results Presentation
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — ONE of the highlights of Absa's results for the year, reported yesterday, has to be the increased dividend. The group lowered its dividend cover to 2,9 times due to its strong capital position, resulting in a dividend of 200c a share.
Last year, the dividend cover was raised to 3,8 times, from 3,6 times in 2003.

Original_Search_Title: How Army Officer Found Himself Hawking Water
Headline: Kenya: How Army Officer Found Himself Hawking Water
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A middle-aged man pushes a mkokoteni (hand cart) of jerrycans along Oginga Odinga Street in Kisumu.
Dominic Anzala Odongo, who claims to have been wrongfully dismissed from the armed forces, arranges his jerrycans of water near Kenya Railways estate in Kisumu. He now makes a living selling water.

Original_Search_Title: Name And Shame
Headline: Zimbabwe: Name And Shame
Body_Text:
Harare — ZIMBABWEANS have always hoped for full disclosures on those who are
destroying the economy through corrupt practices. Such an expose' would
constitute part of the national efforts to lower the boom on the now common
and widespread corruption.
But up until now, all they have been getting from the authorities is
rhetoric and very little if any, action. The best the government has done so
far is to admit that corruption has reached alarming levels. Seemingly
blinded or intimidated by the prominence, wealth and power of those involved
in sleaze, government has, to a large extent, refused to move a step further
and has instead curiously kept a lid on the identity of the culprits be they
in the private or public sectors.

Original_Search_Title: Mengo Attack Remembered
Headline: Uganda: Mengo Attack Remembered
Body_Text:
Kampala — WHEN Baganda wear banana fibre and barkcloth, they are in mourning. And so were scores of Baganda on Tuesday, as they marched from Mengo to Kasubi tombs, to mark the 1966 attack on Bulange.
It is 39 years since former president Milton Obote forced Kabaka Frederick Muteesa II into exile.

Original_Search_Title: Dismissed DPP Reveals Names of Prominent Officials Linked to Sh6.4Bn Cocaine Bust
Headline: Kenya: Murgor Spills the Beans
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Sacked Director of Public Prosecutions Philip Murgor yesterday named
seven prominent people he says tried to influence a Sh6.4 billion cocaine
case that may have caused his dismissal on Wednesday.
Murgor They include a Cabinet minister, a politician and senior
lawyers. Murgor also named police officers he believes are interfering with
the investigation, prosecution and disposal of the high stakes drug
consignment seized almost simultaneously in Mombasa and Nairobi late last
year.

Original_Search_Title: Keembe's Long Road to Development
Headline: Zambia: Keembe's Long Road to Development
Body_Text:
Taking development to a rural area is not an easy task. For a leader,
this calls for commitment, a sense of passion and a strong drive to work for
the underprivileged and the very poor at the grassroots.
It requires a personality that can endure and ensure that the community
is not left behind in any development process and economic activity of the
country.

Original_Search_Title: NIC Analyses Nigeria Failed State Scenario; Obasanjo Dismisses Report
Headline: Nigeria: U.S. Intelligence: Nigeria Will Fail in 15 Years
Body_Text:
Abuja —  They are living in the past, says Obasanjo
The United States National Intelligence Council in a document entitled
"Mapping Sub-Saharan Africa's Future" [pdf] has
predicted "outright collapse of Nigeria" as a nation-state within the next
15 years.

Original_Search_Title: Legislators Get Trained On Law Making
Headline: Nigeria: Legislators Get Trained On Law Making
Body_Text:
Lagos —  The Centre for Constitutional Governance (CCG) with the support of
National Endowment for Democracy (NED), Washington DC, USA sponsored a
two-day training workshop for state legislatures and Community Development
Committees (CDC) of the south west states last week. AKINWALE AKINTUNDE
reports
The programme which was essentially a training workshop, focused on
facilitating sustainable participatory mechanism between civil society and
legislature in the lawmaking process started with a welcome address by the
Executive Director of Centre for Constitutional Governance, Dr. Beko
Ransome-Kuti.

Original_Search_Title: Ottilie Abrahams: 'Born to Make a Difference'
Headline: Namibia: Ottilie Abrahams: 'Born to Make a Difference'
Body_Text:
Windhoek — BECAUSE members of the notorious South African security police were baying for her blood, she was spirited out of the country disguised as a secondary school pupil apparently 'expelled from school after falling pregnant'.
Part of this pregnancy expulsion plot involved covering her face with red clay tribal powder make-up (otji-ze) and dressing herself in an oversized Ovaherero traditional dress while using the name Elsa Kairazikira. We are talking about a woman who has been through thick and thin but who never wavered in her convictions.

Original_Search_Title: When a Sentence to Jail Can Be a Sentence to Death
Headline: Cote d'Ivoire: CÃ´te d'Ivoire: When a Sentence to Jail Can Be a Sentence to Death
Body_Text:
Dimbokro — After five years in prison, Prospere Kongo's only desire is to get out
alive. "Here it's like, as soon as one guy dies, the next is about to drop
dead," the illiterate taxi driver said.
Prospere said he was caught with two accomplices when they tried to rob
a Lebanese trader at gunpoint. The three men were jailed in Dimbokro, a
small town in central Cote d'Ivoire. But Prospere's mates are already
dead. "They contracted some kind of skin infection and died," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Has Made the Presidency Attractive
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Has Made the Presidency Attractive
Body_Text:
More than 60 years ago, British war hero Prime minister Winston Churchill, who led Britain to victory and set it on the path of economic prosperity, said: "When I was called upon to be prime minister, now nearly two years ago, there were not many applicants for the job. Since then perhaps the market has improved," Churchill informed the House of Commons in January 1942.
That was Prime Minister Churchill then, but it can as well be said of President Yoweri Museveni, Uganda's undisputed Biblical Moses.

Original_Search_Title: Resign, Kako Tells Bishop Kamya
Headline: Uganda: Resign, Kako Tells Bishop Kamya
Body_Text:
Masaka — The Parents Teachers Association (PTA) of Kako Secondary School has
asked the Bishop of West Buganda Diocese, the Rt. Rev. Samuel Ssemakula
Kamya, to resign his position of Chairman of Board of governors of the
school.
Kamya has also been asked to pay Shs14 million, he allegedly owes the
school.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Already Airborne As the Opposition Sleeps On
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Already Airborne As the Opposition Sleeps On
Body_Text:
The leg is set for the 2006 general elections. President Yoweri Museveni, according to political onlookers has already kicked off the campaigns, though none of his helpers would want to admit it.
Foremost, he toured Eastern Uganda in February this year. Last week he raked through Mid and Western Uganda under the flagship of eradicating poverty, showing appreciation for the support to the area has given the Movement and "kwenegyereza", (a Runyankore word meaning "to push further") development projects as he variously stated it in the nine-day meet-the-people engagement tour.

Original_Search_Title: Kisanja Councillor Chased From Lc5 Council Meeting
Headline: Uganda: Kisanja Councillor Chased From Lc5 Council Meeting
Body_Text:
Nebbi — A councillor was on Monday thrown out of a district council session for wearing dry banana leaves (essanja) around his neck as a sign of support for a third term for President Yoweri Museveni.
Mr Ronald Nyathirombo, who represents Kango sub-county in the district council, was ordered by the Speaker, Mr David Ringe Chan, to leave the meeting because he was shabbily dressed.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Already Airborne As the Opposition Sleeps On
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Already Airborne As the Opposition Sleeps On
Body_Text:
The leg is set for the 2006 general elections. President Yoweri Museveni, according to political onlookers has already kicked off the campaigns, though none of his helpers would want to admit it.
Foremost, he toured Eastern Uganda in February this year. Last week he raked through Mid and Western Uganda under the flagship of eradicating poverty, showing appreciation for the support to the area has given the Movement and "kwenegyereza", (a Runyankore word meaning "to push further") development projects as he variously stated it in the nine-day meet-the-people engagement tour.

Original_Search_Title: An Imperfect Style of Democracy - But It Works
Headline: South Africa: An Imperfect Style of Democracy - But It Works
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — A BRITISH diplomat asked for my assessment of last week's general
election
in Britain. "The election was credible," I said, "and the result was a
legitimate reflection of the wishes of British voters."

Original_Search_Title: Kenya, Uganda Lose $140m in Illicit Trade
Headline: East Africa: Kenya, Uganda Lose $140m in Illicit Trade
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Illicit cross-border trade between Kenya and Uganda is estimated to cost about $140 million annually in losses to their national treasuries.
At a regional workshop held at the Windsor Golf and Country Club Nairobi last week, partner states decried the rate of illicit trade, which has not only affected their revenue base, but also posed a threat to human lives.

Original_Search_Title: Kenya Has Become a Banana Republic
Headline: Kenya Has Become a Banana Republic
Body_Text:
Nairobi — When they voted Narc at the last election, many Kenyans expected a New Deal. They are still waiting as the country descends into a series of farcical comedies
Historians rank Franklin D. Roosevelt as one of America's five greatest presidents. He was elected to four unprecedented terms, and could have gone for the fifth, were it not for high blood pressure and arteriosclerosis that killed him on April 12, 1945.

Original_Search_Title: Smugglers Deny Exchequer Sh320m, Say Industrialists
Headline: Kenya: Smugglers Deny Exchequer Sh320m, Say Industrialists
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kenyan traders are the biggest promoters of illegal trade along its
borders, which is estimated at Sh320 million annually.
Participants at a regional workshop held in Nairobi heard that smugglers
operating along the Kenya-Uganda border exported goods and services worth
US$4.7 million (Sh350 million).

Original_Search_Title: Ethnic Fighting Leaves 10,000 Sleeping Out, Afraid to Go Home
Headline: Cote d'Ivoire: Cote D Ivoire: Ethnic Fighting Leaves 10,000 Sleeping Out, Afraid to Go Home
Body_Text:
Duekoue — Few of the 10,000 people who fled ethnic violence in this western Cote d'Ivoire town are thinking about going home, with thousands choosing to sleep out in the grounds of a local church even though they are packed together like sardines and there is little to eat.
The trouble in Duekoue erupted last week, when the Guere people in the town refused to join a strike to protest security problems that had been organised by the Dioula ethnic group.

Original_Search_Title: Prospects in Limpopo Under Spotlight
Headline: South Africa: Prospects in Limpopo Under Spotlight
Body_Text:
Polokwane — Limpopo's future prospects as an investment destination have
received a boost during an Indaba sponsored by Trade and Investment Limpopo
(TIL).
TIL CE Joe Mathebula told the gathering of industrialists and other business
entrepreneurs that Limpopo's strategic location as the gateway to Southern
African Development Community (SADC), as well as its ever-growing rural
consumer market, made it an attractive option for potential investors.

Original_Search_Title: Rail Assets to Stay in State Hands - Erwin
Headline: South Africa: Rail Assets to Stay in State Hands - Erwin
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — SA's rail infrastructure will remain firmly in state hands for the
foreseeable future, says Public Enterprises Minister Alec Erwin.
The minister was responding yesterday to a question about labour unions'
fears that govern-ment planned to privatise rail utility Spoornet, which
would spark fears of job losses.

Original_Search_Title: Spar Records Increase in Customers
Headline: Zambia: Spar Records Increase in Customers
Body_Text:
Lusaka — ABOUT 100,000 customers enter Spar every month, Spar Arcades Limited
acting general manager Wongani Muthiya has said.
And Spar Arcades yesterday launched a promotion where customers aged 55
years and above would every Tuesday be granted a 5 per cent discount.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Threatens to Arrest FDC Man
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Threatens to Arrest FDC Man
Body_Text:
Kabarole — President Yoweri Museveni has threatened to arrest the Forum for Democratic Change (FDC) chairman for Kabarole district, Mr Edison Asaaba.
He is accusing him of holding illegal talkshows in Kabarole.

Original_Search_Title: President Excites Bundibugyo
Headline: Uganda: President Excites Bundibugyo
Body_Text:
Kampala — THOUSANDS of people wearing dry banana leaves and singing pro-third term slogans on Saturday welcomed President Yoweri Museveni to Bundibugyo town.
Museveni, who is currently on a tour of mid-western Uganda, assured the excited crowd at the Boma Grounds that the UPDF was now better equipped than ever before. The Police estimated that there were over 20,000 people at the ceremony. Quite a number wore the NRM yellow T-Shirts with 'Third Term' inscribed at the back.

Original_Search_Title: Opening of Major By-Passes to Ease City's Traffic Load
Headline: Kenya: Opening of Major By-Passes to Ease City's Traffic Load
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Traffic congestion in Nairobi may soon ease with the opening of two of three by-passes.
The Eastern by-pass is expected to be opened by the end of June, followed soon after by the Southern one, says Roads and Public Works Minister Raila Odinga.

Original_Search_Title: MDC Must Adopt a New Paradigm
Headline: Zimbabwe: MDC Must Adopt a New Paradigm
Body_Text:
WE know that the reason the MDC has lost the last two elections has nothing to do with the economic wizardry of the Zanu PF party.
Judging from two case studies, Michael Manley's Jamaican assault on the American Bauxite Companies (1971-75) and Idi Amin's assault on the Asian merchants in Uganda (1971-1979), the Mugabe land experiment in Zimbabwe has no chance whatever of succeeding. The MDC, as the opposition party, must therefore search for a new paradigm.

Original_Search_Title: 'My Name is Flavour; I Sell Flavoured Condoms'
Headline: Kenya: 'My Name is Flavour; I Sell Flavoured Condoms'
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Excuse me, please," the beaming lady walks up to a couple enjoying their evening drink at a popular Nairobi pub.
"Hey, you are having a lovely time, how is the evening?" she carries on as she pulls a chair uninvited.

Original_Search_Title: A Glimpse At Hell's Kitchen
Headline: Kenya: A Glimpse At Hell's Kitchen
Body_Text:
Nairobi — This is a bowl in the earth's belly that's as hot as hell
Hell's Kitchen isn't for the faint hearted - that's if you land there in the heat of the midday. We decide on an impromptu lunch standing near the Sabaki bridge under which the thick mud laden waters of the river flow into the bay of Malindi. The fruit vendors are happy to sell us all that's in season - mangoes, bananas, pineapple and the deliciously cool watermelon. Not bad, I think to myself, lunch for five at a cost of Sh100, besides it being healthy and good for the waistline. The thing about being at the coast is that you're constantly in and out of swimsuits and so it's better to stay in shape.

Original_Search_Title: Sure, Bureaucracy's Not Working, But What If It Did ... to the Letter?
Headline: South Africa: Sure, Bureaucracy's Not Working, But What If It Did ... to the Letter?
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — A COMMON cry in suburban SA is that we are governed by a dysfunctional state.
In many ways I agree, but sometimes I wonder, in such cases, whether you should be careful what you wish for, because you might just get it.

Original_Search_Title: American Ambassador Promises to Bring in Investors
Headline: Cameroon: American Ambassador Promises to Bring in Investors
Body_Text:
He made the declaration after visiting the one-stop shop and the Douala Port.
The United States Ambassador to Cameroon, Niels Marquardt has said he will bring in more investors to Cameroon. Mr. Marquardt was speaking in Douala yesterday after a one-day visit that took him to the one-stop shop and the Douala Port. At the one-stop shop which was his first stop, the United States Ambassador handed over Certificates of Appreciation to the Managing Directors of the Ports Authority, the one-stop shop and Director of Customs for the excellent job they have been doing. The certificates he said, were a sign of appreciation of the American Government with the efforts put in by the three services to make the Port of Douala and the one-stop shop the best in the region. It is, he added because of the excellent work put in by these services that the Chad-Cameroon pipeline (which is the highest investment of the World Bank in the Central African region), registered a great success.

Original_Search_Title: Nateete Residents Strike Over Road
Headline: Uganda: Nateete Residents Strike Over Road
Body_Text:
Kampala — TRAFFIC flow between Kibuye and Nateete on Masaka Road came to a
standstill on Monday as hundreds of people demonstrated against the poor
state of the road.
Protesters from Kibuye, Nateete and Nalukolongo city suburbs lined up on
the road carrying placards and tree branches.

Original_Search_Title: Strong-Arm Tactics Are Not the Answer
Headline: Kenya: Strong-Arm Tactics Are Not the Answer
Body_Text:
Nairobi — At a time when the security situation is terrible, scary and seemingly
uncontrollable, it is seductive to resort to "tough guy" measures as a
response.
In recent times, we have seen suggestions that the ways to deal with
insecurity include restoring the illegal "shoot-to-kill" approach of the
police, and powers of chiefs to the levels that led to severe repression of
communities.

Original_Search_Title: Banana Wilt Hits Western Uganda
Headline: Uganda: Banana Wilt Hits Western Uganda
Body_Text:
Mbarara — The banana bacterial wilt has been cited in Bushenyi and Ntungamo. The
head of the National Banana Research Programme at Kawanda Research Centre,
Mr Joseph Sabiiti, said the disease was discovered in 2001 in Mukono
district and had since spread to more than 21 districts in.
"It has now spread to Bushenyi and Ntungamo in western Uganda
districts," Sabiiti said at the district stakeholders' workshop on banana
bacterial wilt identification and management, last Wednesday.

Original_Search_Title: All Hands Must Be On Deck to Remove the 'Banana Peel' - Nnamani
Headline: Nigeria: All Hands Must Be On Deck to Remove the 'Banana Peel' - Nnamani
Body_Text:
Lagos —  New Senate President, Chief Ken Nnamani, recently granted our
correspondent, COSMAS EKPUNOBI an interview in which, he speaks of his
relationship with his state's governor, Dr. Chimaroke Nnamani, and his new
assignment as the helmsman of the apex legislative chamber in the
country. Excerpts:
WE saw you holding hands with your state governor while inspecting some
of the projects under construction in the state, this is contrary to the
feelings in some quarters about your relationship with him. How did it
happen?

Original_Search_Title: Useful Tips for New Senate President
Headline: Nigeria: Useful Tips for New Senate President
Body_Text:
Lagos — Nothing demonstrates the transient nature of power more than the seat of the Senate President. They say it is Musical Chairs and Banana Peel. They mean the same thing. It is about how it has become traditional for the occupant of the seat to fall from the exalted position - and mostly in a disgraceful manner. It is not by mere coincidence that only Anyim Pius Anyim left at the expiration of his tenure. Even then, he was lucky to have escaped the pitfalls to which his predecessors had fallen preys.
Anyim came, and having learnt from the fall of his predecessors, devised three ingenuous survival strategies. What did he do? He feigned loyalty to President Obasanjo; he decentralised the Senate budget, giving each committee and each Senator, equal access to funds, thereby deceiving his colleagues into believing the centre was no longer as attractive as before. Therefore, there was less interest in the Senate President's seat. Lastly, he carried the leadership of his party, the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) along within his limited resources. For instance, when there was threat from the party leadership, Anyim was able to rein the party in by making the world know that he had once received a request from the party for the sum of N120 million to organise a retreat. The threat from the party chairman then, Chief Audu Ogbeh, died thereafter. Wabara came at the beginning of this dispensation, swearing and assuring himself that he would break the jinx of the disgraceful fall by completing his four-year tenure.

Original_Search_Title: Parents Debunk Film Star's 'Resurrection'
Headline: Nigeria: Parents Debunk Film Star's 'Resurrection'
Body_Text:
Parents of Balaraba Mohammed, a film star who lost her life on her wedding day in a ghastly motor accident on Kaduna-Zaria expressway, have debunked rumours that their daughter had risen from death.
Rumours that late Balaraba had resurrected spread like wild-fire in almost all major cities of the North recently."

Original_Search_Title: Herbs Helping Fight HIV And Aids
Headline: Zimbabwe: Herbs Helping Fight HIV And Aids
Body_Text:
MANY rural communities in most parts of Zimbabwe are realising the importance of using natural remedies such as herbs to treat some HIV-related opportunistic infections because they have been let down by the ailing health system.
Zimbabwe's health sector has deteriorated over the years and rural communities have been the worst affected as shortages of drugs and other essential medicines continue to dog rural district hospitals and clinics.

Original_Search_Title: I Agree with Nnamani That the Era of Bannana Peels in the Senate is over - Sen Adogba
Headline: Nigeria: I Agree with Nnamani That the Era of Bannana Peels in the Senate is over - Sen Adogba
Body_Text:
Lagos — Senator Joshua Adogba (Benue) is the Vice Chairman Senate Committee on NDDC, in this interview with our Senate correspondent Cosmas Enpunobi he threw more insight on why Senator Nnamani was unanimously elected and why the senate should support Obasanjo's war against corruption.
SENATOR Nnamani was last week elected fifth senate president what does this new leadership hold for the legislature, are you not worried about the many changes?

Original_Search_Title: Subsidies Short-Change the Developing World
Headline: Namibia: Subsidies Short-Change the Developing World
Body_Text:
Windhoek — SUBSIDIES and protectionist measures, especially in the developed world, hinder the access of international markets of African fish products and have also led to the extinction of certain fish species.
This, according to the Permanent Secretary in the Ministry of Fisheries and Marine Resources, Nangula Mbako, "has serious consequences for developing nations that depend heavily on revenues from fish exports".

Original_Search_Title: MMCI Reaches Out to Rural Areas
Headline: Zambia: MMCI Reaches Out to Rural Areas
Body_Text:
IF at all there is a non-governmental organisation (NGO) that is close
with the grassroots level and assisting them with most of their requirements
in the communities, it is the Maureen Mwanawasa Community Initiative
(MMCI).
Formed with the objective of alleviating poverty among women, the MMCI
has not only assisted women's clubs along the line of rail but in the rural
areas as well.

Original_Search_Title: 'Sergeant Doctor' Gives Up His Big Guns
Headline: Cote d'Ivoire: Cote D Ivoire: 'Sergeant Doctor' Gives Up His Big Guns
Body_Text:
Djebonoua — As children played by the roadside and women sold bananas to lorry drivers at this rebel checkpoint on the main north-south highway in Cote d'Ivoire, two military jeeps emerged from the surrounding bush dragging behind them field guns painted with the name of the local rebel commander, "Sergeant Doctor."
Slowly - one of the 120 mm canons had a puncture - the big guns were towed 16 km north to the rebel capital Bouake.

Original_Search_Title: Senate Adjourns Screening of Osuji, Osomo's Successors
Headline: Nigeria: Senate Adjourns Screening of Osuji, Osomo's Successors
Body_Text:
Abuja — The Senate yesterday adjourned till next legislative day (Tuesday), screening of nominees named by President Olusegun Obasanjo, to replace the dismissed Minister for Education, Professor Fabian Osuji, and former Housing and Urban Development Minister, Mrs Mobolaji Osomo.
Those awaiting Senate screening are Mrs Chinwe Obaji (Imo) and Prince Adetokunbo Kayode (Ondo).

Original_Search_Title: Mayuge Celebrates On Tigawalana's Bail
Headline: Uganda: Mayuge Celebrates On Tigawalana's Bail
Body_Text:
Kampala — ULULATION engulfed Mayuge town yesterday as thousands of Baker Ikoba
Tigawalana's supporters celebrated his release from jail, writes Abubaker
Mukose and Moses Nampala.
Thick dust filled the air as excited supporters donning essanja (dry
banana leaves) relentlessly stomped their feet on the streets.

Original_Search_Title: Kisanja Welcomes Museveni to Gulu
Headline: Uganda: Kisanja Welcomes Museveni to Gulu
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni on Sunday received a warm welcome from the
Movement youth and pro-third term supporters in Gulu district.
The youth donning kisanja (dry banana leaves) lined up along the
airfield road chanting, "no change, give Museveni another term, we want a
third term."

Original_Search_Title: Graduands Oppose Kisanja On Party
Headline: Uganda: Graduands Oppose Kisanja On Party
Body_Text:
Gulu — University graduands from Gulu, Kitgum and Pader districts on Saturday disagreed with kisanja supporters during a joint graduation party.
The graduands, who had gathered in front of the office of the District Education Officer, were taken by surprise when kisanja supporters dressed in dry banana leaves stormed the place from where they were to match to Pece.

Original_Search_Title: Hundreds Displaced By Inter-Ethnic Violence in Confidence Zone
Headline: Cote d'Ivoire: CÃ´te d'Ivoire: Hundreds Displaced By Inter-Ethnic Violence in Confidence Zone
Body_Text:
Boli — An exchange of insults between two children of different ethnic groups
mushroomed two days later into full-scale fighting between neighbours in
this large village in the no-man's land between government and rebel lines
in Cote d'Ivoire.
Houses were burned, cattle were killed, several people received machete
wounds and 1,000 members of the minority Dioula community in Boli last month
trekked out to seek shelter in the rebel sector.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Asks Mbeki to Stop Besigye
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Asks Mbeki to Stop Besigye
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has warned former presidential candidate Dr. Kizza Besigye against using South Africa as a platform against Uganda.
Addressing a press conference at State House Nakasero yesterday, Museveni said he recently met South African President Thabo Mbeki, in New York, over the matter.

Original_Search_Title: Stop the Campaigns, ECK Pleads As Violence Starts
Headline: Kenya: Stop the Campaigns, ECK Pleads As Violence Starts
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Electoral Commission of Kenya was last evening headed for a
collision with Cabinet ministers after it called for an end to the "Yes" and
"No" vote campaigns until the civic education exercise was completed.
ECK Chairman Samuel Kivuitu put up a press advertisement calling for an
end to the campaigns till the stipulated period - October 21 - a day after
Vice President Moody Awori led 11 ministers in a strategy meeting, which
drew a programme for a nationwide "Yes" vote campaign beginning Friday.

Original_Search_Title: D-Day for Historic Constitution Vote
Headline: Kenya: D-Day for Historic Constitution Vote
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Monday, November 21 will be decision day for the new Constitution.
That is the day the national referendum will be held when 11.8 million
registered voters will cast their ballot for either Yes or No.

Original_Search_Title: Constitution Countdown Begins
Headline: Kenya: Constitution Countdown Begins
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kenyans will go to the referendum to adopt or reject the Proposed New Constitution on November 21, the Government announced yesterday.
Electoral Commission of Kenya (ECK) chairman Samuel Kivuitu set off the countdown to the historic poll, but warned that it would cost more than the Sh3 billion allocated by the Government.

Original_Search_Title: The Death of Pafo Led to Triumph of Kiisanja
Headline: Uganda: The Death of Pafo Led to Triumph of Kiisanja
Body_Text:
Kampala — WHEN she was still a member of the powerful Parliamentary Advocacy Forum (PAFO), MP for Bugabula, Salaamu Musumba told reporters in 2003 that PAFO had over 120 MPs behind the fight against life presidency.
"We are now working to consolidate those numbers," she said. At the time, the pro-kisanja camp in parliament was not sure of their numbers. In fact, because of this, they started agitating for the issue of Article 105 to be taken to the people and resolved in a referendum. The anti-kisanja agitators refused.

Original_Search_Title: Only God Can Stop 3rd Term Bid - Sen Bunza
Headline: Nigeria: Only God Can Stop 3rd Term Bid - Sen Bunza
Body_Text:
Despite President Olusegun Obasanjo's declaration that the tenure of his administration ends in 2007, there are strong indications that the move to manouver a third term in office for the president may be real.
Senator Farouk Bello Bunza (ANPP/Kebbi Central), says President Olusegun Obasanjo's resolve and that of some governors to stay in power beyond 2007 is real and can spell doom for the nation. He particularly lamented that governors keen on holding on to power are now at the fore front to actualise this agenda.

Original_Search_Title: Self-Succession: Swimming Against the Tide
Headline: Nigeria: Self-Succession: Swimming Against the Tide
Body_Text:
One must start this analysis with a question: Can self-succession advocates resist the rapidly moving armour of popular and moral resistance? At every turn, those canvassing for the extension of President Olusegun obasanjo's tenure beyond 2007 are now fast realizing that Nigerians are far smarter and perspicacious in seeing through their shenanigans than they could possibly ever believe. Their quandary is heightened by the fact that their strategy of denial is being roundly defeated every passing day. And since they want to be foxier than the fox, a few self-serving, sycophantic politicians are trying desperately hard to make Nigerians believe such obnoxious agenda does not exist.
However, what is interesting about the self-succession controversy is no longer the denial strategy, but the rising opposition to such agenda by prominent Nigerians across the ethnic, political and religious divide. For example, the pan-Yoruba socio-politico-cultural organizational, Afenifere, has launched a salvo of opposition to the smart moves to extend President Obasanjo's tenure beyond 2007, urging Nigerians to rise with the same moral revulsion they demonstrated against a similar trick by the late General Sani Abacha to succeed himself.

Original_Search_Title: NRM Numbers Are a Hoax, Says Bidandi
Headline: Uganda: NRM Numbers Are a Hoax, Says Bidandi
Body_Text:
Kampala — Former Minister of Local Government, Mr Bidandi Ssali, has criticised President Yoweri Museveni's campaign catch words of "let's vote to get rid of them" in the recently concluded referendum, saying they were unconstitutional.
Bidandi said constitutionally, the issue was changing the political system but not chasing away people from the Movement.

Original_Search_Title: Brewing Wine From Pawpaw And Hibiscus
Headline: Uganda: Brewing Wine From Pawpaw And Hibiscus
Body_Text:
Kampala — "MY cherished dream has come true at last. Safinah, this has been my
argument all along that one day someone would brew wine out of pawpaws," a
young man told his wife during the recent Gatsby Small Scale industrial
exhibitors show at Uganda Manufacturers Association (UMA) grounds.
The couple had come to one Tanzanian Gatsby stall exhibiting wines
brewed from pawpaw, Roselle Hibiscus, Sabdariffa (Hibiscus) and banana.

Original_Search_Title: IMF Must Hear This
Headline: Cameroon: IMF Must Hear This
Body_Text:
An IMF mission is currently in the Cameroons to find out whether the government has respected the conditionalities to reach the completion point of the Highest Indebted Poor Country, HIPC-Initiative, and benefit from a debt cancellation of FCFA 1,400 billion.
In fact, if the IMF and the other Bretton Woods institutions are serious, they would know that Cameroon cannot reach the completion point, because the government is not serious and is dishonest.

Original_Search_Title: Falae, Balarabe Musa Oppose Obasanjo's 3rd Term
Headline: Nigeria: Falae, Balarabe Musa Oppose Obasanjo's 3rd Term
Body_Text:
Former finance minister and APP presidential aspirant in the 1999
general elections, Chief Olu Falae, has cautioned President Olusegun
Obasanjo against attempts to extend his tenure beyond 2007, saying that
"Nigeria is not a banana republic for the people to be taken for granted."
This is coming on the heels of remarks by the chairman, Conference of
Nigerian Political Parties (CNPP), Alhaji Balarabe Musa, that president
Obasanjo will do everything possible to hang on to power beyond 2007 because
the President would be sent to prison on account of his constitutional
misdemea-nours since 1999.

Original_Search_Title: Hawkers Score Big
Headline: South Africa: Hawkers Score Big
Body_Text:
Mafikeng — North West street hawkers are smiling all the way to the bank
after scoring a huge profit during the soccer semi-finals of the Vodacom
Challenge in Mafikeng at the weekend.
Galaletsang Ntshupetsang of Magogoe, said her profits rose by more than
40 percent whenever the Vodacom Challenge was played at Mmabatho
Stadium.

Original_Search_Title: The TAC is Unbowed By 'Donkiepiels'
Headline: South Africa: The TAC is Unbowed By 'Donkiepiels'
Body_Text:
The government should launch an immediate investigation into the police's use of rubber bullets and tear gas against peaceful Treatment Action Campaign (TAC) demonstrators in the Eastern Cape, Human Rights Watch said on Thursday.
In a statement from New York, the organisation's Jonathan Cohen called the police action a "shocking irony", adding that "South Africa should be easing the suffering of people with Aids, not violently dispersing peaceful demonstrations".

Original_Search_Title: Let Laz Look At Everything
Headline: Zambia: Let Laz Look At Everything
Body_Text:
Lusaka — ARROGANCE and lack of humility does not pay, it just ends up compounding one's problems.
Honesty and humility on the other hand can get someone out of very big problems or mitigate in a very big way one's tribulations.

Original_Search_Title: Will Cameroonians Accept Another Fuel Price Increase?
Headline: Cameroon: Will Cameroonians Accept Another Fuel Price Increase?
Body_Text:
The government last weekend increased the price of fuel, petrol, by FCFA 16. The FCFA 16 raise is the highest in recent times. The new price took effect all over the national territory on Monday, July 4.
The fuel price increase is the second this year. About five months ago the government raised fuel prices by FCFA 5.

Original_Search_Title: Igbo Community in Lagos Hosts Senate President
Headline: Nigeria: Igbo Community in Lagos Hosts Senate President
Body_Text:
Lagos — Igbo community in Lagos would this month play host to the Senate
President, Senator Ken Nnamani for his recent elevation to the exalted
office of the nation's apex legislature.
This plan was disclosed to Journalists in Lagos by the Chairman, Board
of Trustees of Igbo Community in Lagos, His Royal Highness, Igwe
M.U. Chukwuka. He said the reception, which the President-General of Igbo
Community, Chief Jonathan Nnaji who is the head of the planning committee is
to display the community's solidarity with the senate president.

Original_Search_Title: Issue of New State House Raises Hackles in National Assembly
Headline: Namibia: Issue of New State House Raises Hackles in the NA
Body_Text:
Windhoek — CoD President Ben Ulenga caused a furore in the National Assembly on
Tuesday evening when he told parliamentarians that he would have
expected President Hifikepunye Pohamba to have "postponed" the
building of the new State House in line with commitments to use
resources prudently on priority areas.
"It is sad that the President's actions and will don't seem to go
into the postponement of building State House.

Original_Search_Title: The Change of Guards (2)
Headline: Nigeria: The Verdict: The Change of Guards (2)
Body_Text:
Lagos — As the Agusta A109 Power helicopter crossed the Third Mainland Bridge
into the lagoon within two minutes of take-off at Ikeja, I looked with
sadness at the scene below me -- how could human beings inhabit such squalor
at Okobaba, Ebute-Metta?
Lazy brown waters snaked through the clusters of makehsift houses in a
textbook illustration of the word slum. I wished I was with a government
official to see how he would react to the scene below and perhaps to ask
whether those helpless Nigerians, whose votes are usually vital in election
manipulation, had not been abandoned. But the moment was fleeting as the
helicopter overflew the Banana Island, perhaps the most expensive area of
Lagos designed for the super rich.

Original_Search_Title: Officials Wrangle, Liberian Football Bleeds
Headline: Liberia: Officials Wrangle, Liberian Football Bleeds
Body_Text:
In the streets of the Liberian capital, Monrovia, a teenage footballer defines the state of what used to be the country's leading sport with a formula: Liberian football-Oppong = Disaster. He's not the architect of this formula but it is the word in Monrovia and the grim reality one faces.
Since Weah retired from active football and now seemed to have washed his hands off all affairs following what many Liberians including sports officials think was a bias and an unnecessary technicality to bar him from standing for the FA's top post, Liberian football has become a lamb duck sport.

Original_Search_Title: Metaphor of the Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Metaphor of the Banana Peel
Body_Text:
The banana peel has been at the top of the Nigerian polity for a long time.
THE renowned Bini folklorist and Akwette King Musician, Sir Victor Uwaifo and his Melody Maestros, reminded us of the sweetness of banana in his song "Sweet Banana Sweet". The song made waves in the late sixties and early seventies.

Original_Search_Title: Soccer Craze On Satellite TV
Headline: Kenya: Soccer Craze On Satellite TV
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Two weeks ago, Manchester United lost to Chelsea, and Everton was defeated by Arsenal in English Premier League football matches televised live in many countries, including Kenya, thanks to satellite television.
On the two nights the matches were played, numerous Nairobi pubs had more patrons than is usual for a mid month weeknight. Kenyans were out to watch the top four English Premiership clubs' final encounter, and their second last match before the league season ended last Sunday.

Original_Search_Title: Rally Behind RBZ
Headline: Zimbabwe: Rally Behind RBZ
Body_Text:
Harare — THE Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe yesterday upped the ante in its relentless fight against all-pervading corruption engulfing the nation. And that is as it should be.
There is need for renewed commitment to deal with corruption.

Original_Search_Title: Police, AD Disagree Over Killing of Party Chieftain
Headline: Nigeria: Police, AD Disagree Over Killing of Party Chieftain
Body_Text:
Osogbo —  Lagos House calls for probe
OSUN State Police Command and the Alliance for Democracy (AD) yesterday disagreed over the motive behind Sunday's killing of the party's chieftain, Alhaji Hassan Olajokun.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Should Consider 'Domestic Relations' in Quest for Third Term
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Should Consider 'Domestic Relations' in Quest for Third Term
Body_Text:
Nairobi — War might erupt anytime in Uganda. But this time it will not be about
politics. The impending war will be about women. Precisely, the ability to
acquire a second woman without the first wife interfering in the
process.
War is a serious thing and had the war cry not been issued by a serious
group, we would have taken it for a joke. It is none other than the radical
sect of Muslims, the Tabliqs, which has stated clearly that should the
Domestic Relations Bill (DRB) pass in its present form, where a man must
seek the consent of his wife if he wants to take on a second one, they will
wage a jihad (holy war).

Original_Search_Title: Customary Land Rights Versus Those of the Registered Owner
Headline: Kenya: Customary Land Rights Versus Those of the Registered Owner
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There is intergenerational equity where land is held by one generation for the benefit of succeeding ones. The land was ancestral. It devolved to one person on the death of the other (the father). It was unregistered land held under custom, but the tenure changed during the consolidation process and the subsequent registration. Mukangu vs Mbui [eKLR] E.O. O'Kubasu , E.M Githinji, P.N. Waki JJA, Court of Appeal at Nyeri Civil Appeal 281 of 2000, 10th December 2004
A father owned two parcels of land, one inherited from his father and the other one bought over time. Owing to bad blood, his son Gerald Mutwiri Mbui sued him (Mbui Mukangu) in 1993. The son asked the court to stop him alienating the two parcels of land and to force him to transfer portions of them to him. The son did not succeed in that attempt, but he continued to occupy the land.

Original_Search_Title: Jazz: Live At the Iridium - Monty Alexander
Headline: South Africa: Jazz: Live At the Iridium - Monty Alexander
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — PIANIST Monty Alexander must be one of the most prolific jazz artists on
the scene, with an album appearing, regular as clockwork, each year - and
occasionally twice a year.
His latest for Telarc, LIVE AT THE IRIDIUM, is a quartet set with
bassist Hassan Shakur, percussionist Robert Thomas Jr and drummer Mark
Taylor.

Original_Search_Title: Privatisation Creates Fewer Jobs, Says RDC
Headline: Uganda: Privatisation Creates Fewer Jobs, Says RDC
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Kampala Resident District Commissioner (rdc), Dr Stanley Kinyatta,
has asked the Government to review privatisation, saying it has not created
many employment opportunities as expected.
Kinyatta said most investors come with their own personnel.

Original_Search_Title: International Criminal Court a Waste of Time
Headline: Kenya: International Criminal Court a Waste of Time
Body_Text:
Nairobi — There is little to admire about the last four years of US foreign policy; except its utter contempt for the International Criminal Court (ICC). When George W. Bush came to power in late 2000, he made it abundantly clear that no American - soldier or civilian - would ever be handed over for trial by the The Hague-based ICC. Period. A round of applause for Dubya, if you please!
Established on July 17, 1998 under the "United Nations Diplomatic Conference of Plenipotentiaries on the Establishment of an International Criminal Court", the Rome Statute was initially adopted by 120 nations including the three East African countries. The statute came into force on July 1, 2002 and since then, any individual anywhere (the USA excepted of course) who commits certain crimes (genocide, ethnic cleansing, rape, war criminality etc), can be extradited to The Hague, to be tried by mainly European lawyers, before mainly European judges and eventually sentenced to lengthy jail-terms in mainly European prisons.

Original_Search_Title: Petty Traders Stay Put
Headline: Botswana: Petty Traders Stay Put
Body_Text:
Francistown — Despite the numerous attempts to evict them, petty traders
still operate freely in what are supposed to be no go areas.
The Francistown City Council (FCC) has lamented that the traders occupy
parking lots and inconvenience those who want to buy from shops. They also
disturb the flow of traffic as most of their stalls extend to the road.

Original_Search_Title: Most Senators Rigged Elections - Sen. Tsauri
Headline: Nigeria: Most Senators Rigged Elections - Sen. Tsauri
Body_Text:
Lagos — ECHOES of the controversial 2003 general elections reverberated yesterday on the floor of the Senate when Senator Umaru Tsauri, chairman Senate Services Committee alleged that most senators rigged elections.
He was however promptly rebuked by his colleagues for making such sweeping allegation. Senators in separate reactions to the accusation also called for the immediate scrapping of States Independent Electoral Commissions (SIECs) to reduce electoral fraud in future.

Original_Search_Title: A Signature Line for Ken Nnamani
Headline: Nigeria: A Signature Line for Ken Nnamani
Body_Text:
Lagos — IT"s neither too early nor too late to begin to ask how history will remember the leadership of the current National Assembly, a place someone in cold anger, described as an assemblage of legislators it has been our misfortune to have in the 21st century. While this may seem unkind, or a sweeping statement, lacking the necessary facts to round up all members as bribe takers, six years isn't too short to mull over the happenings there, in particular, in the Senate.
This is so because, every so often, there's something distressingly puzzling, even terrifying to worry about how history will in the end judge the honourable members, and what lesson can the new Senate President learn from the ledger of errors and lack of incisive observations that have caused his predecessors their plum position.

Original_Search_Title: Don't Cause Disaffection Between the Nnamanis - Lawmaker Warns
Headline: Nigeria: Don't Cause Disaffection Between the Nnamanis - Lawmaker Warns
Body_Text:
Lagos — A member of Federal House of Representatives from Enugu State, Chief U.S.A. Igwesi has warned rumour mongers in the state to desist from causing unnecessary disaffection and carrying unsubstantiated stories on the relationship between the new Senate President, Chief Ken Nnamani and the Governor of Enugu State, Chimaroke Nnamani.
He gave the warning while fielding questions from journalists at National Assembly Complex, during which he described the relationship between Governor Nnamani and the new Senate President as cordial, intimate, brotherly and that capable of further uplifting the progress and peace presently being enjoyed by the people of the State.

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani And the 'Banana Peel' Phobia
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani And the 'Banana Peel' Phobia
Body_Text:
Lagos — Senator Ken Nnamani may well be a child of destiny. He did not exactly
set out to wear the crown fate has thrust on him now. But the crown fits
well. So it seems.
But uneasy lies the head that wears the crown. The crown comes with its
own variety of thorns. In the senate, financial temptation and power tussle,
more the first, has come to be the thorn that leads to the banana peel. As
an "ordinary" senator, Nnamani was away from the klieg light, at least most
of the time. But now as an exalted senator, a primus inter peers, Nnamani
has become a votary. Two weeks ago, he was sworn-in as the Senate
President. The fifth in a-little-less-than six years. The turn-over has been
veritably high. Nothing accounts for this high turn-over except the now
famous allegory of Banana Peel, a quaint metaphor stamped on the lexicon of
the legislature, nay the polity, by the late erudite Dr Chuba Okadigbo,
himself a former senate president, and a victim of the slippery banana
peel. Ever since his fall, the fear of the banana peel has become the
beginning of wisdom among the senate presidents. But none has been able to
have requisite wisdom to either side-step the banana peel or avoid it
altogether.

Original_Search_Title: 'I'm Determined to Break the Banana Peel Jinx' - Senate President
Headline: Nigeria: 'I'm Determined to Break the Banana Peel Jinx'
Body_Text:
Lagos —  Last Thursday, it was Herculean getting the Senate President, Senator
Ken Nnamani to sit down for a thirty-minute interview. It is not that Onwa
(the Moon), as he is popularly addressed by his colleagues, is press- shy,
