but the demands of his new office was beginning to take his toil. After two
courtesy visits first by, the management of Habib Bank and then the
Senators' Forum led by Senator Alex Kadiri, Nnamani allowed Kola
Ologbondiyan into his office for "fifteen minutes of my time." But the
interview ended one-hour after.
It's been a week plus since your colleagues elected you the President of
the Senate of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. How has it been?

Original_Search_Title: From Sweeper to Art Exhibitor
Headline: Uganda: From Sweeper to Art Exhibitor
Body_Text:
Kampala — There is a biblical proverb that goes: "The race is not only for the swift, wise or the strong, but time and chance happens to them all".
Matthias Tusiime seems to fit in quite well considering his latest exploit. Having worked as a casual worker at the Margaret Trowell School of Industrial and Fine Art, Makerere University, for more than half a decade, he only recently pulled a very extraordinary stunt when he unveiled a repertoire of artworks that were consequently exhibited at the Makerere University gallery.

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani: Banana Peel! I'll Break the Jinx
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani: Banana Peel! I'll Break the Jinx
Body_Text:
Abuja — Senate President Ken Nnamani yesterday assured that he would break the "banana peel" that had slipped the tenure of his predecessor to an abrupt end as he (Nnamani) and his colleagues are determined to restore the glory of the Senate.
Nnamani, who spoke while receiving members of the Senators Forum led by its Chairman, Senator Alex Kadiri, urged the members of the forum not to wait till when there is a "banana peel" before advising his office.

Original_Search_Title: No More Govt 'Help' for Senators - Nnamani
Headline: Nigeria: No More Govt 'Help' for Senators - Nnamani
Body_Text:
Abuja — NEnugu government lays red-carpet for him today
SENATORS can no longer solicit assistance from government agencies in the course of their oversight functions. This is under fresh guidelines to check corruption rolled out yesterday by the new Senate President, Ken Nnamani who receives a red-carpet treatment in his home state today on the occasion of his first official visit.

Original_Search_Title: Chimaroke, Ken Nnamani's Relationship Cordial - Lawmaker
Headline: Nigeria: Chimaroke, Ken Nnamani's Relationship Cordial - Lawmaker
Body_Text:
Abuja — The lawmaker representing Nkanu Federal Constituency of Enugu State, Hon Uchenna Igwesi has debunked allegation of friction between the new Senate President Senator Ken Nnamani and the Enugu State Governor Dr. Chimaroke Nnamani.
The lawmaker warned those behind the "rumour" to desist from doing so, insisting that the relationship between the Senate president and the Governor was "Cordial, intimate, brotherly and that capable of uplifting the progress and peace presently being enjoyed by the people of the state".

Original_Search_Title: Movement Advised to Accept Mistakes
Headline: Uganda: Movement Advised to Accept Mistakes
Body_Text:
Bushenyi — The former army commander, Maj. Gen. Mugisha Muntu, has said one of the
major problems with the Movement government, is that it does not want to
accept its mistakes.
He said Col. Dr Kizza Besigye wrote a paper criticising the government
and a 'war' was opened against him instead of inviting him to discuss the
issues he had raised.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Halt Debate to Discuss Insecurity
Headline: Kenya: MPs Halt Debate to Discuss Insecurity
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Business of the House was yesterday put aside for an hour to discuss the
escalating insecurity.
The extraordinary step was taken after Embakasi legislator David Mwenje
sought an adjournment motion, saying the country was under siege as thugs
continued to take charge.

Original_Search_Title: Let's Enhance Pressure Against Unfair Trade
Headline: Zambia: Let's Enhance Pressure Against Unfair Trade
Body_Text:
Lusaka — WITH warmth and fraternity we welcome Bishop of Wales and Bath Reverend
Peter Price to our country and the ideas he stands for.
His call on poor countries to enhance pressure in calling for fair trade
among countries in the world deserves the attention of all.

Original_Search_Title: The Insider: So Rotten, a Banana Republic Would Blush
Headline: Zimbabwe: The Insider: So Rotten, a Banana Republic Would Blush
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — MASSIVE vote-rigging, corruption that would "disgrace a banana
republic", and a postal voting system that was "wide open to fraud".
Sounds like British Prime Minister Tony Blair's assessment of the recent
Zimbabwean election, doesn't it? But, in fact, these are the words used by a
UK judge, Richard Mawrey, last week to describe the shenanigans perpetrated
by six of Blair's Labour candidates in last year's local council elections,
with the implicit consent of their party.

Original_Search_Title: Farewell to the People's Princess
Headline: Uganda: Farewell to the People's Princess
Body_Text:
Kampala — SHE was Lady Irene Drucilla Namaganda's incarnate. Princess Alice
Namaganda Zalwango, who recently passed away, shared a lot in common with
her grandmother, the mother of Sir Edward Muteesa II, after whom she was
named.
Not so much in looks as in character; both were strong willed. Zalwango,
44, died on March 23, at the East Pretoria Hospital in South Africa, where
she was being treated for breast cancer for two months. Grandmother and
granddaughter succumbed to the same disease.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Speaks Out On Obote
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Speaks Out On Obote
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has said the Zambia-based Uganda People's
Congress (UPC) leader, Dr. Milton Obote, will answer for the crimes against
humanity that were committed in his time when he returns.
"I now hear that Obote wants to return. They should tell him that he
must answer for the crimes that were committed by his government. They
murdered people in a deliberate way," Museveni said. He was addressing
thousands of people who braved the scorching sun and dust at Kikunyu,
Kikandwa, 54km on the Kampala-Hoima road, during Mityana North MP Godfrey
Kiwanda's wedding reception on Saturday.

Original_Search_Title: Much Ado About Hand-Cuffs
Headline: Nigeria: Much Ado About Hand-Cuffs
Body_Text:
Lagos — I am tempted, for the first time in a very long time, to agree with those who believe that social critic and lagos lawyer Chief Gani Fawehinmi plays to the gallery. But I would rather wave the temptation aside given the respect I have for the legal icon who has over time proven to be a man of deep convictions. But the hasty press statement in which he carpeted Economic and Financial Crime Commission (EFCC) for bringing the erstwhile Inspector-General Mr Tafa Balogun to court in hand-cuffs did not go down well with some of us.
Except that the Ondo high chief has made it abundantly clear that he would never be caught in court as a defence counsel for people charged with systemic corruption, one would have been tempted to conclude that Gani wanted to be hired to lead Balogun's team of lawyers. The former police boss can evidently foot the bill. This is not taking anything away from Gani who has been among the most consistent crusaders for social justice in the country.

Original_Search_Title: Artists Review Art Bill
Headline: Namibia: Artists Review Art Bill
Body_Text:
Windhoek — A SUGGESTION that the only formally registered artists' union in the country be assured and afforded automatic representation in the to-be-formed Arts Council was last Friday unanimously accepted by invited artists who took part in a discussion forum.
A meeting between some 20 invited artists from various creative spheres and the Oruuano Artists' Union was as a matter of urgency convened at the Katutura Community Arts Centre.

Original_Search_Title: Genocide Survivor's Association President Dismisses Ruseabagina's Actions
Headline: Rwanda: Ibuka Dismisses
Body_Text:
Kigali — The genocide survivor's association (IBUKA) president Francois Xavier Ngarambe has dismissed heroic claims by Paul Rusesabagina, saying the latter just wanted to make money out of the 1994 mayhem.
Ngarambe made these remarks during a press conference at Nyarugenge District headquarters on the eve of the Eleventh commemoration of the genocide.

Original_Search_Title: Mbarara Selected for Banana Project
Headline: Uganda: Mbarara Selected for Banana Project
Body_Text:
Mbarara — The district has been selected for a pilot study code-named Matooke Market Development Project.
Mbarara is the leading producer of bananas (matooke) in the country.

Original_Search_Title: Focus On Economy, Mend International Relations
Headline: Zimbabwe: Focus On Economy, Mend International Relations
Body_Text:
Harare — ZIMBABWE'S controversial parliamentary elections have come and gone. And so is the madness and controversy associated with election time where everything else is done for political expedience.
The voice of reason and influence of realities are usually ignored as the magic influence of populist phraseology takes centre stage.

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani Sworn-in, Unfolds Agenda
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani Sworn-in, Unfolds Agenda
Body_Text:
Lagos — Wabara apologises - Anenih: No condition is permanent
New Senate President, Chief Ken Nnamani, yesterday visited Aso Rock Villa shortly after he was sworn-in and said his priority was "to stabilise the Senate and reclaim our image in the eyes of the public."

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani, Beware of Senate's Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani, Beware of Senate's Banana Peel
Body_Text:
Lagos — In an unusual acknowledgment of some virtues in the Obasanjo administration, a colleague, Mallam Ujudud Shariff, praised the President's efforts at social reforms in a piece entitled "The Anti-Corruption Hurricane" in yesterday's edition of the Daily Trust. As a journalist and a member of the editorial board of the newspaper, Shariff has written scathing, but informed criticisms of President Olusegun Obasanjo and his administration.
But this time round the writer paused to "doff" his hat for the President. His words : "Reflecting on the successive events that occurred last week in Nigeria especially on the anti-corruption hurricane blowing across Abuja in particular and the nation in general, one gets the strong feeling that at long last the battle against corruption has started". Sharif was, of course, referring to the sacking of two ministers within a month, the arraignment of the former Inspector-General of Police, Mr. Tafa Balogun as well as the Bribe-for Budget allegation that has caused Senator Adolphus Wabara his much-cherished job as the Senate President.

Original_Search_Title: Wabara Resigns, Begs Senators
Headline: Nigeria: Wabara Resigns, Begs Senators
Body_Text:
Abuja —  Nnamani now Senate President
CHIEF Adolphus Wabara yesterday resigned as president of the Senate just as he begged Nigerians especially senators, who he had wronged to forgive him.

Original_Search_Title: Making Bark Cloth for 75 Years
Headline: Uganda: Making Bark Cloth for 75 Years
Body_Text:
It is a Friday evening, and Mzee Eriyazali Kaganda is just from the
forest, clad in a rather old tattered shirt and a pair of equally old
shorts, a panga, spear and a hunting net in hand. He walks with such
agility, something that makes one doubt the fact that he is old.
"Mufunye mukisa okunsingana, kuba mbadde manyi emissu jijja kawungezi
nnyo" (which loosely translated means, "You are just lucky to find me, I
thought the edible rats were to appear late in the evening) says the half
smiling old man.

Original_Search_Title: Is Museveni Falling Out With His Friends in the West?
Headline: Uganda: Is Museveni Falling Out With His Friends in the West?
Body_Text:
For the first time since Yoweri Museveni took control of this country in
1986, the opposition staged a peaceful demonstration in the heart of the
city without hindrance.
Last week's political protest against what they regarded "a national
crisis which Museveni and his government have plunged the country into" has
inadvertently symbolised Uganda's new political direction.

Original_Search_Title: Who is the Pharaoh in APC?
Headline: Sierra Leone: Something to Think About: Who is the Pharaoh in APC?
Body_Text:
Standing on Lamina Sankoh Street in the early hours of last Tuesday
morning waiting to receive a friend traveling from Lungi to Freetown with
the early bird ferry. I saw a very short object that looks like a human
figure dressed in a lawyer's suit with a white color peeping out from the
front rear of his shirt.
What is this object that looks like a human figure, so short as if
suffering from lack of pituitrine that has given him a stunted growth?

Original_Search_Title: Fahnbulleh Dissects Present Political Trends
Headline: Liberia: Fahnbulleh Dissects Present Political Trends
Body_Text:
- Says People Must Be Involved In Statecraft
Dr. Henry Boima Fahnbulleh is said to be a respected Liberian political
figure who has won the admiration of contemporarily revolutionary and
progressive Liberians. Last week, he viewed and dissected the present
political drama at stake in an interview with newsmen and blamed it on the
concept most Liberian politicians have adopted.

Original_Search_Title: Schools Lack Facilities for Disabled
Headline: Uganda: Schools Lack Facilities for Disabled
Body_Text:
Banana trees, jackfruit trees and unfinished classroom blocks
characterise Kitemu Integrated Primary School. Located in the remote areas of Mpigi, Kitemu Primary School is no ordinary school. It caters
for children with several learning disabilities. Mr Steven Serwanga, the
headmaster of the school says: "we have tried to address disability by
teaching the disabled together with other students so that they can feel
accepted in society."
In the Primary Seven class, we are ushered in with a standing ovation
from the rest of the students. Godfrey Wamala struggles to get on his feet,
the metal callipers on his legs, for rehabilitation, seem too heavy. He
finally gets up with a smile, which leaves you wondering if disability is
really a hindrance.

Original_Search_Title: Past Senate Presidents: How They Left Office
Headline: Nigeria: Past Senate Presidents: How They Left Office
Body_Text:
Lagos — At the beginning of the present democratic dispensation (Fourth Republic), a need arose for the ruling Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) as was in the case with the National Party of Nigeria (NPN) in 1979, to come up with an acceptable zoning formula for the country. The party therefore decided to zone its elective positions between the six geo-political zones.
In the zoning formula that was to emerge, the South-west took the presidency, the heirarchy of the party zoned the the No.2 (Vice President) slot to the North-east and Senate Presidency to the South-east; Speaker to the North-west, Deputy Speaker to the South-south zone.

Original_Search_Title: Obusinga Inquiry Starts
Headline: Uganda: Obusinga Inquiry Starts
Body_Text:
Kasese — The committee appointed by the President to investigate the
justification of the Obusinga bwa Rwenzururu early this year, will start
public hearings in Kasese and Bundibugyo districts on Monday, Bukonzo East
MP, Mr Kithende Kalibogha, has said.
He was addressing worshippers at Kitswamba SDA Church in Kitswamba
sub-county in Kasese on Saturday. Kalibogha is a member of the committee
headed the Second Deputy Premier Mr Henry Kajura.

Original_Search_Title: Police Disperse Anti-Third Term Demo
Headline: Uganda: Police Disperse Anti-Third Term Demo
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE anti-riot Police violently broke up an anti-third term demonstration
yesterday.
The demonstration, dubbed 'the one-million march' was staged by a dozen
people.

Original_Search_Title: Former Kampala Mayor, MP Arrested During Anti-Third-Term Protest
Headline: Uganda: Sebaggala, Pulkol Held Over Protest
Body_Text:
Kampala — 17 arrested over anti-3rd term demo
The police yesterday bust the second anti-third term demonstration and
arrested 17 protestors. Five of its organisers were also held for six
hours.

Original_Search_Title: Jacobson Feels Odd But is Happy to Settle Here
Headline: Uganda: Jacobson Feels Odd But is Happy to Settle Here
Body_Text:
I rushed into the British Council premises at Rwenzori Courts panting, expecting to find Jeremy Jacobson pacing to and fro, impatiently looking at his watch.
Despite arriving on time, the line-up of formalities before I could gain entry to the director's office meant that I was late for the interview.

Original_Search_Title: Wondrous Prophesy of Dry Banana Leaves
Headline: Uganda: Wondrous Prophesy of Dry Banana Leaves
Body_Text:
Kampala — Once upon a time in the central part of this goodly land of ours if it was the turn of a particular wife to host her husband-of-many for the night, the wife would say, "Tonight it is my kisanja."
The meaning was that the dry banana leaves (essanja) that constituted her bed were going to have their share in accommodating the rotating husband.

Original_Search_Title: Wabara: Prelude to a Fall
Headline: Nigeria: Wabara: Prelude to a Fall
Body_Text:
Lagos — Prof. Fabian Osuji, the sacked Minister of Education headed for a
Federal High Court in Abuja to stop the Federal Government from prosecuting
him over the N55 million bribery saga that rocked the country last
week.
Osuji has claimed his dismissal was political. But unlike the executive
which has sacrificed its own principal actor, the Senate is yet to give a
clue as to where the indictment hanging on its leadership may end, even
though investigations have commenced in earnest. In this report, Olawale
Olaleye examines some of the undercurrent presently playing out in the
Senate and wonders whether or not the Senate will also sacrifice Wabara like
the executive did Osuji

Original_Search_Title: Govt is Right On Parties
Headline: Uganda: Govt is Right On Parties
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Government's directive to all district officials to allow political
parties to freely operate is timely and a step in the right direction. It is
crucial that the Government takes measures to create a political climate
that will allow all political actors and forces to operate freely and
compete for power if the country is to establish a genuinely multiparty
system.
The country is undergoing important political changes. The Government is
taking steps to move the country from the Movement system to multiparty
politics. The Constitutional (Amendment) Bill that, among other things,
seeks to provide for the forthcoming elections to be held under a multiparty
political system is before Parliament. So far, over 15 political parties,
including the National Resistance Movement (NRM) and the Uganda People's
Congress (UPC) have been duly registered and issued with certificates of
registration. They are entities legally recognised. Of all the old parties,
only the Democratic Party, ironically, the country's oldest party, is
unregistered, largely because of reckless internal power struggles. The
Thursday opposition demonstration in the city against the third term also
demonstrated that the Government is gradually opening up the political
space. Obviously, it was unfair that the Police only gave the demo a nod at
the eleventh hour and even dictated the time it should be held, yet there
were no such conditionality for a previous demo organised by the pro-kisanja
activists.

Original_Search_Title: Barclays Donates
Headline: Uganda: Barclays Donates
Body_Text:
Kampala — Barclays Bank donated Easter goods worth Shs500,000 to needy children of Reachout Village Ministries in Munyonyo, a Kampala suburb.
"When the needy receive such donations, they also feel good and not discriminated especially during holiday cerebrations," the Head of Retail Banking Barclays Bank, Ms Vivian Igundura, said on Saturday.

Original_Search_Title: This is Where Wabara Stands(1)
Headline: Nigeria: This is Where Wabara Stands(1)
Body_Text:
Lagos — THOSE who follow events in Nigeria will never cease to be amazed at why every story that comes forth from the National Assembly, the lawmaking body, in particular, the Senate often spew forth in a sordid, tawdry form, tedious and embarrassing. As if scandal has become its second nature, the present one is like a novel that just rolled off the printers, full of juicy chapters, with bizarre, sleazy characters.
That, unfortunately, has become the curious state of affairs in our politics and, indeed, it's the poisonous reality inside everything Nigeria and what our politicians have made of politics in the land. Within and outside the country, government is seen as riddled with corruption. It is no surprise why the current edition of The Economist describes our National Assembly as a "habitually irresponsible house and one of the world's filthiest arena of politics."

Original_Search_Title: Pulkol Joins Sebaggala
Headline: Uganda: Pulkol Joins Sebaggala
Body_Text:
Kampala — FORMER External Security Organisation director general David Pulkol has joined Alhaj Nasser Sebaggala to form a new pressure group called Forces for Change to force President Yoweri Museveni out of office.
Yesterday, the pressure group that included MPs Latif Ssebaggala (Kawempe North), Michael Mabikke (Makindye East), said they will hold a peaceful demonstration code-named 'Operation Knock-Out Kisanja' on March 31 in Kampala "in support of constitutional rights and in solidarity with the international community action calling on the Government of Uganda to respect presidential term limits, the rule of law and constitutionalism."

Original_Search_Title: Marchers Back Bob Geldof
Headline: Uganda: Marchers Back Bob Geldof
Body_Text:
Kampala — HUNDREDS of anti-third term campaigners demonstrated yesterday in
support of Irish star Bob Geldof's call to President Museveni not to seek
another term in office.
They marched for 50 minutes to the British High Commission on Parliament
Avenue where they handed over a memorandum to the chief security officer to
give to the High Commissioner, before going to Parliament, where they
petitioned the speaker, Edward Ssekandi.

Original_Search_Title: Parties Free to Operate
Headline: Uganda: Parties Free to Operate
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Government has directed district officials to let registered
political parties operate normally and warned of punitive measures against
those who ignored the directive.
Information minister Dr. James Nsaba Buturo told journalists at the
weekly press briefing that the directive was intended to ensure that the
time leading to next year's general elections was peaceful.

Original_Search_Title: Calm As Anti-Third Term Supporters Demonstrate
Headline: Uganda: Calm As Anti-Third Term Supporters Demonstrate
Body_Text:
Kampala — For the first time opposition groups yesterday held a peaceful
demonstration against giving President Yoweri Museveni a third term.
Hundreds of demonstrators, mainly youth of the six major opposition
groups, marched from the Constitution Square through the city streets to the
British High Commission on Parliament Avenue.

Original_Search_Title: Kayunga to Buy Shs2m Ssanja for Museveni's Visit
Headline: Uganda: Kayunga to Buy Shs2m Ssanja for Museveni's Visit
Body_Text:
Kayunga — Movement supporters in the district have earmarked Shs2m to purchase
essanja (dry banana leaves).
The leaves will be used to welcome President Yoweri Museveni who is
expected in the area late this month.

Original_Search_Title: Corruption: No Inflation in the Senate
Headline: Nigeria: Corruption: No Inflation in the Senate
Body_Text:
Throughout the current Senate corruption saga, if there is any useful
observation to make about the upper chamber of the National Assembly, it is
that the manager of its internal economy deserves our congratulations for
managing it so well.
So well indeed that in more than two years of working and transacting
business, there has virtually been no inflation in Senatorial prices. When
Mallam Nasir el-Rufa'i was being confirmed as minister of the Federal
Capital Territory in 2003, for instance, the Senators demanded N54 million
from him. If today they demanded N55 million from former education minister,
Professor Fabian Osuji, they were only being magnanimous on two
counts—charging negligible inflation and not taking into account the
net present value of a ministerial appointment. Which is just as well.

Original_Search_Title: The Next Wave of Pan-Africanism
Headline: Africa: Politics: The Next Wave of Pan-Africanism
Body_Text:
Kingston — For Jamaican dub poet and Rastafarian Mutabaruka, the question of
whether Pan-Africanism is still relevant is almost incomprehensible, so
obvious is the answer to him.
"It must be relevant because most of the Jamaican population is of
African stock, and we have never been able to forge that link between the
motherland and the Caribbean," he told IPS. "It's very important to decide
on our next step (to develop) a South-South relationship because we've
always been looking to the North."

Original_Search_Title: The Pains a `Rebel Sympathiser' Went Through
Headline: Rwanda: The Pains a `Rebel Sympathiser' Went Through
Body_Text:
Arusha — Leonard Tulimukaga was last week acquitted
of participating in the genocide by the Gacaca courts of his
neighbourhood.
But in 1994, it had been a different story. When his eldest son and nephew
had joined the rebel Rwandese Patriotic Front (RPF), he had been accused
of being their accomplice.

Original_Search_Title: Gulu Kids Play Their Pain, Sorrow Away
Headline: Uganda: Gulu Kids Play Their Pain, Sorrow Away
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE first thing that catches your eye is the army tank parked near a
mango tree at the grass-thatched UPDF detachment, with its barrel ready to
fire. To a stranger, seeing soldiers seated next to a huge gun can increase
the heartbeat. But the Internally Displaced People (IDP) at Unyama, six
kilometres along Gulu - Kitgum Road, are accustomed to such company.
The dilapidated buildings of Unyama National Teachers College (NTC)
campus, several clusters of tiny grass huts, and unkempt children in rags,
reflect how far this part of the world has borne the brunt of war. But the
community here is stoic and life goes on against all odds.

Original_Search_Title: Kisanja Fans Cause Chaos At Nateete
Headline: Uganda: Kisanja Fans Cause Chaos At Nateete
Body_Text:
Kampala — Violence erupted on Tuesday as police and members of Uganda Young Democrats (UYD) tried to disperse Movement supporters who turned up dressed in essanja for the Women's Day celebrations in Nateete.
The function was organised by Kampala City Council (KCC) at Natete ground.

Original_Search_Title: Clergy Urged to Preach Entrepreneur Skills
Headline: Uganda: Clergy Urged to Preach Entrepreneur Skills
Body_Text:
Kayunga — The Bishop of Mukono diocese, Eria Paul Luzinda, has urged the clergy to equip Christians with entrepreneur skills on top of spiritual development.
Luzinda was on Sunday addressing Christians of Ndeeba Arch deacon in Kayunga town where he confirmed 67 Christians. He said there was no way Christians could contribute to the development of the church when they are not economically empowered.

Original_Search_Title: The Law Breakers...
Headline: Nigeria: The Law Breakers...
Body_Text:
Lagos — Shortly after the controversy that greeted the allegation by Mallam Nasir el-Rufai that some Senators demanded bribe from him to facilitate his clearance as Minister, the embattled Senate President, Senator Adolphus Wabara, was in Lagos to meet with some selected editors at the Lagos Airport Hotel. It was to tell his own side of the el-Rufai allegation for which there was no proof but which most Nigerians believed as credible because of the notorious reputation of the Abuja law makers.
That day, Wabara told his tale and we (editors) were ready to give him the benefit of the doubt, especially when he said he has an only child and one thing he would never do was to mortgage the future of the boy by bringing his name into disrepute. It was a rather emotional Wabara that we saw that day and I, in my usual sentimental nature, felt for him; believing a man who has just one child quite naturally has fewer needs and is not likely going to be greedy with money. I was wrong. But that was not even the highlight of that day. In the course of the interaction, which was light hearted, one of the senators who came with Wabara said rather exuberantly: "El-Rufai said God is his witness. But you people should know we in the Senate don't have the power to summon God to come and testify...and it's like the God of nowadays does not have the capacity to act..."

Original_Search_Title: Kisanja Fans, UYD Clash At Women's Fete
Headline: Uganda: Kisanja Fans, UYD Clash At Women's Fete
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Kampala district women's Day celebrations yesterday turned rowdy
when Movement supporters wearing dry banana leaves (essanja) engaged in a
fist fight with the Uganda Young Democrats (UYD).
UYD and other youth from Nateete, a Kampala suburb, barred about 50
Movementists in yellow T-shirts and caps with portraits of President
Museveni from a tent.

Original_Search_Title: Only Donors And a Pro-People's Parliament Will Save My Race
Headline: Uganda: Only Donors And a Pro-People's Parliament Will Save My Race
Body_Text:
I commend, Mzee Bidandi Ssali's candour in his recent interview, in
Mwenda Live. Were I a lecturer, I would make the reading of Oweyegha
Afunaduula's "Let's raise the threshold of the presidency... ." (The
Monitor, March 8), and Dr. M.K.Mulera's "Why is Museveni dancing after
Nkuuhe's song ends?" (Monitor March 14), compulsory! Thus in this solemn
article I shall be similarly candid.
I have every right - and obligation to speak all subjects, affecting
fellow Ugandans, my descendants and me. I have been in the service of the
people these 56 years. Having been a clerk in the Ankole Kingdom government
for a few months I took up the job of Co-operative Assistant in the then
newly established Co-operative Department in April 1949; where, insisting on
the staff rights, my employers usually docketed me "ring leader," but
generously considered me talented enough to stay.

Original_Search_Title: It's Right Between the Lines: Museveni Surely Going Nowhere
Headline: Uganda: It's Right Between the Lines: Museveni Surely Going Nowhere
Body_Text:
For the second time in as many weeks, President Yoweri Museveni has come
close to saying he will be a candidate in the March 2006 Presidential
elections.
During national celebrations marking the International Women's Day at
Mpigi Police Grounds, Museveni openly defended the move to lift Presidential
term limits, saying that the third term (kisanja) crusade was intended to
safeguard the country's achievements under the Movement from the
opposition.

Original_Search_Title: 'Western Cultures Promoting HIV/Aids' - Museveni
Headline: Uganda: 'Western Cultures Promoting HIV/Aids'
Body_Text:
Munyonyo — President Yoweri Museveni on Monday blamed some western cultures for
partly being responsible for the HIV/Aids spread in Uganda.
"The traditional African family code has been eroded by imitation of the
lifestyles from the western world," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Students Storm UK Embassy
Headline: Uganda: Students Storm UK Embassy
Body_Text:
Kampala — STUDENTS from universities and other tertiary institutions yesterday
held a demonstration at the British High Commission and Parliament against
rock star Bob Geldof, for saying President Museveni should not seek another
term.
The demonstrators from Makerere, Nkumba and Mukono universities, Kibuli
and Shimoni teachers training colleges, who called themselves concerned
patriots, were wearing essanja (banana leaves), the symbol of a third term
for Museveni.

Original_Search_Title: Protestors Condemn Britain, Bob Geldof
Headline: Uganda: Protestors Condemn Britain, Bob Geldof
Body_Text:
Kampala — Demonstrators protesting to what they called "unfriendly remarks against
the sovereignty and independence of Uganda" yesterday stormed the British
High Commission and the Parliament in Kampala.
The crowd of several hundreds, calling themselves Concerned Patriots,
condemned remarks by a British minister who recently questioned Uganda's
commitment to democracy and Irish rock star and aid campaigner Bob Geldof
who accused President Museveni of wanting to rule for life.

Original_Search_Title: Gacaca Sentences Former Militia Member, Acquits Beer Trader
Headline: Rwanda: Gacaca Sentences Former Militia Member, Acquits Beer Trader
Body_Text:
Arusha — Dressed in their Sunday best, over which they wore flowing robes in
national colours, the nine-member Gacaca court of Rugenge Secteur in Kigali
City take their seats.
An excited crowd jostle to get their place under the shade of a
makeshift shelter. The "Saturday trials" can resume.

Original_Search_Title: All Set for Schools Drama Fete
Headline: Kenya: All Set for Schools Drama Fete
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The national schools and colleges drama festivals begin in Kakamega tomorrow.
The event, which brings together more than 5,000 performers and producers, is sponsored by the Nation Media Group. It will be held at Kakamega High School until March 31.

Original_Search_Title: Stop Arrests, Intimidation
Headline: Uganda: Stop Arrests, Intimidation
Body_Text:
In the space of only four days, security personnel have arrested two
Forum for Democratic Change (FDC) supporters ostensibly in connection with
the opposition party's t-shirts.
We condemn these actions because they undermine the spirit of opening up
the political space. Last Wednesday, Internal Security Organisation
operatives arrested an FDC coordinator in Rukunguri over wearing a party
t-shirt. The arresting authorities said it was a rebel uniform.

Original_Search_Title: Union Gets Big Bucks for Art Projects
Headline: Namibia: Union Gets Big Bucks for Art Projects
Body_Text:
Windhoek — A GRANT of N$879 610 was last week handed over to the Oruuano Artists
Union as part of a long-term agreement since 2000 with the Finnish Embassy
in Windhoek.
"The artists union has since its inception seven years ago in 1998
worked tirelessly and continuously towards its main goals of protecting the
rights and works of all Namibian artists, and developing and promoting
artists in all the different disciplines and giving them a sense of identity
in their profession," said Charge de Affaires, Seija Kinni, when she
presented a cheque and signed a long-term agreement with the union at its
small and modest office.

Original_Search_Title: How Much Chewing Gum Will Save a Continent?
Headline: Africa: How Much Chewing Gum Will Save a Continent?
Body_Text:
London — All that is needed from the rich to save Africa is the price of "half a
stick of chewing gum a day from everyone," rock star and now star Africa
activist Bob Geldof declared Friday.
That, he figured, would add up to the 25 billion dollars a year that the
Commission for Africa has recommended by way of extra aid to Africa until
2010. After that, the Commission appointed by the British government
recommends a further 25 billion dollars a year extra aid. Which would mean
the equivalent of a full stick of chewing gum a day, going by the new
'Bob-onomics' for Africa.

Original_Search_Title: Gorilla Fund Supports Youth
Headline: Rwanda: Gorilla Fund Supports Youth
Body_Text:
Ruhengeri — Dianah Fossey Gorilla Fund is supporting 28 youth artists
from different districts of Ruhengeri province.The gorilla fund supports
youth income generating activities such as painting, designing of sign-post,
among others.
Others benefiting include teachers who help the youth to acquire life
skills.

Original_Search_Title: Kibwetere Massacre, the Forgotten Event
Headline: Uganda: Kibwetere Massacre, the Forgotten Event
Body_Text:
It is now five years since Joseph Kibwetere, a self-styled prophet and
bishop, burnt up more than 1,000 Ugandans in one of the world's worst
massacres.
Francis Niwagaba visited Kanungu to find out the developments if any : -

Original_Search_Title: Kenyan Regulator Saga Rumbles On
Headline: Kenyan Regulator Saga Rumbles On
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — The Kenyan communications minister, Raphael Tuju, has seconded Dr James
Kulubi - currently responsible for government ICT policy - to replace the
director-general of the Communications Commission of Kenya (CCK), Sammy
Kirui, who has been sent on indefinite leave.
This follows last week's shock decision by the Kenyan government to
dissolve the CCK, supposedly due to investigations into improprieties in the
selection of the country's second national operator.

Original_Search_Title: Essanja Supplier Wants Pay
Headline: Uganda: Essanja Supplier Wants Pay
Body_Text:
Mbale — A man who supplied essanja (dry banana leaves) to Movement supporters
during President Yoweri Museveni's visit to Mbale has not been paid.
Mr Hussein Wazemba, of Bukonde, on Friday alleged that he was contacted
by Alhajji Wamboga, a driver to Mbale LC5 chairman, to supply them with 20
bundles of essanja at Shs205, 500.

Original_Search_Title: Economy Doomed, Warns Labour Body
Headline: Zimbabwe: Economy Doomed, Warns Labour Body
Body_Text:
ZIMBABWE'S much vaunted economic turnaround programme is doomed if the
government fails to complement the drive demonstrated by the central bank,
the Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) has said.
In an audit of the economic turnaround programme being spearheaded by
Reserve Bank Governor, Gideon Gono, the ZCTU said the RBZ alone would not
arrest the current economic hemorrhage.

Original_Search_Title: OTT Bows Out - Bye Bye!
Headline: Zimbabwe: OTT Bows Out - Bye Bye!
Body_Text:
NOTHING lasts forever, but now Over The Top has gone over the top and
reached the end of the line. An unceremonious and hurried departure from the
land of my ancestors sees OTT shivering in the cold of Harare North, Unit
K.
Some of you may have heard of our flight from the forces of Zany "law
and order." Facing imminent arrest, three upstanding Zimbabwean hacks, doing
nothing more than plying our honest craft, spent Valentine's Day in the
company of some very sarcastic policemen. They vowed we were off to
jail. Four raids in two days, illegal searches and endless threats persuaded
us that it would be safer elsewhere. So did our lawyer.

Original_Search_Title: Talking Point: Museveni's Enemies Must Stand Up And Be Counted
Headline: Uganda: Talking Point: Museveni's Enemies Must Stand Up And Be Counted
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It seems almost inevitable that President Museveni will rule Uganda for than 20 years.
Mr Museveni has reneged on an explicit promise contained in his 2001 election manifesto where he asked voters to support him for a second five-year term which would be his very last as President.

Original_Search_Title: Sir Apollo Kaggwa's Crumbling
Headline: Uganda: Sir Apollo Kaggwa's Crumbling
Body_Text:
Kampala — SIR Apollo Kaggwa's country house is coming up with age.
Perched up on a hill in Manyangwa, Gayaza (21 km from Kampala), once the pride of his family and a status symbol in the late 1880s, the castle has lost its glamour.

Original_Search_Title: 'Ebbinu Makeke' At Nakivubo Stadium
Headline: Uganda: 'Ebbinu Makeke' At Nakivubo Stadium
Body_Text:
Kampala — GRAND Celebration of the year, Ebbinu Makeke it was dubbed - and how it
attracted a mammoth crowd. People started trickling in as early as 8:00am
and when the first artiste, Abdu Murasi, took to the stage at 3:00pm, the
stadium brimmed with seas of sweaty but enthusiastic fans who never seemed
to mind sweltering heat conditions.
Eagles Production Band provided back up act to many artistes who gave
live performances. Many, like Mesach Ssemakula, Prince Paul Job Kafeero,
Sheila Nvanungi, Bobi wine and Julian Kanyomozi, Jose Chameleone, Ragga Dee,
Irene Namatovu, Catherine Kusasira and Butcherman of Fire Base Crew, put up
a spirited performance attracting a thunderous applause from satisfied
fans.

Original_Search_Title: Wrong to Exclude 30 FDC Women
Headline: Uganda: Wrong to Exclude 30 FDC Women
Body_Text:
Kampala — ON Tuesday a women's group from the Forum for Democratic Change (FDC)
was blocked from attending the rally for International Women's Day
celebrations despite it being a national event.
It was a huge rally and the 30 women would probably have passed
unnoticed in the crowd. But the Presidential Guard did not allow them to
pass the security checkpoint because they objected to the group wearing FDC
teeshirts and carrying an FDC flag.

Original_Search_Title: FDC Women Blocked From Mpigi Celebrations
Headline: Uganda: FDC Women Blocked From Mpigi Celebrations
Body_Text:
Kampala — Forum for Democratic Change (FDC) women were yesterday blocked from
participating in national Women's Day celebrations in Mpigi, reports Hamis
Kaheru.
Bugabula South MP Salaamu Musumba said the Presidential Guard Brigade
(PGB) intercepted her group of about 30 women as they tried to go through
the metal detector at the security check-point.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Defends Ekisanja
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Defends Ekisanja
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Museveni yesterday said the political crusade which he
described as 'kisanja' was intended to safeguard the achievements of the
Movement from the opposition.
Museveni, speaking at the International Women's Day national
celebrations at the Mpigi Police Grounds, compared the opposition to kites
waiting to prey on the Movement's chicks.

Original_Search_Title: Police Block FDC At Women's Rally
Headline: Uganda: Police Block FDC At Women's Rally
Body_Text:
Mpigi — Women supporters of the opposition Forum for Democratic Change (FDC)
were yesterday blocked from attending the national celebrations marking the
International Women's Day at Mpigi Police District headquarters.
President Yoweri Museveni presided over the celebrations.

Original_Search_Title: Bushenyi Gets Alternative Energy Saving Stoves
Headline: Uganda: Bushenyi Gets Alternative Energy Saving Stoves
Body_Text:
Bushenyi — The district is the first to benefit from a new energy saving scheme
aimed at curbing high fuel consumption.
The scheme, on a pilot project, is under the ministry of Energy and
Mineral Development, with the support of the Germany Development Cooperation
(GTZ).

Original_Search_Title: The Developmentalists
Headline: South Africa: The Developmentalists
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — FOR anyone who may not have noticed, we now live in what the president
and his ministers call a "developmental state". It's a sort of mixed-economy
hybrid anywhere between "leading western industrial" and "banana republic"
in which the state takes upon itself the right (or obligation) to intervene
in the economy to fix anything it feels isn't working
properly. Developmentalism is an ideology all of its own.
A thoughtful definition comes from Thandika Mkandawire, a senior United
Nations economist, who says developmental states are defined traditionally
as ones that have a "mission" to achieve high rates of accumulation and
industrialisation and derive their legitimacy from their ability to do
so. Interestingly, he also says the state's elites have to "own" the mission
so that it becomes, as it were, the only game in town and that, lastly, the
definition of such a state emphasises capacity to implement policies and is
sufficiently autonomous from "myopic private interests" to be able to make
long-term strategy.

Original_Search_Title: Florence Kaboggoza, Mubende: Makes Waragi From Jackfruit
Headline: Uganda: Florence Kaboggoza, Mubende: Makes Waragi From Jackfruit
Body_Text:
Kampala — TO many people, jackfruits are for children but jackfruits delivered Namwandu Florence Kaboggoza, from poverty.
Kaboggoza in her late 40s has discovered that the juice from jackfruit pulp can be used to brew of waragi, a local potent gin now.

Original_Search_Title: Let Us Raise the Threshold of the Presidency of Uganda
Headline: Uganda: Let Us Raise the Threshold of the Presidency of Uganda
Body_Text:
Kampala — When in 1986 President Yoweri Museveni pronounced his famous "This is
not a mere change of guards. It is a fundamental change", there were loud
ululations.
Many approved of the capture of power through the barrel of the gun, and
many have gone on to approve that "Ensi egula mirambo" (A country costs
blood or dead bodies), which President Museveni has often conveniently
recited whenever it has been necessary to cultivate adequate silence and
fear among Ugandans, for example at election time.

Original_Search_Title: JM Was Last Seen Entering CID Car With Ben Gethi, Says Wife
Headline: Kenya: JM Was Last Seen Entering CID Car With Ben Gethi, Says Wife
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In our continuing serialisation of the verbatim proceedings of the Paliamentary Select Committee probing the March 1975 brutal murder of Nyandarua North MP JM Kariuki, today we carry the testimony of JM's wife, Terry Kariuki.
The committee members were Elijah Mwangale (chairman), Mark Mwithaga (acting chairman), Burudi Nabwera, Martin Shikuku, Grace Onyango, Charles Rubia, Jean Marie Seroney, K.S Mwavumo, Hon Kitonga, Hon Muriuki and Hon Gachago among others.

Original_Search_Title: Arts & Culture: The Joyous Trap
Headline: Uganda: Arts & Culture: The Joyous Trap
Body_Text:
Nairobi —  Dr David Kaiza goes to a breathtakingly executed, playful yet sensual
performance by two danseuses from Cap Verde
EVEN BY THE USUALLY HIGH standards of Alliance Francaise, the dance
performance, Duas Sem Tres, by the Cape Verde group, Raiz Di Polon, which
has wowed audiences around the world, provided a brilliant evening at the
National Theatre in Kampala on February 19.

Original_Search_Title: My Husband Holds Me Back - Edem
Headline: Nigeria: My Husband Holds Me Back - Edem
Body_Text:
Lagos — At this era the sacred institution of marriage is treated with levity by most youngsters, the 8th All Africa Games Abuja 2003 gold medallist, Emen Edem, has said that her deep respect for the age old rites is the major reason why she will not dump Nigeria.
Married to her coach Isaac Osagie, the dark complexioned athlete who said retiring from the tracks is too far away from her mind until she puts her spikes at the Olympics, told Sportschampion recently in Lagos that she has a lot of reasons to bid bye to Nigeria but for her marital vow she will go ahead to compete for fatherland.

Original_Search_Title: Follow Your Consciences in the Fight for Democracy
Headline: Uganda: Follow Your Consciences in the Fight for Democracy
Body_Text:
Toronto — Dear Tingasiga:
Few subjects fascinate me as much as the French Revolution of 1789. I was introduced to the subject by Monsieur Gillard, my history teacher at King's College, Budo.

Original_Search_Title: Bring More Investors Not Aid - Migereko Tells Dubai
Headline: Uganda: Bring More Investors Not Aid - Migereko Tells Dubai
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Minister of Tourism, Trade and Industry, has called for more investors and opening up for trade opportunities to boost economic growth.
Speaking at a recent function in Dubai, where he represented President Yoweri Museveni, Mr Daudi Migereko, said Uganda needs trade opportunities to finance its economic growth.

Original_Search_Title: Tens of Thousands Raped, Few Prosecuted
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: D.R. Congo: Tens of Thousands Raped, Few Prosecuted
Body_Text:
Kinshasa —  Judicial Reforms Needed to Ensure Justice for Victims of Sexual
Violence
In eastern Congos conflict, government troops and rebel fighters have
raped tens of thousands of women and girls, but fewer than a dozen
perpetrators have been prosecuted by a judicial system in dire need of
reform, Human Rights Watch said in a report released on the eve of
International Womens Day.

Original_Search_Title: SA 'Must Prepare for Life After Metals'
Headline: South Africa: SA 'Must Prepare for Life After Metals'
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — MINERAL resources are inevitably finite, at least until alchemy becomes
an exact science.
A central challenge for SA's policy makers is to create spin-offs from
today's mining activity to provide sustainable jobs when the holes have been
dug, the metals and minerals extracted, and the mining companies have moved
on.

Original_Search_Title: Campaigns Start With Tour of East
Headline: Uganda: Campaigns Start With Tour of East
Body_Text:
Kampala — To the opposition, President Museveni started campaigning for the 2006 elections with his recent marathon tour of eastern Uganda. To the Movement/NRM government, the campaign is indeed on. Only that it is a different sort of campaign. It is a campaign to increase household incomes and chase away poverty from the countryside.
The President's weeklong tour, which ended on Wednesday, took him to Mbale, Tororo, Sironko, Pallisa, Kapchorwa and Busia districts.

Original_Search_Title: Kisoga: the Land Where Spirits Reign
Headline: Uganda: Kisoga: the Land Where Spirits Reign
Body_Text:
Kampala — I clamber onto a white Nissan van covered with red ochre dust. Here in
Kisoga, the number of passengers willing to board determines the vehicle's
capacity. I am, therefore, not surprised when 22 passengers are crammed into
the van. I sit on the tattered brown rear seat, sandwiched between two fat
women, wearing flowered gomesi. The journey to the land of spirits
begins.
The road to this mysterious destination is a torment. It is a hardcore
surface with potholes.

Original_Search_Title: Fight Against Graft is a Collective Task
Headline: Kenya: Fight Against Graft is a Collective Task
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The resignation of a single individual has rarely had such a devastating effect within the corridors of power here in Kenya. As such the recent resignation of Mr John Githongo from as Permanent Secretary for Governance and Ethics, deserves close scrutiny.
In a country like Kenya the "withdrawal" as well as the "resumption" of donor funding is the stuff of first page headlines. We are always wondering what these donors will be up to next. And the response to Mr Githongo's departure from within the donor community certainly suggests that this particular resignation was seen - by the German and American Ambassadors at least - as an epochal event.

Original_Search_Title: Imam Blames Domestic Violence On Increasing Women Freedoms
Headline: Uganda: Imam Blames Domestic Violence On Increasing Women Freedoms
Body_Text:
Kayunga — The increasing cases of domestic violence and marriage break-up have
been attributed to the liberation of women that has been promoted by the
government.
The head of Imams in Uganda, Sheikh Mozarta Batte, made the observation
on Tuesday while addressing muslims during Duwa prayers at the home of Mr
Gugwa Muwada, in Bwetyaba , Kayunga sub-county.

Original_Search_Title: Spread of Banana Wilt Worrying, Say MPs
Headline: Uganda: Spread of Banana Wilt Worrying, Say MPs
Body_Text:
Kampala — The banana wilt disease could become a national disaster if the Government does not intervene, MPs have said.
They said the wilt was spreading at an alarming rate and was almost covering the whole country.

Original_Search_Title: Tangle of Red Tape 'Choking Banks Around the World'
Headline: South Africa: Tangle of Red Tape 'Choking Banks Around the World'
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — INCREASING industry regulation remains the biggest risk facing banks
globally.
According to a study conducted by London's Centre for the Study of
Financial Innovation and sponsored by PricewaterhouseCoopers, the growing
regulation of banks is eating into profits and weakening the banking
system. It also threatens to drown some of the industry's smaller
members.

Original_Search_Title: FDC Not the Saint It Claims to Be
Headline: Uganda: FDC Not the Saint It Claims to Be
Body_Text:
Kampala — Last week, I featured on the Capital Gang talk show with Hon. Augustine
Ruzindana to debate the unfolding political events in the country. Crucial
to the debate, was the launch of FDC manifesto or platform as Ruzindana
emphasized.
The platform is nothing else, but a ten point programme. The FDC interim
leaders are not new political actors that perhaps one would be anxious of
seeing their political performance, but rather people who have been on the
stage since 1986. And therefore, a lot of questions without any answers,
surround FDC's alleged new role of putting Uganda back on the democratic
path.

Original_Search_Title: Liberia's Troubled Lone Star Gets Ready for Senegal
Headline: Liberia/Senegal: Liberia's Troubled Lone Star Gets Ready for Senegal
Body_Text:
Monrovia — Caretaker coach "Kofi Bruce" will have his hands full against the Lions'
marksman, El Hadji Diouf and company in Dakar on March 25.
The financially-trouble national football team of Liberia, the Lone
star, has commenced training for the final rounds of the 2006 finals of
World and African Nations Cups qualifiers. The Lone star's first match
against Senegal in Dakar comes on March 25, 2005.

Original_Search_Title: UPC Caucus Boss Joins Movement
Headline: Uganda: UPC Caucus Boss Joins Movement
Body_Text:
Kampala — BUTEBO county MP Dr. Stephen Malinga on Monday crossed from the UPC
party to the Movement.
Malinga, the chairman of the UPC parliamentary caucus, crossed to the
movement at a rally addressed by President Yoweri Museveni at Budaka
sub-county headquarters.

Original_Search_Title: The JM Confessions
Headline: Kenya: The JM Confessions
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Following JM Kariuki's brutal murder 30 years ago, angry parliamentarians formed a select committee to track down his killers.
The 11 member select team was chaired by then Bungoma East (now Kimilili) MP, the late Elijah Mwangale.

Original_Search_Title: Mbale Crowd Quizes Museveni Over Pledge
Headline: Uganda: Mbale Crowd Quizes Museveni Over Pledge
Body_Text:
Kampala — Residents of Buwagogo sub-county under the leadership of MP Wesonga Kamana stopped President Yoweri Museveni over his pledge to construct the Mwikaye bridge.
The president, who had just finished addressing a rally in Manjiya county, was travelling back to Mbale town when he was intercepted by the crowd carrying dry banana leaves demanding that he addresses them.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Initiated As Bugisu Elder
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Initiated As Bugisu Elder
Body_Text:
Sironko — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has been initiated into the Bugisu elders' forum.
Museveni was decorated with a headgear and skin of a colobus monkey and received a traditional stool, confirming him as an elder and counsellor.

Original_Search_Title: FDC Man Defects
Headline: Uganda: FDC Man Defects
Body_Text:
Kampala — The former Reform Agenda (now Forum for Democratic Change) youth wing
chairman for Arua defected to the Movement on Thursday.
Jonathan Osumile, who is also the LC3 chairman for Arua hill division,
defected with his secretary for finance, Baifa Amaga, at the launch of
Makerere Ayivu Student's Association at Ayivu county headquarters.

Original_Search_Title: Heed Advice Mr President
Headline: Uganda: Heed Advice Mr President
Body_Text:
Kampala — The visiting Dutch development minister, Ms Agnes Van Arden, has advised
President Yoweri Museveni against his attempting to cling to power, warning
that it would backfire on him.
Museveni has been in power for over 18 years - a long enough period to
have put in place a good roadmap to democratic power transfer. What we see
now are stratagems to score yet another victory in staying - and these are
not even in disguise.

Original_Search_Title: Advocates of True Federalism Have Hidden Agenda - Mantu
Headline: Nigeria: Advocates of True Federalism Have Hidden Agenda - Mantu
Body_Text:
Lagos — Deputy Senate President, Alhaji Ibrahim Mantu yesterday accused advocates of true federalism as those with hidden agenda, arguing that what is needed in the country was good governance and judicious use of natural resources.
Mantu who spoke with newsmen at the Murtala Muhammed Airport, Lagos also said any agenda that would lead to the disintegration of Nigeria at the Political Reform Conference would not be welcomed.

Original_Search_Title: Mbale Demands Luweero Compensation
Headline: Uganda: Mbale Demands Luweero Compensation
Body_Text:
Kampala — MBALE district has demanded a share of the Luweero Triangle rehabilitation funds.
Manjiya county MP David Wakikona told President Yoweri Museveni on Wednesday that the district deserved a share of the funds for its role in the liberation struggle.

Original_Search_Title: The Movement Was Museveni's Vehicle to Rule Forever
Headline: Uganda: Q&A: The Movement Was Museveni's Vehicle to Rule Forever
Body_Text:
Nairobi —  PROF OGENGA LATIGO was elected one of the five vice presidents of
the Forum for Democratic Change, deputising for Dr Kizza Besigye, who lives
in exile in South Africa. DAVID KAIZA spoke to him on the transition to
multiparty politics in Uganda
What are FDC's views on the transition process?

Original_Search_Title: Getting Ready to Take On the World
Headline: Kenya: Getting Ready to Take On the World
Body_Text:
Nairobi —  In the second and final part of his account on Kenya's cross-country
training camp at Kigari, Embu, athletics correspondent PETER NJENGA, who
spent a few days at the camp, looks at what happens after breakfast.At 9:00
am, the athletes are scattered in the camp before the routine warm-up.
Isabella Ochichi takes time off the training to attend to matters of
cleanliness at Embu.

Original_Search_Title: Western Union Storms Koidu Town
Headline: Sierra Leone: Western Union Storms Koidu Town
Body_Text:
One of the fastest ways to receive money worldwide is through the
Western Union Money Transfer Scheme, operating under the Union Trust Bank
Limited, has now been extended to the people of the Kono District with the
opening of its Koidu Branch in the township over the weekend.
The simultaneous opening of the branch of the Bank and the provision of
the Western Union Money Transfer facility was embraced by the people of
Koidu, manifested in the collaboration with the management and staff of the
establishment in a float 'bubu' parade involving the popular "Wan Pot'
comedians along the main streets of the township.

Original_Search_Title: Did VP Bukenya Visit Shrine to 'Top Up' Kisanja Time?
Headline: Uganda: Did VP Bukenya Visit Shrine to 'Top Up' Kisanja Time?
Body_Text:
Kampala — You would be endangering your life if you tried to stand between many an African politician and a witchdoctor. Only death does the two part.
A month ago, Gulu Municipality MP Nobert Mao, commenting on January's cabinet reshuffle in The Monitor, noted its coincidence with VP Gilbert Bukenya's visit to the shrine weeks earlier.

Original_Search_Title: Kadaga Sues Musumba
Headline: Uganda: Kadaga Sues Musumba
Body_Text:
Kampala — DEPUTY Speaker Rebecca Kadaga has sued MP Salaamu Musumba (Bugabula South) over defamation.
Kadaga said on January 22, Musumba falsely told The Monitor newspaper that she (Kadaga) distributed dry banana leaves (essanja) in Mafubira, Jinja, on the official parliamentary pick-up truck, during the President's visit to Busoga.

Original_Search_Title: High Fuel Bills Result Into Food Shortage
Headline: Kenya: High Fuel Bills Result Into Food Shortage
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The fuel shortage has greatly affected food supply in Nakuru Town and
the surrounding areas.
A Nation survey indicated a shortage of both ripe and raw bananas from
Uganda.

Original_Search_Title: Movt Cyclists Disrupt FDC Rally
Headline: Uganda: Movt Cyclists Disrupt FDC Rally
Body_Text:
Kampala — What was meant to be a peaceful FDC rally in Kyenjojo district on Friday almost turned into a fight with NRM supporters.
Flashing the third term signs, the cyclists wearing essanja (dry banana leaves) stormed the venue of the FDC rally.

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani's Quest for a New Senate
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani's Quest for a New Senate
Body_Text:
Lagos — Amid the serial scandals that have rocked and buffeted the Senate of the
Federal Republic of Nigeria in the past six years of the country's nascent
democratic experiment, there is a nagging temptation, nay inclination, in
certain quarters to write off in a hurry and completely too, the boundless
potentials of, as well as the unexplored possibilities which the upper
chamber of the National Assembly as presently constituted is doubly capable
of.
It is considerably the case that long years of military siege mentality
continue to shape or condition our perceptions and notions of democracy, its
processes, and mechanisms in addition to popular reflexes towards its
institutions and structures.

Original_Search_Title: MP Madzima Pledges to Fulfill Poll Promise
Headline: Zimbabwe: MP Madzima Pledges to Fulfill Poll Promise
Body_Text:
Harare — Nyanga Member of Parliament Cde Paul Madzima has pledged to fulfill his election promise to improve road networks, construct dams and improve communication networks for the area.
The move is set to improve the living standards of many people, especially in Nyanga North.

Original_Search_Title: Biodiversity Experts Call for More Action
Headline: Cameroon: Biodiversity Experts Call for More Action
Body_Text:
Some 65 biodiversity experts drawn from several Ministries in
Yaounde, NGOs, and women lobby groups have agreed that more action is needed
if Cameroon must meet the 2010 target set by the International Biodiversity
Convention.
All countries bound by the convention have to strive for a 50 percent
reduction of their biodiversity loss by this date.

Original_Search_Title: Let the Emperor Rule If People Cannot See He is Naked
Headline: Uganda: Let the Emperor Rule If People Cannot See He is Naked
Body_Text:
I was visiting a Kenyan friend in Nakuru-Kenya last month when news came from Uganda that hundreds supporting the return of President Yoweri Museveni to power after 2006 took to the kanmpala streets to demonstrate against anti-third term critics.
The demonstrators, who called themselves, concerned patriots and clad in essanja (dry banana leaves), a symbol of third term for Museveni, chanted: "we want third term," and denounced the Irish Rock star Sir Bob Geldof and the British government for opposing the amendment of the Constitution to remove term limits hence allowing Museveni run for another term.

Original_Search_Title: How UPC Was Formed
Headline: Uganda: How UPC Was Formed
Body_Text:
Some time in 1960, a decision was taken to merge the UNC with the Uganda
Peoples' Union led by Rwetsiba. A decision was made and the Uganda Peoples'
Congress (UPC) was born.
In this third part of our series, Dr Obote tells Andrew Mwenda about the
pre-independence struggle and the beginning of Uganda's suffering.

Original_Search_Title: Senate Presidency And the Banana Peels...
Headline: Nigeria: Senate Presidency And the Banana Peels...
Body_Text:
Lagos — The Senate, which is otherwise called the Upper Chamber of Nigeria's legislature can pass for a haunted house. The Senate in its character, make-up and business can make an epic in thriller, horror, or the classic cloak and dagger genre of movies which casts men in perpetual chase of power, money and influence.
Everybody is familiar with the story and history of instability in the Senate. And in the same way, Nigerians have become used to the contradiction, which casts a slide of our national dilemma: a house of parliament, which is presupposed to be a chamber of wise men, tempered by age, experience and nobility, prefers by reason of its modus operandi and character to display all the palpable manifestations of instability, immaturity and horrifying lack of will to do the right thing.

Original_Search_Title: Senate Presidents And the Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Senate Presidents And the Banana Peel
Body_Text:
Lagos — One of the most memorable quotes of Dr. Chuba Okadigbo, the late Senate President, in the last four years of his life was the one in which after he was removed from office, he warned his successor, Anyim Pius Anyim, to beware of the proverbial banana peel around the office of the nation's chief legislator which ensures the fall from grace of the occupants of the office.
The recent ~Education Ministry bribery scandal which is sure to lead to the disgraceful fall of Chief Adolphus Wabara from the Senate Presidency may have once again proved Okadigbo right. For six years of the First Republic, the nation produced two Senate Presidents, Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe and Chief Nwafor Orizu, Orizu succeeded Zik when the latter moved on to become the nation's ceremonial president. In the over four years duration of the Second Republic, only Dr. Joseph Wayas presided over the nation's highest law making chamber. Even, the aborted Third Republic had two Senate Presidents, Dr. Iorchia Ayu and Senator Ameh Ebute

Original_Search_Title: Tenywa Ready
Headline: Uganda: Tenywa Ready
Body_Text:
Fans 'ambush' president Museveni in Ntungamo
Cranes coach Mohammod Abbas has landed a defender boost after
United States based Sulieman 'Bonseu' Tenywa confirmed his
availability to the team last weekend.

Original_Search_Title: Brilliant But Helpless
Headline: Uganda: Brilliant But Helpless
Body_Text:
Kampala — Just when she was almost sure to take hold of a bright future, Beatrice Nakafuma's wishes and dreams seem to have come tumbling down.
The 16-year-old would love to become a medical doctor once she succeeds to continue with her education. But she is not even sure she will have her next meal.

Original_Search_Title: Why I Moved the Motion-Opara
Headline: Nigeria: Why I Moved the Motion-Opara
Body_Text:
Lagos — The Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Hon Austin Opara, who led a move to stop foreign debt repayments after efforts to get the creditors to give Nigeria some leverage failed, spoke with Ahamefula Ogbu
What informed your motion asking the federal government to stop payment of its foreign debt?

Original_Search_Title: Kenyan ICT Regulator Disbanded
Headline: Kenyan Regulator Disbanded
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — In an unprecedented step, which has had industry bodies questioning its
legality, the Kenyan government has dissolved the nation's regulator.
According to Joseph Mucheru, chairman of the Telecommunications Service
Providers of Kenya, the decision to disband the Communications Commission of
Kenya (CCK) and relieve the director-general (DG) of his duties has thrown
the entire industry into disarray.

Original_Search_Title: Zanu PF Must End Paranoia Against All Media
Headline: Zimbabwe: Zanu PF Must End Paranoia Against All Media
Body_Text:
WHAT Zanu PF has suffered from since independence is a paranoia against
any media which criticises its policies. This paranoia jelled perfectly with
Jonathan Moyo's own delusions of grandeur, to give birth to their joint
testament to a persecution mania to end all paranoia - the Access to
Information and Protection of Privacy Act (AIPPA).
The government's reaction to Wilf Mbanga's latest media venture, The
Zimbabwean, is typical of this psychological malady, which manifests itself
in hysterical outbursts against any attempt to find fault with Zanu PF, the
government or President Robert Mugabe.

Original_Search_Title: Matooke to Be Availed in Powder Form
Headline: Uganda: Matooke to Be Availed in Powder Form
Body_Text:
Kampala — VERY soon matooke will be available in powder form. Thanks to a new banana drying technology, which the Government is acquiring from Germany.
The project, which the Government is undertaking in conjunction with Makerere University, Department of Food Technology, will see German manufactured Solar Tunnel Driers, known as Honenkeim established in the banana-rich districts of Bushenyi and Mbarara.

Original_Search_Title: Trade Imbalance Worries Museveni
Headline: Uganda: Trade Imbalance Worries Museveni
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has expressed concern over the huge trade
imbalance between Uganda and the United Arab Emirates (UAE), writes Joyce
Namutebi.
He said the imbalance should be addressed. He encouraged UAE companies
to start buying Uganda's products, a press statement issued last week
said.

Original_Search_Title: Return to NRM, Museveni Tells Ssemogerere
Headline: Uganda: Return to NRM, Museveni Tells Ssemogerere
Body_Text:
Kamwokya — President Yoweri Museveni yesterday called upon Democratic Party leader Paul Kawanga Ssemogerere to abandon opposition and join the National Resistance Movement.
Mr Museveni had earlier asked members of DP and other political parties to join the Movement, instead of struggling with "dying or dead institutions."

Original_Search_Title: Stop President From Campaigning, EC Told
Headline: Uganda: Stop President From Campaigning, EC Told
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Electoral Commission (EC) has been asked to restrain President
Yoweri Museveni from "campaigning" before official campaigns are
declared.
Opposition politicians expressed concern that Museveni's tours in the
countryside over the past weeks were nothing but campaigns targeted to
disadvantage other players ahead of elections next year. Speaking at a
stakeholders public dialogue organised by the EC on the topic "Elections
under a multi party political dispensation: implications, challenges and way
forward," in Kampala on Friday, the parties said the President was taking
unfair advantage of his position to campaign.

Original_Search_Title: I Am Still in UPC - Malinga
Headline: Uganda: I Am Still in UPC - Malinga
Body_Text:
Kampala — BUTEBO MP Dr. Stephen Malinga has said he has not crossed to the
Movement because some issues have not been resolved.
Malinga, who heads the Uganda People's Congress (UPC) parliamentary
caucus, told The New Vision yesterday that he was under pressure from his
constituency to cross to the Movement so that the area can be granted a
district status.

Original_Search_Title: At 125, Kogo Has Straddled Three Centuries
Headline: Kenya: At 125, Kogo Has Straddled Three Centuries
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kogo Kesendany, is now blind but moves about with the aid of her cane.
When she lost her last born son, then aged 91 on Christmas Day last year, Elizabeth Kogo Kesumo Kesendany thought God had forgotten her.

Original_Search_Title: I Looked Silly Before Bwindi's Gorilla
Headline: Uganda: I Looked Silly Before Bwindi's Gorilla
Body_Text:
Kampala — The rolling outlines of a grand sweep of distant hills gently disappear in a cloud of the early morning mist. My muscles are aching, my toes are blistered and my pulse is racing like that of an athlete. I huff and puff but fail to catch my breath.
The imposing mountain vegetation seductively invites six of us to its interior, where half of the world's 600 mountain Gorillas call home. The prospective encounter with Bwindi's mountain gorillas has for a very long time ranked high on my adventurous craving.

Original_Search_Title: Walk 'Sycophancy' Talk, Prof. Nsibambi
Headline: Uganda: Walk 'Sycophancy' Talk, Prof. Nsibambi
Body_Text:
Kampala — Prof. Apollo Nsibambi, our Prime Minister, gave very good advice to Africans in general about sycophants (See: Sycophants ruining Africa - PM. The Monitor, January 28, 2005), but he would do his country good by stressing this advice to his boss and friend, President Yoweri Museveni, since it is these very sycophants that are ruining our dear country in the name of service, not to the country, but to Mr Museveni as a person.
While at the opening of the consultation workshop of Commission for Africa at Speke Resort Munyonyo on 27th January, the professor reportedly said that for Africa to get out of the poverty cycle, it requires visionary and focused leadership backed by non-sycophantic followers who are critical of the actions of their leaders.

Original_Search_Title: Dressing Up Ugandan Style
Headline: Uganda: Dressing Up Ugandan Style
Body_Text:
Kampala — Searching for an identity of place, I take a stroll along Kampala's
streets. The eye, being curious and stubborn, picks out boutiques. But of
interest were the unique ones; unique because of what is on the shelf.
From Dewinton road, up to the Cultural Village at the National theatre,
straight to Buganda road and going down Kampala road, is an African glamour
that makes the eye "smile".

Original_Search_Title: What Are Uganda's Real Economic Woes?
Headline: Uganda: What Are Uganda's Real Economic Woes?
Body_Text:
Kampala —  The recent fall in world cotton prices has had a serious impact in
several west and central African countries
THERE has been widespread criticism of certain trade policies pursued by
rich countries, and how they prevent poorer countries from becoming serious
players in international trade.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Gives Nod to Long List of Demands
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Gives Nod to Long List of Demands
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The most important aspect of President Kibaki's tour of the three Gusii
districts is that he commissioned the Kisii-Chemosit road.
Otherwise, everything else was pledges, and many wonder if those he made
after a memorandum from the local leaders will be realised. He agreed to
almost everything they demanded.

Original_Search_Title: 'How Igbo Can Secure Presidency in 2007'
Headline: Nigeria: 'How Igbo Can Secure Presidency in 2007'
Body_Text:
Lagos —  2007 may still be a couple of years away, but the various
geo-political zones aspiring to produce the next president for the country
are not leaving things to chance. One of the zones currently laying claim to
the presidency is the South-east.
In this interview with Agha Ibiam, the governorship candidate of the
All Progressive Grand Alliance (APGA) in Abia State during the last
gubernatorial election, Chief Onwuka Kalu speaks on the Igbo presidency
project, efforts chanelled towards realising it, its feasibility and why the
South-east failed to clinch the presidency in 2003, among other issues.

Original_Search_Title: Mutale Hosts 10,000 Movement Mobilisers
Headline: Uganda: Mutale Hosts 10,000 Movement Mobilisers
Body_Text:
Kampala — An official from Kalangala Action Plan (KAP) has said they have invited 10,000 Movement mobilisers countrywide for a retreat at Matembe in Luweero to lay strategies for the 2006 general elections.
"It is time to reorganise our mobilisers. We shall not wait until it is too late. We want to impart new skills into our people to be prepared for the tasks ahead," the KAP official, known as Commandant Naome Tasumba Opio, told The Monitor by telephone yesterday.

Original_Search_Title: Biraro Bars Essanja At Heroes' Re-Burial
Headline: Uganda: Biraro Bars Essanja At Heroes' Re-Burial
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE deputy chief of staff Brig. Benon Biraro on Friday barred mourners at the re-burial of former FRONASA commander Martin Mwesiga and NRA commander Lt. Col. Samuel Magara from wearing essanja, saying the function was military and not political.
The re-burial of the two brothers, killed during the struggle against Idd Amin and Obote's regimes, took place in Rutooma in Kashari, Mbarara district.

Original_Search_Title: Deadly Effects of Miraa-Like Drug
Headline: Kenya: Deadly Effects of Miraa-Like Drug
Body_Text:
Nairobi — "Manze hii something ni poa, yaani steam yake ni noma sana kushinda ya veve..steam yake inakutake world ingine - .(This thing is so cool, its maximum effect is more vicious than that of miraa (khat), it carries you to another world).
How can a human being chew that many dry leaves like a goat? Is what goes on in my mind as I watch the lanky lad with stoned eyes explain to me his "poison".

Original_Search_Title: Agoa Trade Grows, But Slowdown Ahead
Headline: East Africa: EA Agoa Trade Grows, But Slowdown Ahead
Body_Text:
Nairobi — THE AFRICAN Growth and Opportunity Act is continuing to fuel expansion of Kenya's textile and clothing industry while also encouraging investments in this and other sectors in Tanzania and Uganda, according to a new US government report.
Kenya's exports to the US under Agoa's duty-free terms grew rapidly in the first six months of 2004, the most recent period measured in the report by the US International Trade Commission. Kenya sold $132 million worth of goods to the American market from January to June last year. The corresponding figure for 2003 was $90 million.

Original_Search_Title: Judiciary Undergoes Radical Changes
Headline: Zimbabwe: Judiciary Undergoes Radical Changes
Body_Text:
Harare — OF all the three arms of Government, the institution that has undergone
the most radical transformation is the judiciary, not least because it is
the arm of Government from which black Zimbabweans were barred from even
during the concessions which were made during the short-lived
Zimbabwe-Rhodesia.
Trade-ins saw a lot of shameless black faces trying to add colour to
what was to be a Rhodesian ghost. Due to the magnitude of the changes in the
judiciary, it would not be possible to cover the 25-year period in one
article. This week, therefore, we will only look at what the general picture
was like in 1980, and the efforts which were made to transform the judiciary
in that year alone, we will deal with all changes in subsequent
instalments.

Original_Search_Title: Brig. Biraro Blocks Kisanja Crowd At Burial
Headline: Uganda: Brig. Biraro Blocks Kisanja Crowd At Burial
Body_Text:
Mbarara — The Assistant Chief of Staff, Brig. Benon Biraro, on Friday blocked President Yoweri Museveni's supporters from waving dry banana leaves (essanja) at the re-burial of Martin Mwesiga and his brother, Samuel Magara.
The essanja-carrying crowds stormed the burial grounds at Ogwengoma, Rutoma in Mbarara district waiting to cheer Museveni, but were prevented by the army on Biraro's orders. Some of the enthusiasts hooded their cars and bicycles with the ssanja. Essanja is a symbol used by campaigners for a third term for Museveni.

Original_Search_Title: How Unlikely Enemy Betrayed Top NRA Rebel Magara
Headline: Uganda: How Unlikely Enemy Betrayed Top NRA Rebel Magara
Body_Text:
Kampala — President Yoweri Museveni was this weekend set to formally lay to rest the bodies of two brothers, both comrades of the President in his long politico-military struggles in Uganda, as part of activities to mark the army week. The bodies of Martin Mwesiga (killed in 1973) and Sam Magara (killed in 1982) were exhumed in Mbale and Kampala respectively last week for a formal burial ceremony at their home in Rutooma, Mbarara, report Solomon Muyita & Grace Matsiko:
From the outside, the house off Balintuma Road in Mengo, Kampala, looked ordinary. It was set in a low-lying area, next to St. Claire Nursery School, so it was not in view of everybody. Inside the house were two ordinary city families, one of Ambassador Katenta Apuuli and another of Dr John Kamanyire.

Original_Search_Title: Wealth for All in 2205 AD
Headline: Uganda: Wealth for All in 2205 AD
Body_Text:
Kampala — It must have become obvious to everybody by now that in the politician's box of tricks repetition is far more valuable than truth. For instance, if you allowed a politician to make the declaration that two plus two equals four, but restricted him to doing so only once, he would prefer that you allowed him to declare that two plus two equals seven, but gave him the liberty to spread this arithmetic at will, day or night, and in all the villages he can reach.
Just like we have to live with the circumstance that footballers will dribble, and cartoonists will always choose a look from the zoo to match your nose, we have to accept that repetition is one of the tricks that make politicians tick. And out of resignation or despair, there comes a time when most citizens can only avoid sliding into madness by treating the repeated message as an exercise in humour; a source of fun.

Original_Search_Title: Resting in Peace, And Beauty
Headline: Kenya: Resting in Peace, And Beauty
Body_Text:
