Nairobi —  Were former minister Karisa Maitha to visit Langata Cemetery today, he
would rest easier knowing that the dead are now well taken care of, writes
FRANKLIN AWORI
When he was minister for Local Government, the late Karisa Maitha toured
the Langata Cemetery in Nairobi and profusely apologised to the dead for the
poor treatment they had been receiving at the hands of the cemetery's
caretakers. On behalf of the nation, the minister said he was sorry for the
neglect the dead had had to endure over the years.

Original_Search_Title: Return of the Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Return of the Banana Peel
Body_Text:
Lagos — WHEN in the space of four years, the leadership of the nation's upper legislature, the Senate, had to change three times, a number of people had come to the irrevocable conclusion that something lies in the exalted seat that destroys its occupant.
It may well have been inpolitik to have reasoned that way, after all politics is nothing more than the management of intrigues. And for the Senate, the four years presented a huge dose of intrigues, dragging down with it two of the most flamboyant politicians the nation has ever known in recent years.

Original_Search_Title: Illegal Lumbers Fell Mabira Trees At Night
Headline: Uganda: Illegal Lumbers Fell Mabira Trees At Night
Body_Text:
Kampala — ILLEGAL loggers in Mabira forest, Mukono district, are felling trees at night to evade arrest.
Gazetted as a forest reserve about a century ago, the 300 square kilometres of Mabira form the largest forest in central Uganda. It habours about 300 species of birds.

Original_Search_Title: Golden Jubilee for a Childless Marriage in the First 10 Years
Headline: Uganda: Golden Jubilee for a Childless Marriage in the First 10 Years
Body_Text:
Kampala — A native doctor told Joyce she was bewitched by her mother but Joyce has no step-mother
After 10 years in marriage without a child, life for Philipo Kamya and Joyce Nansembe of Nakwero, Wakiso district became a nightmare. But this did not end their marriage.

Original_Search_Title: Development: State's Obligation to the People
Headline: Zambia: Development: State's Obligation to the People
Body_Text:
TODAY, the development concept has become elusive in many people's minds. But whether it is in small or big terms, every person agrees that it leads to a better society.
Nowadays we hear countries being divided into developed, developing and underdeveloped nations, according to the living standards of the people.

Original_Search_Title: Cosatu Not Welcome: Mangwana
Headline: Zimbabwe: Cosatu Not Welcome: Mangwana
Body_Text:
Harare — THE planned "fact-finding" mission to Zimbabwe by the Congress of South African Trade Unions (Cosatu) will not be tolerated, the Minister of Public Service, Labour and Social Welfare, Cde Paul Mangwana, said yesterday.
The minister said the delegation was supposed to seek clearance with the Zimbabwean authorities through the South African Labour Minister before proceeding to Zimbabwe.

Original_Search_Title: Women NGOs Back 3rd Term
Headline: Uganda: Women NGOs Back 3rd Term
Body_Text:
Kampala — SOME women NGOs in Kampala district have expressed support for a third
term for President Yoweri Museveni.
At a ceremony in Kamwokya, the women, led by gender minister Zoe Bakoko
Bakoru, attacked the opposition and vowed to overwhelm Museveni with votes
in 2006.

Original_Search_Title: From Bicameral to Executive System
Headline: Zimbabwe: From Bicameral to Executive System
Body_Text:
Harare — ZIMBABWE became a republic on April 18 1980, with a bicameral system of government. Bicameralism is the practice of having two legislative or parliamentary chambers.
Thus, a bicameral legislature is a parliament that consists of two Chambers or Houses - an upper house, the Senate, and a lower house, the House of Assembly.

Original_Search_Title: MP Denied Seat On NRM Day
Headline: Uganda: MP Denied Seat On NRM Day
Body_Text:
Kabale — Rukiga MP, Mr Jack Sabiiti, was denied a seat at the NRM 19th anniversary at Kakatunda, Bukindi sub-county on Wednesday.
He told The Monitor recently, that giving him a seat at such a function was not an issue, but denying him a platform to speak would be unfortunate.

Original_Search_Title: Poet Mapanje Thrills Makerere
Headline: Uganda: Poet Mapanje Thrills Makerere
Body_Text:
Kampala — Malawian poet and human rights activist Jack Mapanje, who went to prison
for his writing in 1987, read from his controversial works at a Monday event
hosted by the Uganda Women Writers Association, or Femrite, at Makerere
University.
"I was behind bars for three years, seven months, 16 days and more than
12 hours," the radical poet now at work on a prison memoir told the audience
last Monday. He was held without charges on the orders of former Malawian
President Kamuzu Banda.

Original_Search_Title: Rakai DP Boss Angers Movt Fans
Headline: Uganda: Rakai DP Boss Angers Movt Fans
Body_Text:
Kampala — RAKAI Democratic Party (DP) chairman Joseph Kabugu angered Movem-ent supporters during the 19th NRM anniversary when he removed essanja (dry banana leaves) from a group of drama actors and dropped them in front of the LC5 chairman, Vincent Semakula.
Essanja is a symbol for third term.

Original_Search_Title: Let the Circus Carry On
Headline: Namibia: Let the Circus Carry On
Body_Text:
When I was a KORTBROEK LAAITIE in the Old Location, today Hochland Park,
I used to enjoy the antics of some well-trained dudes and their buddies
though not exactly from the same tribe of mankind, performing various tricks
during the annual BOSWELL WILKIE Circus.
This particular event used to be top on the menu of local entertainment
and those who claimed to be streets ahead of the rest would take you through
the ropes - these were now boys who spent a good chunk of their tutorship
years in the capital city.

Original_Search_Title: Pambazuka News 191:
Headline: Africa: Pambazuka News 191:
Body_Text:
A Weekly Electronic Forum For Social Justice In Africa
NOTE: To view the links that are referenced below, please copy the url
and paste into your browswer.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Calls for Regional Integration at NRM Anniversary
Headline: East Africa: Museveni Calls for Regional Integration
Body_Text:
Kampala — Thousands of Movement supporters wearing dry banana leaves (kisanja),
the symbol for a third term for President Yoweri Museveni, yesterday took
centre stage at the national celebrations to mark the 19th NRM anniversary
in Kololo.
The celebration to mark 19 years of NRM in power, turned into a campaign
rally as supporters who included bodaboda cyclists and women groups marched
past the guests and a smiling Museveni, shouting pro-third term
slogans.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Visit Made Impact in Busoga, Says Musumba
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Visit Made Impact in Busoga, Says Musumba
Body_Text:
Jinja — President Yoweri Museveni ended his five-day tour of Busoga sub-region on Tuesday.
If the welcome he received on day one is anything to go by, the opposition faces a strong Movement challenge.

Original_Search_Title: PGB Guards Arrest Intruder At Kololo
Headline: East Africa: PGB Guards Arrest Intruder At Kololo
Body_Text:
Kampala — President Museveni yesterday said, Uganda, Kenya and Tanzania would have one elected President by 2013.
Gen. Museveni was addressing the nation at the 19th NRM/NRA victory day anniversary at Kololo.

Original_Search_Title: Government Warns Cosatu Members
Headline: Zimbabwe: Government Warns Cosatu Members
Body_Text:
Bulawayo — THE Government yesterday warned members of the Congress of South African Trade Unions (COSATU) who have threatened to bulldoze their way into the country to protest against alleged human rights violations that they will face the full wrath of the law.
The Minister of Home Affairs, Cde Kembo Mohadi said this in the wake of renewed threats by Cosatu to defy the Zimbabwean Government and storm into the country without proper clearance.

Original_Search_Title: Journalist Sentenced to Four Months in Prison for Libel
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Journalist Sentenced to Four Months in Prison for Libel
Body_Text:
Paris — Reporters Without Borders today reiterated its appeal to the
Democratic Republic of Congo's transitional government to amend its
press legislation after DÃ©o Mulima Kampuku of the independent daily
La RÃ©fÃ©rence Plus was sentenced in absentia to four months in prison
and a fine for alleged libel in an article about corruption in an oil
company.
Kampuku has been in hiding since the end of December after learning
that he was going to be prosecuted.

Original_Search_Title: Media Push AG for Press Freedom Bill
Headline: Kenya: Media Push AG for Press Freedom Bill
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Media organisations want Attorney-General Amos Wako to publish a press
freedom Bill once and for all. They also want all archaic laws curtailing
press freedom repealed to stop the Government from abusing them.
The Kenya Union of Journalists (KUJ), Media Council of Kenya, and the
Association of Media Women in Kenya (Amwik) petitioned the AG to have the
Freedom of Information Act enacted, saying it was long overdue.

Original_Search_Title: Where Are All These Chinamen?
Headline: Central Africa: Over The Top: Where Are All These Chinamen?
Body_Text:
IN recent months troubled central Africans have been told a great deal
about how hoardes of Chinese investors will save their country from economic
perdition. Over The Top, though, has yet to find any real evidence of these
Chinese investors.
More to the point, last time the Chinese came to the troubled central
African basket case, they built a very substantial sports' stadium. This was
a wonderful thing for the country, but it did little for the economy because
they brought their own labour - and their own labour saved money by eating
many of the dogs in the capital's western suburbs.

Original_Search_Title: Cadre 001 Tells of His Bush War Escapades
Headline: Uganda: Cadre 001 Tells of His Bush War Escapades
Body_Text:
Kampala — He can recall faces, strategies, even exact times of meetings and battles. For Mr Ibrahim Lulika Ssalongo, the Luweero Triangle war against the harsh regime of Milton Obote seems much closer than 1981.
The debonair Lulika, 58 now and popularly known as Cadre 001, tells tales of the war from his home in Nakulabye in Kampala. He keeps a book signed by bush war commanders such as Brig. Jim Muhwezi, Maj. Gen. Joram Mugume and Annet Kalungi, a woman commander, who later died.

Original_Search_Title: Speaker Linked to Backers of Museveni Bid
Headline: Uganda: Speaker Linked to Backers of Museveni Bid
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Deputy Speaker of Uganda's Parliament, Ms Rebecca Kadaga, was seen distributing dry banana leaves (essanja) in Mafubira, Jinja, hours after President Museveni started a tour of Busoga.
Essanja is the symbol for activists supporting a third term for President Yoweri Museveni.

Original_Search_Title: Kadaga Distributes Kisanja in Busoga
Headline: Uganda: Kadaga Distributes Kisanja in Busoga
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Deputy Speaker of Parliament, Ms Rebecca Kadaga, was on Thursday
seen distributing dry banana leaves (essanja) in Mafubira, Jinja, hours
after President Museveni started a tour of Busoga.
Essanja is the symbol for pro-third term activists.

Original_Search_Title: Bulldogg Unmasked
Headline: Botswana: Bulldogg Unmasked
Body_Text:
On Monday Dwilila Mothusi was amongst artists, who were grasping at straws trying to find possible leads to the whereabouts of the absent-without-leave producer and distributor, Robert "Dargie" Ddagirira.
However, on a cloudy Wednesday, he seems to have calmed down. He has come to terms with the fact that Dargie has betrayed all of them.

Original_Search_Title: Obua Seeks Third Term
Headline: Uganda: Obua Seeks Third Term
Body_Text:
Kampala — A section of football fans dressed in dry banana leaves (Essanja) welcomed incumbent FUFA president Denis Obua as he returned his nomination forms yesterday and confirmed he would seek a third term in office.
With placards praising Obua and urging him to become FUFA life president, the fans mobbed his car and decorated him with Essanja - the Movement symbol for a third term bid.

Original_Search_Title: Danger in the Neighborhood
Headline: Botswana: Danger in the Neighborhood
Body_Text:
Francistown — Phase Six made headlines recently after body parts were
fished from three pit latrines in the estate. As residents are still reeling
from the trauma of the findings, danger is looming from near-by uncovered
mine shafts.
The residents have vowed to push the relevant authorities to cover or
fence-off the shafts because they pose a danger, especially to
children. From the mine shafts, a putrid smell engulfs the area and there
are no signs to warn people of the looming danger. Tall grass has grown
around most of the pits, making it difficult for a first time visitor to
notice them easily.

Original_Search_Title: Obua Talks of 'Kisanja'
Headline: Uganda: Obua Talks of 'Kisanja'
Body_Text:
Mengo — Incumbent Denis Obua says his opponents in the forthcoming Fufa general elections are amateurs who are yet to attain the ability to govern a federation.
Obua who held a press conference at Mengo Fufa House as he returned his nomination papers yesterday, described his main opponent Mr Lawrence Mulindwa as a novice.

Original_Search_Title: Family Shaken After Burning Flare Ordeal
Headline: South Africa: Family Shaken After Burning Flare Ordeal
Body_Text:
A relaxed afternoon soon turned to panic for a Hout Bay family when a burning flare landed on their property.
Lionel and Gail Theron, and their son Farrell were enjoying lunch at the weekend when the parachuted flare landed near a paddock about 80m from the house.

Original_Search_Title: Hope for Banana Exporters
Headline: Uganda: Hope for Banana Exporters
Body_Text:
Kampala — Banana exporters who have been transporting their produce by air to
Europe are closely watching a trial shipment by land and sea via the Kenyan
port of Mombasa that could spell lower costs for them.
The sea route promises to cut by 45 percent, shipment costs of fruits
and vegetables from Uganda to the United Kingdom, the single biggest
destination for Uganda's matooke. Matooke currently goes for $2.50 per kg
and costs $1.85 per kg to ship.

Original_Search_Title: Ear to the Ground: He Who Does Not Wash His Face, Won't Wash His...
Headline: Uganda: Ear to the Ground: He Who Does Not Wash His Face, Won't Wash His...
Body_Text:
Kampala — Driving around Kampala, and travelling on the highway beyond the Kibuye
roundabout (except to Entebbe) and Jinja today, leaves me - and I imagine
other like-minded people of my generation - with a feeling I last had in
1985.
I returned to Uganda in July 1985. I had no plans to stay around for
long, but that changed a handful of hours later when Milton Obote was
overthrown in a coup. Uganda at that time was a difficult place.

Original_Search_Title: Journalist Sentenced to Four Years in Prison for Defamation
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Journalist Sentenced to Four Years in Prison for Defamation
Body_Text:
On 5 January 2005, DÃ©o Mulima Kampuku, a journalist with the Kinshasa-based daily "La RÃ©fÃ©rence Plus", was sentenced in absentia to four years in prison with no parole and ordered to pay 63,260 FC (approx. US$145) in damages and legal fees for defaming Guillaume Bolenga, president of the Cobil Oil (formerly Mobil Oil) company's management committee. "La RÃ©fÃ©rence Plus" was also found liable and ordered to pay a symbolic fine of 100 FC (less than one US dollar) in damages.
On 23 December 2004, Mulima and "La RÃ©fÃ©rence Plus" management were summoned to appear at a 27 December Kinshasa/Pont Kasa-Vubu court hearing. Neither the journalist nor the paper's representatives appeared before the court on that day.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki On Three-Day Tour to Meet His Othaya People
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki On Three-Day Tour to Meet His Othaya People
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki this weekend toured his Othaya home and spent three days to the delight of the locals.
The president got a rare opportunity to sleep in his Othaya "palace" for the first time since he took over power two years ago.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni's Tanzanian Hideout Remains a Secret
Headline: Uganda: Museveni's Tanzanian Hideout Remains a Secret
Body_Text:
Kagera, Tanzania — I examined this small house, built of red bricks and rusted bati roof in
a small village in Tanzania's Kagera district, in search of proof that this
was a place where heroes come from. I was told this was where President
Yoweri Museveni had stayed whilst co-ordinating guerrilla war against the
despotic Ugandan regime of Idi Amin between the 1970s and 1980s.
Amidst the banana and coffee tress that are a regular part of village
life here, there was a trench where we were told that Mr Museveni and his
fighters used to hide during daytime.

Original_Search_Title: Scholars Speak On Kisanja
Headline: Uganda: Scholars Speak On Kisanja
Body_Text:
Kampala —  They say MPs will grant Museveni third term, then disorder will
follow
Parliament reconvenes in two weeks after the Christmas recess. Members
will shortly after start debating the government White Paper that, among
other things, contains proposals on the transition from the Movement to a
multiparty political system.

Original_Search_Title: Out On a Limb
Headline: Kenya: Out On a Limb
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Away with rodent citizenship
It appears that for the past 30 years, the people of Nairobi have been eating fruits and vegetables sold off a rat-infested mound of garbage and they did not seem to notice.

Original_Search_Title: Uganda Ships Fresh Bananas to Europe
Headline: Uganda Ships Fresh Bananas to Europe
Body_Text:
Kampala — BANANA exporters are carrying out a trial shipment of fresh matooke by
land and sea via Mombasa to Felixtowe Port in the UK with the hope of
opening up a new land/sea route.
If the trial is successful, it will reduce shipment costs of fruits and
vegetables to Europe from $1.85 to 50 cents per kilo.

Original_Search_Title: Dialysis Treatment Costs Prohibitive
Headline: Zimbabwe: Dialysis Treatment Costs Prohibitive
Body_Text:
Harare — THE poor, suffering from kidney ailments and in desperate need of
dialysis treatment, could die owing to inadequate medical facilities and the
prohibitive cost of the treatment in private institutions.
The temporary closure of Parirenyatwa Hospital dialysis unit, the only
institution capable of accommodating a large number of dialysis patients,
has compounded the problem.

Original_Search_Title: Mataure's Arduous Journey to Stardom
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mataure's Arduous Journey to Stardom
Body_Text:
Harare —  As they say dynamites come in small packages.
When a little boy in primary school found time to play the traditional
guitar, "bhanjo" in local lingua, with friends in the neighbourhood and
sometimes would just imitate playing drums on cardboards, little did he know
he would grow to become an internationally-acclaimed drummer, having
performed and recorded with who is who of not only Zimbabwean music of all
genres, but also those from the region, particularly South African and even
beyond.

Original_Search_Title: Catoca Project to Double Diamond Production
Headline: Angola: Lunda-Sul: Catoca Project to Double Diamond Production
Body_Text:
Saurimo — The Catoca Mining Society expects to double its diamond production from three million to six million carats per year, the project director general, Manuel Ganga Junior, announced Tuesday.
Speaking on Tuesday during a visit carried out in Saurimo city, by some journalists, where they could observe the real production rate of the project, Mr Junior added that the increase will be possible resulting with the functioning, from next March, of Catoca's second production centre (Modulo Dois) or module two.

Original_Search_Title: ...cnpp, Atiku Group Lament -VP's Men Meet Today
Headline: Nigeria: ...cnpp, Atiku Group Lament -VP's Men Meet Today
Body_Text:
Lagos/Enugu/Kaduna/Maiduguri/Jos — REACTIONS poured in, in torrents, yesterday to the resignation of Chief Audu Ogbeh as national chairman of Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) with the Conference of Nigeria Political Parties (CNPP) describing it as a turning point for Nigeria.
Chairman CNPP Alhaji Balarabe Musa reacted personally just as the Atiku Consensus Movement (ACM) said the resignation was "a design calculated to frustrate Atiku Abubakar's presidential ambition come 2007."

Original_Search_Title: Manufacturers Face Bumpy Ride Under Common External Tariff
Headline: East Africa: Manufacturers Face Bumpy Ride Under Common External Tariff
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Prices of second hand motor vehicles in Kenya could go down by as much as 30 per cent in the coming weeks as customs authorities in East African Community begin applying the common external tariff for the new Customs Union.
But for local manufacturers, the champagne will have to stay on ice for a while as they adjust to stiffer competition from imports.

Original_Search_Title: Mt Elgon Gives in to Erosion
Headline: Uganda: Mt Elgon Gives in to Erosion
Body_Text:
Kampala — MOUNTAIN Elgon in Mbale district continues to experience soil
erosion. The State of Environmental Report (SER) 2002 confirmed the 1991
district estimate that soil erosion was responsible for 80% of the land
degradation. The most affected areas include the steep slopes of Mt. Elgon
that experience mass wasting.
Mary Wamimbi, Mbale district environmental officer, says landslides have
been a problem for long. The heavy El Nino rains of 1997 and 1999 left 48
people dead and 10,000 displaced.

Original_Search_Title: Zanu-PF Maintains Consistency
Headline: Zimbabwe: Zanu-PF Maintains Consistency
Body_Text:
Harare — ZIMBABWE was born at the stroke of midnight on Friday April 18 1980 at Rufaro stadium after a protracted 87-year labour period (1893 to 1980).
The bouncing baby, fathered and mothered by living and fallen heroes and heroines, was delivered by the then Chief Justice Hector Macdonald and christened by the late Archbishop Patrick Chakaipa.

Original_Search_Title: Muwanga's Fun Factory
Headline: Uganda: Muwanga's Fun Factory
Body_Text:
Kampala — While she stars on TV and in plays, she is uncomfortable with her fame
She is a darling of many viewers on various television series; Kyekyo! (That's it) on WBS TV, Bibaawo (These things happen) on Africa Magic Dstv, and That's Life Mwattu (That's life dear) which used to feature on UTV. She is also part of the cast in various plays staged by her Ebonies group across the country.

Original_Search_Title: Sounding Off Jimmy Katumba's Re-Emergence
Headline: Uganda: Sounding Off Jimmy Katumba's Re-Emergence
Body_Text:
Kampala — Jimmy Katumba's voice is imprinted on the Ugandan music scene. For over decade, between 1979 and 1991, the man dubbed as Uganda's answer to Jim Reeves together with his backup outfit, The Ebonies were all the rage, enthralling audiences with a song repertoire that spanned traditional folktales to patriotic songs.
One of the songs from the latter category was Uganda My African Home, a song Katumba co-wrote in the '80s together with Peter Penfold, the then British High Commissioner to Uganda. The lyrics are a cursory journey to Uganda's major landmarks, people and culture. Katumba hopes to use it to herald his return. His backup outfit this time is a bevy of melodious voices with a fancy name, The Breves, derived from the longest musical staff notation.

Original_Search_Title: Banyoro State Their Case of Plunder Against British Govt
Headline: Uganda: Banyoro State Their Case of Plunder Against British Govt
Body_Text:
Kampala — For some years now, officials of Bunyoro-Kitara Kingdom in western Uganda have been talking about suing the British government for its campaign of death and demolition as it battled to subdue Omukama Kabalega and establish colonial control over his domain. The kingdom now believes it has assembled enough primary evidence to take the British government to court. Bamuturaki Musinguzi looked at the evidence, some of which is drawn from handwritten reports the colonial military officers kept, and reports:-
Capt. J.H.S. Gibb of the Worcestershire Regiment, commanding the Mruli Expedition that attacked the Bunyoro-Kitara Kingdom in 1893, knew his marching orders: capture Kabalega's treasure.

Original_Search_Title: Mbarara to Tax Property
Headline: Uganda: Mbarara to Tax Property
Body_Text:
Kampala — Mbarara Municipal Council has hired a team to visit people's homes to value their property and levy appropriate taxes to raise revenue, Mbarara municipality mayor Wilson Tumwine has said.
Tumwine said the money to be raised from the business community would complement the revenue the council was collecting from graduated tax.

Original_Search_Title: Joy At City Market As Storage Row is Resolved
Headline: Kenya: Joy At City Market As Storage Row is Resolved
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government yesterday ended a stand-off between Wakulima Market traders and the city council when it introduced a Sh10 fee for overnight storage of produce.
At the same time, the ripening of bananas at the market was allowed as long as the owner pays the overnight fee, Local Government assistant minister Maina Kamanda said.

Original_Search_Title: Small Islands to Meet in Mauritius Over Natural Disasters
Headline: Mauritius: Tsunami Impact: Rising Seas, Wild Weather Menace Small Islands
Body_Text:
United Nations — The world's 37 small island developing states (SIDS), which are both
economically fragile and perpetually threatened by cyclones and major
floods, will make an urgent appeal for better disaster preparedness at an
international gathering of world leaders in Mauritius next week.
"I am sure a proposal for some kind of global early warning system will
be initiated by many states, and I believe such a system should be set up
immediately," U.N. Under-Secretary-General for SIDS Anwarul Karim Chowdhury
told IPS.

Original_Search_Title: Mauritius to Host Small Island Developing States Meeting
Headline: Mauritius: Natural and Man-made Disasters Threaten Stability of Small Islands
Body_Text:
London/Nairobi — Vulnerability to natural and man-made disasters including tsunamis and
cyclones is among a range of emerging issues challenging the health and
wealth of the worldÂs small island developing states.
Other issues include pollution and discharge by ships in the Caribbean,
over-fishing in the Pacific and the rising tide of household and other forms
of waste on the Atlantic and Indian Ocean islands.

Original_Search_Title: Great Indaba Key to Zifa Crisis
Headline: Zimbabwe: Great Indaba Key to Zifa Crisis
Body_Text:
Harare —  DESPITE giving 45 years of his life to football, veteran administrator
and former referee, Frank Valdemarca is still going strong and working for
the game albeit more at an international level.
Valdemarca, Zifa's first treasurer at Independence in 1980, later served
in the same capacity for 10 years under the executives of the late Nelson
Chirwa and Leo Mugabe.

Original_Search_Title: City Market Vibrant As Boycott Fizzles Out
Headline: Kenya: City Market Vibrant As Boycott Fizzles Out
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Business resumed yesterday at Nairobi's Wakulima wholesale market as
traders ended a boycott to protest against a ministerial directive outlawing
overnight storage of produce.
However, more than 80 traders complained that they had been left jobless
after Local Government minister Musikari Kombo banned ripening of bananas in
the market.

Original_Search_Title: Kisanja Demand is Naturally Justified
Headline: Uganda: Kisanja Demand is Naturally Justified
Body_Text:
Kampala — This is to sensitise those against Ekisanja that what they are fighting
is a third political stage Uganda, as a country, must go through. Based on
my analysis, Uganda's post-independence political history/future is divided
into three stages like those of a banana leaf.
The lifespan of a banana leaf is in stages.

Original_Search_Title: Five Officers Seized Over Drugs
Headline: Kenya: Five Officers Seized Over Drugs
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Police have arrested five officers at the port of Mombasa in connection with the Sh6.3 billion cocaine haul recently seized in Malindi and Nairobi.
The five, who include customs officials and a clearing agent, were involved in the clearing of the consignment that had the haul at the port. And police indicated last evening that the officials were to be flown to Nairobi for further interrogation.

Original_Search_Title: Wakulima Market is Reopened
Headline: Kenya: Wakulima Market is Reopened
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Nairobi's Wakulima wholesale market reopened yesterday after a weakling clearing exercise that saw 6,000 rats killed and 757 tonnes of garbage removed.
Local Government minister Musikari Kombo yesterday said it was by the "grace of God" that an outbreak of the deadly plague had not occurred in the filthy market he ordered closed on Tuesday.

Original_Search_Title: Trade Exploit Confusion Over Customs Union
Headline: East Africa: Trade Exploit Confusion Over Customs Union
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Confusion reigned at the Isebania border town yesterday following the signing of the new East African Customs Union.
And some traders took advantage of the situation to smuggle goods between Kenya and Tanzania using the panya (illegal) routes.

Original_Search_Title: 'Lumbe' Changes Its Style
Headline: Uganda: 'Lumbe' Changes Its Style
Body_Text:
Kampala — In the past, the dead were accorded a lot of respect; mourners would turn up casually but decently dressed - not overdressed. Burial and okwabya olumbe (last funeral rites) were sorrowful occasions at which one could find himself/herself automatically shedding a tear or two.
But times have changed - or some people believe so. If you were to chance upon such a gathering nowadays you would be forgiven if you were to inquire thus: "who has graduated or whose child had been baptised?"

Original_Search_Title: 'Lumbe' Changes Its Style
Headline: Uganda: 'Lumbe' Changes Its Style
Body_Text:
Kampala — In the past, the dead were accorded a lot of respect; mourners would
turn up casually but decently dressed - not overdressed. Burial and okwabya
olumbe (last funeral rites) were sorrowful occasions at which one could find
himself/herself automatically shedding a tear or two.
But times have changed - or some people believe so. If you were to
chance upon such a gathering nowadays you would be forgiven if you were to
inquire thus: "who has graduated or whose child had been baptised?"

Original_Search_Title: Where Has Our National Pride Gone?
Headline: Nigeria: Where Has Our National Pride Gone?
Body_Text:
Lagos — Wither goest thou? This gives the impression you are going somewhere or planning to go somewhere - a journey perhaps! One supposes you know where you are going. Occasionally, hopefully not often you may not know, such as a child who has just been scolded or who believes he or she has been wronged and thus suddenly plans to run away from home; oftentimes without even thinking of where to run to. Of course, many mentally disturbed or challenged people embark on journeys without an idea of the destination. We hope that we Nigerians are neither such children nor mentally challenged people. Thus, I hope we know or have an idea where we are going.
To successfully plan this trip, we obviously know where we are now. Do we? You need to know where you are at the start of a journey, to be able to properly plan how to get to your desired destination. I often wonder, whether we know where we are, for there seem to be so much delusion as to who we are and where we are Sample: Giant of Africa, 6th largest producer of Oil; largest (population) country in Africa with 135m people, country with most educated Africans. All these may be true in some contexts and not in others, or even evoke a bury-your-head-in-the-sand syndrome, especially when the expectations arising from these statistical facts are nonexistent or mirages.

Original_Search_Title: Questions Often Asked About the New Pact
Headline: East Africa: Questions Often Asked About the New Pact
Body_Text:
Nairobi — What is a customs union?
It is a free trade area in which business people may import and export goods without paying duties.

Original_Search_Title: Lobbyists Call for Better Handling of Town's Refuse
Headline: Kenya: Lobbyists Call for Better Handling of Town's Refuse
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Nakuru residents have been asked to help restore the beauty of the town through better management of garbage.
The call was made by members of the Nakuru Business Association, who have spent thousands of shillings in a beautification project that involves the rehabilitation of roundabouts, toilets and other public places.

Original_Search_Title: Bon Appetit Shows Raise $2.5mln for Charity
Headline: Zimbabwe: Bon Appetit Shows Raise $2.5mln for Charity
Body_Text:
Harare — More than $2.5 million was raised for charity during the recent Bon Appetit live cooking shows, held by Bon Marche stores as part of its 2004 French Connection promotional campaign.
At each of the six shows held between October and December retiring collections were held for selected charities and the money was donated by audiences at the events. Charities selected were Emerald Hill School for the Deaf (Avondale show), Zimcare Trust's St Catherine's School (Eastlea), Dorothy Duncan Centre for the Blind (Chisipite), Pleasantways Home (Mount Pleasant) and Christchurch Helping Hand (Borrowdale - two shows).

Original_Search_Title: And the 2004 Music Awards Go To...
Headline: Uganda: And the 2004 Music Awards Go To...
Body_Text:
Kampala — What a year of mixed fortunes for music! Dark clouds of gloom hang over the country when evergreen Elly Wamala succumbed to cancer of the throat. Some artistes walked away empty handed during the PAM awards and cried foul. Four gave the Kora Awards in South Africa a shot but fired blanks.
Our neighbours Kenya grabbed three Koras! A lot of music was released, but most of it hardly left the shop shelves due to poor marketing strategies. nonetheless, a good number of stars kept the pop flame burning red hot.

Original_Search_Title: Nakiyaga's Wine Has Won International Recognition
Headline: Uganda: Nakiyaga's Wine Has Won International Recognition
Body_Text:
Kampala — AS the Government continues the fight to liberate women from poverty, many of them have come up with unique ideas to earn a living.
One such woman is Florence Nakiyaga Kasirye from Kanumba village in Mityana.

Original_Search_Title: Heed Annan's Call for Unity in Zimbabwe
Headline: Zambia: Heed Annan's Call for Unity in Zimbabwe
Body_Text:
Lusaka — Everything possible should be done in Zimbabwe to build national
unity.
Nothing, or very little, will be achieved in any sphere of Zimbabwean
life without national unity. It will be impossible to achieve meaningful
economic recovery without unity in Zimbabwe among all the contending
political and social forces in that country.

Original_Search_Title: Kadaga Did Not Distribute Essanja in Busoga
Headline: Uganda: Kadaga Did Not Distribute Essanja in Busoga
Body_Text:
Kampala — In The Monitor's edition of January 22, 2005, we published a lead story about the Deputy Speaker of Parliament, Hon. Rebecca Kadaga (above) headlined: "Kadaga distributes Kisanja in Busoga"
We have now established the following:

Original_Search_Title: Kadaga Denies Distributing 'Essanja'
Headline: Uganda: Kadaga Denies Distributing 'Essanja'
Body_Text:
Kampala — DEPUTY Parliament Speaker Rebecca Kadaga has denied allegations that she
distributed essanja (dry banana leaves) to the public during President
Yoweri Museveni's recent tour of Busoga.
Kadaga, who is also Kamuli Women Member of Parliament, has also sued
Bugabula South MP Salaamu Musumba for making the allegations and The Monitor
for publishing the story on January 22, 2005.

Original_Search_Title: Gikomba, Supermarket of the Poor
Headline: Kenya: Gikomba, Supermarket of the Poor
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Jane Ndolo has been rummaging through a huge pile of baby clothes for almost 10 minutes now. Each item picked from the pile is meticulously scrutinised as she looks out for frayed hemlines and missing buttons. Dissatisfied, she throws several of them back onto the pile until she stumbles on a red jumper. She gleefully fishes it out and starts haggling over its price with the vendor. Ndolo wants to buy it for Sh15 even though it's going for Sh25.
Midway through the negotiations, a rotund sweaty man in a tattered coat appears from one of the stores next to a food kiosk with a huge sack on his back. As he races down the path - which can barely accommodate two people at a go - he violently shoves Ndolo onto the pile of clothes. She picks herself up from the pile and hurls a barrage of insults at the unapologetic man.

Original_Search_Title: Villagers Flee Into Mozambique
Headline: Zimbabwe: Villagers Flee Into Mozambique
Body_Text:
Chipinge — At least 16 villagers from Chipinge South fled their homes
last week into Mozambique after they were brutally attacked by suspected
Zanu PF supporters and youth militia for supporting the opposition Movement
for Democratic Change (MDC), The Standard has learnt.
The villagers, mostly from Mariya and Zamchiya areas, are surviving on
wild fruits, mangoes, bananas and the generosity of Mozambicans, who are
also providing them with shelter.

Original_Search_Title: North-Rift Fruit Prices in Sharp Rise
Headline: Kenya: North-Rift Fruit Prices in Sharp Rise
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Prices of fruits in the North-Rift region have increased by about 20 per cent following a drop in supplies. The current dry-spell has occasioned the sharp rise in prices.
Traders at the Eldoret retail market said a 50 kg net of tomatoes was currently selling at Sh1,000 up from Sh800, while onion sold at Sh30 per kg.

Original_Search_Title: Cotton Subsidies Will Make Matters Worse
Headline: Uganda: Cotton Subsidies Will Make Matters Worse
Body_Text:
Kampala — The government has announced a sh7.5b subsidy for the cotton industry
following a decline in the price of cotton on the international market.
Recently, president Yoweri Museveni announced this policy in Iganga with
a lot of confidence thinking it is a panacea for low cotton prices, and
perhaps thinking it will boost the incomes of the peasants in cotton growing
areas.
